select_when: Commitment, alignment, or conflict repair depends on understanding both the factual and emotional content of what another person is saying.
A problem owner or stakeholder is giving partial information, and the next move is to uncover what truly matters to them through careful inquiry.
The team is jumping to advice or action before it has gathered valid data about motives, constraints, and tensions.
Trust depends on demonstrating comprehension clearly enough that the other person can correct, deepen, or confirm the interpretation.

failure_modes.mode: The Line Cook Masquerading as the Chef
failure_modes.description: A listener can collect words without understanding the reasoning chain beneath them and end up with brittle, surface-level understanding.
failure_modes.mitigation: Ask 'how' and 'why' questions that probe the reasoning behind the speaker's statements, not just the conclusions they reached.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Line Cook Masquerading as the Chef

failure_modes.mode: The Assumption of Passive Listening
failure_modes.description: Teams can mistake listening for passive waiting and miss the active work needed to solve subtle mysteries in the speaker's meaning.
failure_modes.mitigation: Treat listening as active cognitive work: paraphrase the speaker's reasoning back to them and check whether your restatement captures what they actually meant.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Assumption of Passive Listening

failure_modes.mode: Premature Elaboration/Action Focus
failure_modes.description: Advice and action can arrive too early, before the emotional and factual dimensions of the problem have both been understood.
failure_modes.mitigation: Employ strategic silence and separate the deliberation phase from the action phase; reflect on both the factual content and the emotional dimension before responding.
failure_modes.source_quote: Premature Elaboration/Action Focus

premortem_questions.description: What truly matters to this person that I still have not heard clearly enough to restate back to them?
premortem_questions.source_quote: I'm gonna find out what truly matters to that person

premortem_questions.description: What do you think the other side does not get about your perspective?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What do you think the other side doesn’t 'get' about your perspective?

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough if we act before we have gathered the real emotional and factual data?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

heuristics.description: Ask how someone thinks, not just what they think, so the reasoning mechanics become visible.
heuristics.source_quote: Don't ask people what they think; instead, ask them how they think.

heuristics.description: Use looping, reflection, and confirmation questions to prove understanding and invite correction.
heuristics.source_quote: Sounds like you are saying… Did I get that right?

heuristics.description: Hold silence briefly after the speaker finishes so the conversation can move past the polished answer toward what really matters.
heuristics.source_quote: Hold the Silence

danger_when: The conversation has already produced enough evidence for a decision or reversible experiment, and more listening would mainly postpone commitment.
The model is being treated as passive politeness instead of active work to reduce misunderstanding, ego noise, and premature solutioning.
active-listening.name: Active Listening

select_when: One side may know more about quality, risk, or intent than the other.
Screening, signaling, or filtering determine outcome quality before commitment.
Pricing, access, or policy design could unintentionally attract the very cases the system is least able to handle well.

failure_modes.mode: False Pattern Recognition
failure_modes.description: False pattern recognition can make teams force a hidden-information story onto a problem that is really about incentives, execution, or design.
failure_modes.mitigation: Apply multiple frames before settling on an adverse selection explanation; test whether incentives, execution failure, or design error better explain the observed outcome.
failure_modes.source_quote: False Pattern Recognition

failure_modes.mode: Framework Fetishism
failure_modes.description: Framework fetishism can hide the assumptions embedded in a screening story and make the selection diagnosis feel truer than it is.
failure_modes.mitigation: Spell out all implicit assumptions in the screening story and pressure-test each against the outside view before acting on the diagnosis.
failure_modes.source_quote: Framework Fetishism

failure_modes.mode: Assuming Asymmetry Without Verifying the Selection Mechanism
failure_modes.description: Assuming asymmetry without verifying the selection mechanism can misdiagnose the failure entirely.
failure_modes.mitigation: Verify the specific mechanism of information asymmetry rather than assuming it exists; structure the problem into logical parts before jumping to the adverse selection explanation.
failure_modes.source_quote: Assuming Asymmetry Without Verifying the Selection Mechanism

premortem_questions.description: What would you have to believe to accept this selection story, and which of those assumptions has not been tested yet?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe to accept a particular thesis or viewpoint?

premortem_questions.description: What is the one-day answer?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What is the one-day answer?

premortem_questions.description: If someone came to me asking about this decision, what would I need to know before claiming hidden quality or hidden risk is the driver?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If someone came to me asking my opinion about this kind of decision, what would I need to know to give good advice?

heuristics.description: Structure the problem before analysis so screening, signaling, or filtering mechanisms are explicit rather than implied.
heuristics.source_quote: Problem Structuring: Rigorously frame a question or hypothesis before complex analysis

heuristics.description: Anchor to the outside view before treating one side's private-information story as the dominant explanation.
heuristics.source_quote: Outside View Anchor: Anchor decisions first to the outside view

heuristics.description: Test the case with multiple models or frames so hidden-information reasoning competes with other plausible explanations.
heuristics.source_quote: Multiple Models/Frames: Break down complex problems using multiple frameworks or mental models

danger_when: Analysts are blaming hidden information even though the real issue may be incentives, execution quality, or poor process design rather than adverse selection itself.
The model is being used as a generic asymmetry story without verifying the actual selection mechanism that sorts good and bad counterparts.
adverse-selection.name: Adverse Selection

select_when: Requirements, stakeholder learning, or technical realities are expected to change while delivery is already underway.
The team can ship in small increments, inspect real feedback quickly, and update the next cycle without waiting for a full-plan reset.
A one-shot plan is creating false certainty, and testing assumptions in sequence is cheaper than betting everything on one forecast.
The work needs explicit hypothesis-driven loops, clear accountability, and a standing one-day answer instead of long planning latency.

failure_modes.mode: Analysis Paralysis (The Hamlet Tendency)
failure_modes.description: Analysis paralysis can delay action until the learning loop dies under bureaucracy and studies.
failure_modes.mitigation: Force a one-day answer: create a coherent summary and hypothesis at any point, then gather only enough data to test it.
failure_modes.source_quote: Analysis Paralysis (The Hamlet Tendency)

failure_modes.mode: Don't boil the ocean
failure_modes.description: Premature convergence can waste effort by boiling the ocean instead of gathering only enough facts to test the current hypothesis.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use the Pareto principle to identify the 20% of facts that drive 80% of the decision, and stop gathering beyond that threshold.
failure_modes.source_quote: Don't boil the ocean

failure_modes.mode: Danger when agile rituals keep running but backlog churn replaces learning, accountability, or explicit decision rights.
failure_modes.description: Agile theater can keep rituals running even while learning, accountability, and decision rights quietly disappear.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pause between thought and action: in each retrospective, check whether the ritual produced a decision change, not just discussion.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when agile rituals keep running but backlog churn replaces learning, accountability, or explicit decision rights.

premortem_questions.description: What is the one-day answer?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What's the one-day answer?

premortem_questions.description: Is this the most important thing you could be doing right now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?

premortem_questions.description: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

heuristics.description: Run a standing one-day-answer synthesis so the team always knows its current hypothesis and next test.
heuristics.source_quote: What’s the one-day answer?

heuristics.description: Gather only enough facts to prove or disprove the current hypothesis instead of expanding discovery indefinitely.
heuristics.source_quote: gather only enough facts to prove or disprove a hypothesis

heuristics.description: Keep the goal-identify-diagnose-design-do loop sequential and continuous so action stays tied to diagnosis.
heuristics.source_quote: This loop must be performed sequentially and continuously for success.

danger_when: External commitments, interfaces, or regulatory gates are fixed enough that agile language would only hide real sequencing and approval constraints.
Agile rituals are replacing learning, accountability, or explicit decision rights with backlog churn and motion theater.
agile-methodologies.name: Agile Methodologies

select_when: The next move depends on knowing what kind of uncertainty is present because some parts are measurable risk and other parts are reducible ignorance.
One branch of the problem needs probabilistic sizing or simulation while another branch needs discovery work, diligence, or model revision.
Confidence, commitment size, and reversibility need to stay linked instead of being treated as separate decisions.
The team is about to compress all uncertainty into one category even though different tools are required for different unknowns.

failure_modes.mode: Conjunctive Event Overestimation
failure_modes.description: Conjunctive optimism can make a complex plan look safer than it is because the chain of dependent conditions is overestimated.
failure_modes.mitigation: Calibrate by quantifying the joint probability of the dependent conditions; shock-test each link independently to see where the chain actually breaks.
failure_modes.source_quote: Conjunctive Event Overestimation

failure_modes.mode: Base-Rate Neglect
failure_modes.description: Base-rate neglect can keep the team anchored in the inside view when the outside view is the only honest way to size aleatory risk.
failure_modes.mitigation: Anchor decisions to the outside view first — what happened historically in the reference class — before adjusting for the specifics of this case.
failure_modes.source_quote: Base-Rate Neglect

failure_modes.mode: The Illusion of Knowing (Overprecision)
failure_modes.description: Overprecision can hide epistemic ignorance by treating fragile assumptions as if they were already earned facts.
failure_modes.mitigation: Run sensitivity analysis on every key assumption; ask 'what would you have to believe?' and check whether each belief has been tested or merely assumed.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Illusion of Knowing (Overprecision)

premortem_questions.description: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.description: Would you be really shocked if the outcome landed outside the range you just gave?
premortem_questions.source_quote: The Shock Test

heuristics.description: Classify uncertainty first so the team knows whether to run statistical sizing or discovery work.
heuristics.source_quote: different forms of decision-making and analysis are required

heuristics.description: Anchor to the outside view and reference classes before adjusting for the specifics of the inside view.
heuristics.source_quote: moving away from an inside view ... towards anchoring decisions in the outside view

heuristics.description: Use simulations and scenario analysis when variability is real enough that one point estimate hides the true range of outcomes.
heuristics.source_quote: simulations and scenario analysis

danger_when: Uncertainty classification is becoming theater and is not changing the actual tool choice, commitment size, or monitoring rule.
The problem is routine and predictable enough that the full aleatory-versus-epistemic distinction would mostly add cognitive cost without changing action.
aleatory-epistemic-uncertainty-recogniti.name: Aleatory Epistemic Uncertainty Recognition

select_when: The task can be decomposed into repeatable steps with clear handoffs, inputs, and output quality checks.
Consistency, auditability, or scale matter more than improvisation because many people or systems must execute the same logic reliably.
A process needs explicit branching rules, bounded edge cases, and coordination across specialized components.
The problem is becoming noisy because the team lacks a shared procedure for how work should move from input to checked output.

failure_modes.mode: Rigid procedure can fail when novelty or ambiguity is doing most of the causal work and the team mis...
failure_modes.description: Rigid procedure can fail when novelty or ambiguity is doing most of the causal work and the team mistakes repeatability for understanding.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test the procedure against a genuinely novel scenario; if the algorithm cannot handle the new case without human judgment, the structure is masking understanding gaps.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when novelty, ambiguity, or human emotion is doing most of the causal work and a rigid procedure is mistaken for actual understanding.

failure_modes.mode: Procedures can optimize visible proxies while ignoring exceptions, tacit knowledge, or changing cont...
failure_modes.description: Procedures can optimize visible proxies while ignoring exceptions, tacit knowledge, or changing context.
failure_modes.mitigation: Build in explicit exception-handling branches and review the algorithm against the broadest mental-model latticework available, not just the procedural domain.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when rules are optimized to visible proxies while exceptions, tacit knowledge, or changing context are treated as noise.

failure_modes.mode: Algorithmic structure can oversimplify living social systems if implementation complexity is treated...
failure_modes.description: Algorithmic structure can oversimplify living social systems if implementation complexity is treated as a clean step-by-step execution problem.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate the analytical clarity of the algorithm from its human implementation; ensure the how-to-execute step accounts for social dynamics, not just logical correctness.
failure_modes.source_quote: Applying purely rational, step-by-step problem-solving to implementation oversimplifies what is required to translate ideas into action within a living, social system.

premortem_questions.description: Which edge case or exception would break this procedure even if the happy path looks clean?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best used when a process benefits from explicit branching rules, bounded edge cases, and coordination across specialized components or agents.

premortem_questions.description: What part of this process is being treated as noise even though it carries decision-critical tacit knowledge?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger when rules are optimized to visible proxies while exceptions, tacit knowledge, or changing context are treated as noise.

premortem_questions.description: If this routine failed in a novel situation, which assumption in the procedure would be the most misleading one?
premortem_questions.source_quote: When we encounter genuinely novel problems, we sometimes persist in using frames that are unhelpful or misleading in the new and different context.

heuristics.description: Break the task into repeatable steps with explicit handoffs so the logic can be checked instead of assumed.
heuristics.source_quote: The task can be decomposed into repeatable steps with clear handoffs, inputs, and output quality checks.

heuristics.description: Define branching rules and bounded exceptions before scale forces many people or systems to improvise inconsistently.
heuristics.source_quote: Best used when a process benefits from explicit branching rules, bounded edge cases, and coordination across specialized components or agents.

heuristics.description: Add quality checks at the output boundary so consistency is earned through verification, not assumed from the procedure name.
heuristics.source_quote: Consistency, auditability, or scale matter more than improvisation because many people or systems must execute the same logic reliably.

danger_when: Novelty, ambiguity, or human emotion is doing most of the causal work and a rigid procedure is being mistaken for actual understanding.
Rules are being optimized to visible proxies while exceptions, tacit knowledge, or changing context are treated as noise.
algorithmic-thinking.name: Algorithmic Thinking

select_when: The audience lacks a usable schema for the concept but does have adjacent experience that can carry the explanation.
Abstraction is blocking understanding even though the underlying idea is sound.
The mechanism can transfer through a comparison that preserves the important structural logic of the target domain.
The main bottleneck is making the idea graspable, memorable, and discussable rather than adding more facts.

failure_modes.mode: Most useful when the audience lacks a schema.
failure_modes.description: People can fail to understand a concept not because information is missing but because they still lack a usable mental bucket for it.
failure_modes.mitigation: Identify what schema the audience already has and connect the new concept to it explicitly; if no existing schema fits, build one with a simple concrete example first.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when the audience lacks a schema.

failure_modes.mode: Best when mechanism transfers along with familiarity.
failure_modes.description: A vivid analogy can mislead when it transfers familiarity without preserving the mechanism that actually matters.
failure_modes.mitigation: Validate the analogy by lining up assumptions: check whether the causal mechanism in the source domain actually maps to the target domain, not just the surface pattern.
failure_modes.source_quote: Best when mechanism transfers along with familiarity.

failure_modes.mode: analogies-and-metaphors vs cognitive-load-theory
failure_modes.description: Layered metaphors can increase interpretation burden instead of reducing it.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use one analogy per concept and provide counterexamples to mark where the metaphor stops applying; do not stack multiple metaphors for the same idea.
failure_modes.source_quote: analogies-and-metaphors vs cognitive-load-theory

premortem_questions.description: What existing schema does the audience already have that could carry this new idea?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when the audience lacks a schema.

premortem_questions.description: Which important structural boundary would this comparison distort if we pushed it too far?
premortem_questions.source_quote: analogies-and-metaphors vs abstraction

premortem_questions.description: Is this comparison reducing obscurity, or just replacing one kind of confusion with another?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Analogies are the antidote to abstraction and buzzwords.

heuristics.description: Use a comparison that carries the structural logic of the idea, not just a catchy image.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when mechanism transfers along with familiarity.

heuristics.description: Choose adjacent experience the audience already understands, then bridge into the new concept.
heuristics.source_quote: Most useful when the audience lacks a schema.

heuristics.description: Use analogy to cut through abstraction and jargon when the idea is sound but still not sticking.
heuristics.source_quote: Works when abstraction is blocking understanding.

danger_when: The comparison is catchy but distorts the structural boundaries of the target domain.
Layered metaphors are adding interpretation burden instead of reducing it.
analogies-and-metaphors.name: Analogies And Metaphors

select_when: A starting point, example, or framing device can help make a complex space more navigable without pretending it is final truth.
Leaders need an initial reference point for scope, expectation, or comparison while staying alert to later correction.
The team benefits from provisional orientation before deeper evidence arrives.
New information needs to be attached to an existing schema so attention and working memory can hold the problem long enough to think clearly.

failure_modes.mode: Ignoring Disconfirming Evidence (Confirmation Bias)
failure_modes.description: Confirmation bias can turn the first frame into blinders that filter later evidence around the anchor.
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge the first intuition with slower, deliberate analysis; actively seek disconfirming evidence before accepting the anchored frame.
failure_modes.source_quote: Ignoring Disconfirming Evidence (Confirmation Bias)

failure_modes.mode: False Pattern Recognition
failure_modes.description: Substitution bias can make a familiar frame feel like the right one even when the problem is genuinely novel.
failure_modes.mitigation: Diversify perspectives by asking what other disciplines or frameworks might apply; test at least two alternative frames before committing to one.
failure_modes.source_quote: False Pattern Recognition

failure_modes.mode: Misestimation of Risk in Systems
failure_modes.description: Anchoring can distort risk in complex plans by underestimating disjunctive failure and overestimating conjunctive success.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use systematic stress testing: simulate the range of outcomes rather than anchoring to the base case; ask whether the worst case is bad enough.
failure_modes.source_quote: Misestimation of Risk in Systems

premortem_questions.description: What other disciplines or frameworks might apply here?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What other disciplines or frameworks might apply here?"

premortem_questions.description: What would you have to believe to accept this perspective?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What would you have to believe to accept this perspective?"

premortem_questions.description: What is generally true of the world, independent of my perspective, concerning this situation?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What is generally true of the world, independent of my perspective, concerning this situation?"

heuristics.description: Use an initial anchor to orient the problem, but keep it explicitly provisional while better evidence arrives.
heuristics.source_quote: leaders need to set an initial reference point for scope, expectation, or comparison while staying alert to later correction

heuristics.description: Adopt the outside-view anchor when the inside view is doing too much interpretive work.
heuristics.source_quote: Adopt the Outside View Anchor

heuristics.description: Mandate dialectic challenge so the first frame meets an explicit antithesis before synthesis.
heuristics.source_quote: Mandate Dialectic Challenge

danger_when: The first number, frame, or analogy is becoming non-negotiable and later evidence is only being interpreted around it.
A familiar frame is being applied automatically to a genuinely novel problem, turning anchoring into substitution bias.
anchoring.name: Anchoring
anchoring.input_type: initial frame or reference point problem

select_when: The system can learn, adapt, or improve through bounded stress rather than merely survive it.
Downside can be contained while variation, feedback, and small failures create useful information or stronger designs.
Uncertainty is unavoidable and the smarter move is to gain from disorder instead of trying to predict it away.
The team needs experiments, de-risking, and feedback loops rather than brittle plans optimized for normal conditions only.

failure_modes.mode: Antifragility fails when people romanticize volatility and expose the system to shocks that are too ...
failure_modes.description: Antifragility fails when people romanticize volatility and expose the system to shocks that are too large to survive.
failure_modes.mitigation: Stay within the circle of competence and enforce intellectual humility about which shocks the system can absorb; bound exposure so failure is survivable.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when people romanticize volatility and expose the system to ruinous shocks that are too large to survive or learn from.

failure_modes.mode: Cognitive Entrenchment (The Expert Trap)
failure_modes.description: Expert overconfidence can make the system brittle by substituting familiar models for the novel structure of the problem.
failure_modes.mitigation: Practice mental agility by asking 'what would you have to believe?' to accept an alternative viewpoint that contradicts the current hypothesis.
failure_modes.source_quote: Cognitive Entrenchment (The Expert Trap)

failure_modes.mode: Over-Seeking Perfection (Analysis Paralysis)
failure_modes.description: Analysis perfectionism can stall the experimentation and feedback loops that antifragility actually requires.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use rigorous inversion — start from what could destroy the system and size the response, rather than perfecting analysis of what might go right.
failure_modes.source_quote: Over-Seeking Perfection (Analysis Paralysis)

premortem_questions.description: What would need to be true for this idea to work?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would need to be true for this idea to work?

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.description: What do we actually know, and where are we still guessing about the risk?
premortem_questions.source_quote: De-Risking the Unknown

heuristics.description: Define the anti-goal and design against catastrophe before chasing upside.
heuristics.source_quote: Inverse Goals (Avoiding Catastrophe)

heuristics.description: Run a portfolio of experiments and compare outcomes instead of betting everything on one forecast.
heuristics.source_quote: Iterative Modeling and Testing

heuristics.description: Use direct engagement and feedback to keep improving the design through real-world stress and correction.
heuristics.source_quote: Harnessing Reality through Feedback

danger_when: Volatility is being romanticized and the system is being exposed to ruinous shocks that are too large to survive or learn from.
Experts are leaning on familiar but brittle frameworks instead of investigating the novel structure of the situation.
antifragility.name: Antifragility

select_when: Disconnected signals only become meaningful once they are linked into a larger pattern or schema.
A new idea becomes usable only after it is connected to an existing structure the audience already understands.
The team needs instant context through analogy or conceptual linkage rather than a long detached explanation.
Understanding depends on relationship and fit between elements more than on any isolated fact.

failure_modes.mode: facts are unusable unless they hang together
failure_modes.description: Isolated facts stay inert when they are not connected to a larger pattern that makes them usable.
failure_modes.mitigation: Hang facts on a latticework of theory by actively connecting them to multiple mental models; isolated data becomes usable only when it reveals a structural relationship.
failure_modes.source_quote: facts are unusable unless they hang together

failure_modes.mode: surface similarity mistaken for structural match
failure_modes.description: The first compelling analogy can become a false proof if no one checks whether the underlying structure really matches.
failure_modes.mitigation: Validate each association by checking whether the causal mechanism transfers, not just the surface pattern; line up assumptions and test each for fit.
failure_modes.source_quote: surface similarity mistaken for structural match

failure_modes.mode: wrong-category import
failure_modes.description: Anchoring on one association hides the rest of the system and misimports the wrong category logic.
failure_modes.mitigation: Deliberately test the association from a different discipline's frame; if a social frame on a business problem or vice versa yields a different conclusion, the original association was too narrow.
failure_modes.source_quote: wrong-category import

premortem_questions.description: Which structural variable made our favorite analogy work, and does that variable really match here?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Which structural variable made the source analogy work in the first place

premortem_questions.description: What important relationship would disappear if our current association turned out to be wrong?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What critical relationship would disappear if our favorite analogy is wrong

premortem_questions.description: What competing association should we test before acting on the first story that feels right?
premortem_questions.source_quote: what competing association should we test before acting

heuristics.description: Use concept mapping to cluster findings into a higher-level pattern before jumping to a slogan.
heuristics.source_quote: Concept Mapping and Synthesis

heuristics.description: Use analogy to create a mental bucket only after checking that the structural fit is real.
heuristics.source_quote: mental bucket

heuristics.description: Link new information to existing schemas so it becomes usable quickly without pretending the first schema is final.
heuristics.source_quote: instant context and understanding

danger_when: A catchy comparison is being treated as proof even though the underlying causal structure has not been checked.
The first helpful association has hardened into the only frame the team is willing to see.
association.name: Association

select_when: Decisions, outputs, or model behavior must be explained clearly enough that others can inspect how the conclusion was reached.
The cost of error is high enough that the organization needs a recoverable trail from evidence to judgment to action.
Trust depends not only on being right, but on being able to show what happened, why it happened, and who changed what.
A process is getting too complex to trust by intuition alone and needs a reconstructable causal chain linking inputs, assumptions, and outcomes.

failure_modes.mode: Traceability can become performative documentation that consumes attention without improving judgmen...
failure_modes.description: Traceability can become performative documentation that consumes attention without improving judgment, accountability, or learning.
failure_modes.mitigation: Tie each traced artifact to a decision it should inform or a correction it should enable; if no decision changes from the trail, the documentation is theater.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when traceability becomes performative documentation that consumes attention without improving judgment, accountability, or learning.

failure_modes.mode: Experts ... may communicate the "punchline" ... without providing the necessary concrete, traceable steps for action.
failure_modes.description: Experts can communicate only the punchline and lose the concrete trail needed for action, audit, or correction.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require experts to document the reasoning chain from evidence to conclusion, not just the conclusion; use scaffolding to make the trail accessible to non-experts.
failure_modes.source_quote: Experts ... may communicate the "punchline" ... without providing the necessary concrete, traceable steps for action.

failure_modes.mode: Using mental models or processes mechanically without deep understanding
failure_modes.description: Familiar frameworks can be applied cargo-cult style, creating an auditable process around the wrong model entirely.
failure_modes.mitigation: Before auditing the process, audit the model: verify that the framework being traced is the right one for this problem, not just the familiar one.
failure_modes.source_quote: Using mental models or processes mechanically without deep understanding

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.description: What assumptions underlying the frameworks we use are not being met?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What assumptions underlying the frameworks we use are not being met?

premortem_questions.description: What would need to be visible for someone else to reconstruct how we got here?
premortem_questions.source_quote: providing a clear, reconstructable causal chain linking inputs, assumptions, logical steps, and intentional design choices to the final result

heuristics.description: Use hypothesis-driven workplans so each analysis step leaves a visible trail from question to evidence to conclusion.
heuristics.source_quote: Hypothesis-Driven Problem Solving: This foundational technique requires strong, testable hypotheses to guide work planning and analyses.

heuristics.description: Make logic trees, premises, and dependencies explicit enough that another reviewer can trace the reasoning back to its source.
heuristics.source_quote: Logic trees are the primary tool used to disaggregate problems into logical, manageable pieces and clearly visualize the structure of the problem area.

heuristics.description: Track assumptions and decision changes in a recoverable form so bad outcomes can be diagnosed at both the person and machine level.
heuristics.source_quote: If the outcome is bad, traceability allows one to synthesize whether the responsible person (RP) is incapable and/or the machine design is flawed.

danger_when: Traceability is becoming performative documentation that consumes attention without improving judgment, accountability, or learning.
The team is treating an auditable process as a linear mechanical blueprint even though the real work still needs interpretation and adaptive judgment.
auditability-traceability.name: Auditability Traceability

select_when: Trust depends on whether words, incentives, and behavior line up under pressure rather than on whether the presentation sounds polished.
The relationship itself will change whether the plan is actually carried out, especially in coaching, advisory, leadership, or negotiation settings.
The real problem is defensive signaling, status maintenance, or fear-driven withholding rather than lack of technical analysis alone.
The work needs grounded candor that puts real-time internal experience into words without substituting style for substance.

failure_modes.mode: Managing Impressions
failure_modes.description: A team can spend energy managing impressions instead of surfacing the truth that would actually let the work improve.
failure_modes.mitigation: Establish constructive confrontation as a team norm so every hypothesis meets its antithesis; make it safe to surface uncomfortable truths.
failure_modes.source_quote: Managing Impressions

failure_modes.mode: Style Without Substance
failure_modes.description: Style can outrun substance when authentic tone is not matched by domain knowledge, factual grounding, or implementation realism.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use the Feynman technique: force simplification and self-explanation to identify gaps where authentic conviction is substituting for verified knowledge.
failure_modes.source_quote: Style Without Substance

failure_modes.mode: Subjective Trap (The Inside View Bias)
failure_modes.description: Authenticity can become a cover for subjectivity if the inside view is never disciplined by outside-view evidence.
failure_modes.mitigation: Anchor to the outside view: actively seek and integrate external perspectives and reference-class data before trusting the authentic inside-view narrative.
failure_modes.source_quote: Subjective Trap (The Inside View Bias)

premortem_questions.description: What conclusions would unbiased others draw if they saw the exact same facts?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What conclusions would unbiased others draw if they saw the exact same facts?

premortem_questions.description: Can I explain this idea in two simple sentences without jargon and still preserve what is true?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Can I explain this idea in two simple sentences that a five-year-old would understand, without resorting to jargon?

premortem_questions.description: Am I focused on executing the process effectively, or only on how I will feel about the outcome?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Am I focused on how I will feel about the outcome (success/failure), or am I fully focused on executing the process effectively?

heuristics.description: Put real-time internal experience into words when that honesty helps the other side see the actual problem more clearly.
heuristics.source_quote: Verbalizing Internal Experience

heuristics.description: Use radical truth and transparency to reduce the hidden workload of impression management.
heuristics.source_quote: Radical Truth and Transparency

heuristics.description: Reduce the message to the essential core so candor is not buried under complexity theater.
heuristics.source_quote: Simplicity and Clarity

danger_when: Authenticity is being used as a shield against evidence, preparation, or accountability for consequences.
The communication problem is being treated as if style alone can compensate for weak domain knowledge or missing factual grounding.
authenticity.name: Authenticity

select_when: The real question is whose judgment deserves initial weight in a specialized or uncertain domain.
Decision speed matters, but the group still has a mechanism for challenge, audit, or second opinion.
A recommendation is being accepted largely because of rank, polish, credentials, or institutional prestige.
The team needs to separate genuine domain authority from authority spillover into adjacent domains.

failure_modes.mode: High-status proposals can become hard to challenge, pulling the team into confirmation bias around t...
failure_modes.description: High-status proposals can become hard to challenge, pulling the team into confirmation bias around the authority's idea.
failure_modes.mitigation: Decouple status from idea: require team members to write opinions independently before sharing, or read them in reverse order of seniority.
failure_modes.source_quote: Authority figures often present proposals that are difficult to challenge because they are associated with high-status proponents or sacred beliefs.

failure_modes.mode: Blind spot barriers and motivated reasoning can make smart people better at rationalizing the author...
failure_modes.description: Blind spot barriers and motivated reasoning can make smart people better at rationalizing the authority's conclusion.
failure_modes.mitigation: Insist on the logic and rationale: ask the authority to provide their reasoning chain and steps, not just the final recommendation.
failure_modes.source_quote: Smart people are particularly susceptible to this risk because they are skilled at constructing compelling narratives to rationalize the expert's conclusion.

failure_modes.mode: Wrong-expert domain dependence lets teams borrow authority from the wrong toolkit.
failure_modes.description: Wrong-expert domain dependence lets teams borrow authority from the wrong toolkit.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use believability weighting: assess who is most likely to be right based on track records and documented expertise in the specific domain at hand.
failure_modes.source_quote: Accepting advice from an authority figure whose expertise does not transfer to the domain at hand ... can lead to suboptimal solutions.

premortem_questions.description: Does the person have the credentials to speak with authority? Which credentials?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Does the person have the credentials to speak with authority? Which credentials?

premortem_questions.description: What about the person causes you to either trust implicitly or react with initial distrust?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What about the person causes you to either trust implicitly or react with initial distrust?

premortem_questions.description: What are the underlying assumptions embedded in this framework or solution?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What are the underlying assumptions embedded in this framework or solution?

heuristics.description: Decouple status from idea quality by collecting judgments independently before the group discussion starts.
heuristics.source_quote: require team members to write down initial opinions and rationales independently before discussing them

heuristics.description: Challenge the basis of authority instead of accepting prestige markers as proof.
heuristics.source_quote: Cultivate a mindset that values inquiry and skepticism, recognizing that A way of seeing is also a way of not seeing.

heuristics.description: Insist on the logic and rationale by asking for the thought chain, not just the answer.
heuristics.source_quote: focus on the How. When seeking advice from an expert, ask them to provide their thought chain and reasoning steps, rather than just the final recommendation.

heuristics.description: Simulate and test authority views with red-team or separate-judgment methods before deference hardens into consensus.
heuristics.source_quote: Employ techniques like team voting to overcome authority bias or use red-team/blue-team competitions to explore alternative perspectives.

danger_when: Credibility is standing in for falsification, evidence testing, or domain-boundary checks.
The problem requires multiple viewpoints, but the group is collapsing too early around one high-status voice.
The authority cue comes mostly from prestige markers rather than relevant track record in the decision's actual domain.
authority-bias.name: Authority Bias
reframing_when: The question imports its framing from an authority figure, expert consensus, or institutional position without testing whether that authority's frame actually fits the current situation.
The question treats the authority's framing as the premise rather than as one hypothesis — the borrowed frame has not been earned through examination of the specific situation.
The question dismisses alternative framings because they contradict expert or institutional opinion, rather than evaluating them on structural merit.
The question conflates the authority's domain expertise with frame-setting authority — the expert may know the answer space but may be framing the wrong question.
authority-bias.reframing_move: Before accepting the authority's framing, ask: 'Is this expert right about the problem definition, or just about the answer to a problem they defined? What would the question look like if we set the frame ourselves?' — the authority-bias reframe separates expertise within a frame from expertise at choosing the frame.

select_when: A forecast, diagnosis, budget, or estimate is vulnerable to a vivid inside-view story that could drown out historical frequency.
A credible reference class exists and the team can compare the current case to that class before arguing from specialness.
The decision depends on calibrating likely ranges and tail outcomes rather than defending a single optimistic base case.
The case feels special mainly because the local narrative is vivid, emotional, or expert-driven.

failure_modes.mode: Base-rate neglect lets vivid case details displace the general frequency of the outcome.
failure_modes.description: Base-rate neglect lets vivid case details displace the general frequency of the outcome.
failure_modes.mitigation: Anchor to the outside view first: collect historical frequency data for the reference class before incorporating case-specific details.
failure_modes.source_quote: Ignoring the general frequency of an outcome because specific case details are compelling, vivid, or emotionally salient.

failure_modes.mode: Substituting a simpler, more readily available model ... often resulting in selecting the wrong reference class.
failure_modes.description: Availability or substitution bias can push the team into the wrong reference class.
failure_modes.mitigation: Scrutinize reference class fit: line up all assumptions that underpin the chosen class and test whether the current case matches each one.
failure_modes.source_quote: Substituting a simpler, more readily available model ... often resulting in selecting the wrong reference class.

failure_modes.mode: Inside-view overconfidence can overwhelm the outside view and distort the prior.
failure_modes.description: Inside-view overconfidence can overwhelm the outside view and distort the prior.
failure_modes.mitigation: Cultivate rationality and humility: recognize that high intelligence does not confer immunity to bias; adopt radical open-mindedness about the base-rate data.
failure_modes.source_quote: Over-reliance on personal experience and beliefs, often amplified in smart people who spin better narratives for their own viewpoint.

premortem_questions.description: What is the average success/failure rate for projects or situations like this, regardless of our unique details?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What is the average success/failure rate for projects/situations like this, regardless of our unique details?

premortem_questions.description: Do the underlying structures and core assumptions of this reference group actually match our current situation, or are we confusing familiarity with fit?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Do the underlying structures and core assumptions of this reference group actually match our current situation, or are we confusing familiarity with fit?

premortem_questions.description: If a believable expert saw only the statistics and not our story, what outcome would they call most likely?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If a believable expert, viewing only the statistics and not my personal narrative, were to assess this, what would they say is the most likely outcome?

heuristics.description: Anchor to the outside view before arguing from the particulars of the case.
heuristics.source_quote: Anchoring to the Outside View: The primary actionable framework derived from this model is the discipline of actively anchoring first to the outside view.

heuristics.description: Use reference class forecasting to compare the case against structurally similar outcomes.
heuristics.source_quote: Reference Class Forecasting: This involves defining a group of past projects or outcomes that are structurally similar to the current situation.

heuristics.description: Apply the Bayes or conditional-probability approach by starting with the prior and then updating with the new evidence.
heuristics.source_quote: The Bayes/Conditional Probability Approach ... incorporating the prior probability (the base rate) before integrating the new, specific evidence.

danger_when: The chosen reference class is too broad, too narrow, or structurally mismatched, making the prior look rigorous while actually misleading the decision.
A real regime shift or causal break is present, but the team is still treating historical frequencies as if nothing important changed.
The prior is being used as a substitute for updating on high-quality new evidence instead of as the starting point for that update.
base-rates.name: Base Rates
base-rates.input_type: forecast or diagnosis with a usable reference class
reframing_when: The question is framed entirely from the inside view — the specific case, the vivid narrative, the founders' passion — without anchoring to the outside view of what historically happens in the reference class.
The question asks 'will our plan work?' without first asking 'what is the historical success rate for plans like ours?' — base-rate neglect is shaping the inquiry.
The question treats the current case as unique when a credible reference class exists that would set a very different expectation for the outcome.
The question focuses on the mean or expected outcome without asking about the distribution — particularly the tail outcomes one or two standard deviations from the base case.
base-rates.reframing_move: Anchor to the outside view: instead of asking about your specific case, ask 'what happens historically when companies/teams/projects like ours attempt this?' — the reference class forecasting reframe replaces inherited narrative with empirical prior.

select_when: A team lacks a shared starting picture of the problem, success criteria, or the facts that later comparisons should be judged against.
Diagnosis, measurement, or ownership depends on agreeing what normal, broken, or improved actually mean.
Later intervention quality will be judged by change from an explicit starting condition rather than by anecdotes.
Work is beginning to sprawl because the goal, frame, or required output has not been clearly set.

failure_modes.mode: Successful outcomes are predicated on defining a clear, accurate starting point.
failure_modes.description: Teams waste effort when they launch analysis or execution without agreeing on the starting condition, target outcome, or frame for comparison.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate goal-setting from solution design: define the starting condition, target, and comparison frame as distinct steps before any analysis begins.
failure_modes.source_quote: Successful outcomes are predicated on defining a clear, accurate starting point.

failure_modes.mode: Improvement claims become impressionistic when normal, broken, and improved were never defined up fr...
failure_modes.description: Improvement claims become impressionistic when normal, broken, and improved were never defined up front.
failure_modes.mitigation: Spell out all assumptions using 'what would you have to believe?' and define measurable thresholds for normal, broken, and improved before intervening.
failure_modes.source_quote: Best used when later intervention quality will be judged by change from an explicit starting condition rather than by impressionistic anecdotes.

failure_modes.mode: Danger when the initial baseline fossilizes into doctrine.
failure_modes.description: A baseline can mislead once it fossilizes into doctrine or tracks a convenient metric that is weakly tied to the real driver of success.
failure_modes.mitigation: Stress-test the baseline against edge cases and revisit it periodically; ask whether the tracked metric still causally drives the outcome or has become a vanity measure.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the initial baseline fossilizes into doctrine.

premortem_questions.description: What exactly is our starting condition, and what evidence says that starting picture is accurate enough to compare against later?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Baseline Establishment is clarity of the starting condition.

premortem_questions.description: Are we defining the outcome first, or are we already drifting into execution and solution design?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Goal setting must be done without thinking about solutions or diagnosis.

premortem_questions.description: Which baseline metric is easiest to measure but least tied to the real causal driver we care about?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger when the chosen baseline metric is convenient to measure but weakly tied to the real causal drivers of success or failure.

heuristics.description: Define the starting picture and success criterion before expanding the work.
heuristics.source_quote: All the successful execution in the world is worthless if it’s not in service of the right outcome.

heuristics.description: Use the baseline as a comparison anchor, but keep checking whether the environment has shifted enough to require a reset.
heuristics.source_quote: Danger when the initial baseline fossilizes into doctrine and the team keeps optimizing around an outdated starting frame.

heuristics.description: Do not confuse having a metric with having the right baseline.
heuristics.source_quote: Danger when the chosen baseline metric is convenient to measure but weakly tied to the real causal drivers of success or failure.

danger_when: The initial baseline has fossilized into doctrine even though the environment or goal has materially shifted.
The chosen baseline metric is convenient to measure but weakly tied to the real causal drivers of success or failure.
baseline-establishment.name: Baseline Establishment

select_when: Negotiation leverage depends on knowing the walk-away path before entering a pressured conversation.
The team must compare a proposed deal against a credible outside option rather than against hope or sunk effort.
Clarity about fallback options changes bargaining posture, sequencing, and the willingness to say no.

failure_modes.mode: Confirmation Bias
failure_modes.description: Confirmation bias can make the current deal look stronger by filtering the real quality of the walk-away option.
failure_modes.mitigation: Enforce the dialectic: challenge every favorable assessment of the deal with its antithesis and require independent evaluation of the walk-away option.
failure_modes.source_quote: Confirmation Bias

failure_modes.mode: Overemphasis on the Base Case
failure_modes.description: Overemphasis on the base case can hide how bad the downside becomes if the preferred negotiation path fails.
failure_modes.mitigation: Stress-test assumptions against outcomes one or two standard deviations from the mean; incorporate reference-class history of similar negotiations.
failure_modes.source_quote: Overemphasis on the Base Case

failure_modes.mode: Premature Execution
failure_modes.description: Premature execution can rush the team into action before the real fallback path and constraints are clear.
failure_modes.mitigation: Promote deliberation before action: distinguish between values and beliefs, map constraints explicitly, and define the BATNA threshold before entering negotiation.
failure_modes.source_quote: Premature Execution

premortem_questions.description: What would we have to believe to accept a contrasting viewpoint, and are we truly open-minded enough to consider it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would we have to believe to accept a contrasting viewpoint, and are we truly open-minded enough to consider it?

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.description: What specific assumptions underlying this framework or alternative are not being met in our current reality?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What specific assumptions underlying this framework (or alternative) are not being met in our current reality?

heuristics.description: Use MECE to compare alternatives cleanly instead of letting options blur together.
heuristics.source_quote: MECE (Mutually Exclusive, Collectively Exhaustive)

heuristics.description: Model competitive responses with game theory before treating the current deal as the only serious branch.
heuristics.source_quote: Game Theory Thinking

heuristics.description: Keep a one-day answer so the best current fallback path is always coherent enough to act on.
heuristics.source_quote: What's the one-day answer?

danger_when: People are declaring a fallback as their BATNA even though it is vague, unexecutable, or worse than continuing to negotiate.
The negotiation is being framed around the preferred deal path without writing down minimum acceptable terms or no-go thresholds first.
batna.name: Batna

select_when: Beliefs should update in proportion to new evidence rather than flip from certainty to certainty after each datapoint.
The decision has some usable signal, but confidence still depends on combining priors, base rates, and fresh evidence.
A short run of wins, losses, or experiments is being treated as proof that the underlying probability structure changed.
The real dispute is whether to update the prior, gather more disconfirming evidence, or declare that the environment has structurally changed.

failure_modes.mode: The Risk of Subjective Priors
failure_modes.description: Subjective priors can make a mathematically tidy update land on a badly distorted conclusion.
failure_modes.mitigation: Anchor to the outside view: consult external reference classes to set the prior before adjusting for case-specific details.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Risk of Subjective Priors

failure_modes.mode: Over-reliance on Simple Models (Maslow’s Hammer)
failure_modes.description: Substitution bias can smuggle in an easier but wrong model instead of the one the problem actually needs.
failure_modes.mitigation: Enforce dialectic standards: require every model choice to meet its antithesis before accepting it as the update framework.
failure_modes.source_quote: Over-reliance on Simple Models (Maslow’s Hammer)

failure_modes.mode: Danger When the Math Becomes Decorative
failure_modes.description: False precision can make the math decorative when the prior or evidence quality has not earned the rigor.
failure_modes.mitigation: Practice intellectual humility: externalize the reasoning structure in diagrams or concept maps and verify that each input has earned its precision level.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When the Math Becomes Decorative

premortem_questions.description: What assumptions undergird this initial probability?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What assumptions undergird this initial probability (prior)?"

premortem_questions.description: What would we have to believe for the opposite hypothesis to be true?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What would we have to believe for the opposite hypothesis to be true?"

premortem_questions.description: What specific facts must be true for this plan to succeed, and how certain are we of each fact?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What specific facts must be true for this plan to succeed, and how certain are we of each fact?"

heuristics.description: Quantify the prior before collecting more evidence so later updates have a visible starting point.
heuristics.source_quote: Quantify Prior Beliefs

heuristics.description: Update iteratively so each posterior becomes the next prior instead of treating each new datapoint as a reset.
heuristics.source_quote: Embrace Iteration and Convergence

heuristics.description: Anchor to the outside view and aggregated data before trusting the inside view story.
heuristics.source_quote: Anchor to the Outside View

danger_when: The prior is weak, politically motivated, or decorative enough that numerical updates would only create false precision.
The team is treating a familiar but mismatched model as if it were the right prior for a genuinely novel problem.
bayesian.name: Bayesian

select_when: A review or debate risks turning into a diagnosis of everyone else's distortions while leaving the team's own reasoning unexamined.
The team sounds self-aware in the abstract, but there is little evidence that outside-view checks or structured reflection are actually changing judgment.
Expertise or intelligence may be amplifying rationalization by making the current story easier to defend rather than easier to test.
The next move should introduce external checks, simplification, or journaling that makes hidden self-serving assumptions harder to sustain.

failure_modes.mode: Awareness of the bias blind spot can become performative self-knowledge that sounds humble without c...
failure_modes.description: Awareness of the bias blind spot can become performative self-knowledge that sounds humble without changing how evidence is weighed.
failure_modes.mitigation: Run a pre-mortem: imagine the project failed and ask what went wrong, forcing the team to surface the biases that performative awareness would have missed.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when awareness of the bias blind spot becomes performative self-knowledge that sounds humble without changing how evidence is weighed.

failure_modes.mode: The model can be used mainly to diagnose other people's distortions, recreating the same blind spot ...
failure_modes.description: The model can be used mainly to diagnose other people's distortions, recreating the same blind spot at a higher level.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use triangulation and peer review: introduce diversity of thinking through mock trials or red-team exercises that turn the diagnosis inward.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the model is used mainly to diagnose other people's distortions, which recreates the same blind spot at a higher level.

failure_modes.mode: being smart can actually make bias worse
failure_modes.description: Intelligence can enlarge the blind spot by improving the quality of rationalizations that defend a preferred story.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use structured reflection: look at patterns of your past mistakes and ask whether you would make the same decision with more time, more data, or different incentives.
failure_modes.source_quote: being smart can actually make bias worse

premortem_questions.description: What if our awareness of this bias changes nothing about how we are currently weighing evidence?
premortem_questions.source_quote: simply knowing about the Bias Blind Spot is often not enough

premortem_questions.description: What external check, peer challenge, or calibration tool could disconfirm the story we currently find most flattering?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Triangulation and Peer Review

premortem_questions.description: Where are we assuming we are the exception instead of subjecting our own reasoning to the same test we apply to others?
premortem_questions.source_quote: the tendency to be much better at recognizing biased reasoning in others than in oneself

heuristics.description: Use the outside view and external calibration when strong self-confidence would otherwise dominate local judgment.
heuristics.source_quote: Cultivating the Outside View

heuristics.description: Flip the lens around with an academic selfie before critiquing others so the first target of scrutiny is your own story.
heuristics.source_quote: The Academic Selfie

heuristics.description: Force simple explanation or journaling so hidden assumptions and rationalizations have fewer places to hide.
heuristics.source_quote: The Feynman Technique

danger_when: The model is being used mainly to brand other people as biased rather than to create a process that can expose the local blind spot.
The team already has strong external instrumentation and the main issue is now missing information rather than self-protective interpretation.
bias-blind-spot.name: Bias Blind Spot

select_when: A plan faces deep uncertainty where average-case forecasts may badly understate tail exposure or fragility.
The team needs to shift from prediction theater toward vulnerability reduction, scenario analysis, or stress-testing because unknown unknowns matter.
A complex system could fail through cascading effects even if each individual component looks tolerable on its own.
The next move should improve resilience and decision quality under surprise rather than pretending the rare event can be forecast precisely.

failure_modes.mode: Danger of Tail-Risk Theater
failure_modes.description: Teams can invoke Black Swan language to justify vague fear, permanent defensiveness, or refusal to prioritize even when concrete resilience moves are available.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use simulations and stress-testing to convert vague tail-risk fear into concrete exposure boundaries, buffers, and monitoring thresholds.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger of Tail-Risk Theater

failure_modes.mode: Overemphasis on the Aberration
failure_modes.description: An aberration can be over-weighted until strategy starts treating the outlier as the new normal.
failure_modes.mitigation: Contextualize the aberration: step back, zoom out, and compare against reference-class data to confirm whether the outlier represents a structural shift or a statistical extreme.
failure_modes.source_quote: Overemphasis on the Aberration

failure_modes.mode: The Rewriting of History (Hindsight Bias)
failure_modes.description: Hindsight can rewrite history and make a genuinely surprising event feel obvious after the fact, blocking real learning.
failure_modes.mitigation: Enforce post-mortem discipline: resist rewriting history, focus on calibrating beliefs, and invite team members to share what they were genuinely uncertain about before the event.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Rewriting of History (Hindsight Bias)

premortem_questions.description: If this decision fails, what is the least obvious reason why that we are not discussing now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If this decision leads to failure, what is the least obvious reason why, that we are not discussing now?

premortem_questions.description: What core strategy must we execute regardless of whether this specific catastrophic event occurs or not?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What core strategy must we execute, regardless of whether this specific catastrophic event occurs or not?

premortem_questions.description: If one essential component fails unexpectedly, what cascading implications should we already be prepared for?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If one essential component of our complex system fails unexpectedly, what are the cascading implications?

heuristics.description: Use systems thinking to inspect interrelationships and failure propagation rather than isolated events.
heuristics.source_quote: Systematic Exploration through Systems Thinking

heuristics.description: Shift from prediction toward scenario analysis and outcome simulation when deep uncertainty makes point forecasts brittle.
heuristics.source_quote: Scenario Analysis

heuristics.description: Separate good decisions from good outcomes so bad luck does not automatically discredit a sound process.
heuristics.source_quote: a good decision may sometimes lead to a bad outcome

danger_when: Black Swan language is being used as vague fear rhetoric even though concrete resilience or prioritization moves are available.
An aberration is being treated as the new normal in a way that would distort strategy and create unnecessary paralysis.
black-swan-events.name: Black Swan Events

select_when: The work requires diagnosing whether someone is stuck at recall, understanding, application, analysis, evaluation, or synthesis rather than treating learning as one vague block.
Skill-building needs a progression from comprehension to execution to judgment instead of a jump straight to critique.
Curriculum, coaching, or onboarding must sequence challenge so deeper performance rests on real foundations.
A learner or team needs a structured roadmap for what kind of thinking comes next.

failure_modes.mode: Evaluation Without Foundation
failure_modes.description: Teams can confuse surface familiarity with real capability when they try to evaluate or critique before enough analysis and understanding exist.
failure_modes.mitigation: Check which cognitive level the team has actually earned: can they explain the concept in plain language and apply it to a new case before judging quality?
failure_modes.source_quote: Evaluation Without Foundation

failure_modes.mode: Danger when the taxonomy is treated as a rigid staircase.
failure_modes.description: A rigid staircase view can freeze learning when iteration, creativity, or revisiting earlier levels is actually required.
failure_modes.mitigation: Treat the levels as a diagnostic guide, not a gate: allow iteration between levels when the problem demands revisiting earlier understanding.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the taxonomy is treated as a rigid staircase.

failure_modes.mode: Analysis Paralysis
failure_modes.description: Overemphasis on lower stages can turn into analysis paralysis that never becomes application or creation.
failure_modes.mitigation: Impose a forcing function to move up: set a deadline for when understanding must become application, and hold the team to it.
failure_modes.source_quote: Analysis Paralysis

premortem_questions.description: At which stage is this learner or team actually stuck: recall, understanding, application, analysis, evaluation, or synthesis?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when the work requires diagnosing whether someone is stuck at recall, understanding, application, or synthesis.

premortem_questions.description: Where does our explanation still fall apart, showing that we have not really built enough understanding to move upward yet?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Where does my explanation start to fall apart?

premortem_questions.description: Are we solving the right problem, or are we climbing stages on top of a flawed definition?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Are we solving the right problem-it may not be the one we were given.

heuristics.description: Sequence challenge so comprehension supports application, application supports analysis, and analysis supports later judgment and synthesis.
heuristics.source_quote: Works best when skill-building needs a progression from comprehension to execution to judgment.

heuristics.description: Use simple explanation and blind-spot checks before asking learners to make complex evaluative leaps.
heuristics.source_quote: The Feynman Technique

heuristics.description: Allow iteration back to earlier levels whenever real learning reveals that the foundation is still weak.
heuristics.source_quote: The presentation of the taxonomy as a literal, gated, and sequential process may overlook the reality that complex learning is often iterative and fluid.

danger_when: The taxonomy is being treated as a rigid staircase that blocks iteration, creativity, or revisiting earlier levels when real learning loops back.
People are trying to evaluate or critique before they have actually analyzed the problem or built enough understanding to support judgment.
blooms-taxonomy.name: Blooms Taxonomy

select_when: Local optimization is masking the true throughput limiter.
Sequence and capacity matter more than aggregate effort.
Fixing one choke point could unlock disproportionate downstream gain.
A migration, launch, or handoff plan assumes every workstream can move in parallel even though one gating dependency paces the whole system.

failure_modes.mode: Mistaking Symptoms for the Constraint
failure_modes.description: Teams can mistake the noisiest queue or highest-status pain point for the real constraint.
failure_modes.mitigation: Trace the flow end to end and measure where work-in-progress actually accumulates; the real constraint is where throughput is physically limited, not where complaints are loudest.
failure_modes.source_quote: Mistaking Symptoms for the Constraint

failure_modes.mode: Parallelizing Around A Gating Dependency
failure_modes.description: Parallelizing around a gating dependency can make the roadmap look busy while throughput stays pinned.
failure_modes.mitigation: Subordinate all other work to the constraint: do not optimize non-bottleneck steps until the gating step is resolved or its capacity is expanded.
failure_modes.source_quote: Parallelizing Around A Gating Dependency

failure_modes.mode: Availability Heuristic / Substitution Bias
failure_modes.description: Availability or substitution bias can push the team toward a familiar frame instead of the true system limiter.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test multiple frames against the throughput data; if a different model identifies a different constraint, the first diagnosis was biased by familiarity.
failure_modes.source_quote: Availability Heuristic / Substitution Bias

premortem_questions.description: Which step would still throttle the whole system if I funded every other improvement on the list?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Which step would still throttle the whole system if I funded every other improvement on the list?

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.description: How does what you're doing solve the problem, and how does it advance your thinking?
premortem_questions.source_quote: How does what you’re doing solve the problem? and How does it advance your thinking?

heuristics.description: Find the key driver instead of optimizing every visible pain point.
heuristics.source_quote: Prioritization via Key Drivers

heuristics.description: Use the Pareto principle to concentrate resources on the few inputs that unlock most throughput.
heuristics.source_quote: The Pareto Principle (80/20 Rule)

heuristics.description: Manage cognitive load by chunking and scaffolding when the bottleneck is working-memory capacity.
heuristics.source_quote: Cognitive Load Management

danger_when: The team is labeling the noisiest queue, most visible complaint, or highest-status pain point as the bottleneck without verifying actual throughput.
Constraint analysis is becoming a substitute for execution, or the model is naming the bottleneck without addressing how the constrained step will actually improve.
bottlenecks.name: Bottlenecks

select_when: A project, analysis, or negotiation needs explicit scope lines before effort spreads into unowned terrain.
The current work is overloaded because limits on responsibility, attention, or working-memory burden have not been set clearly enough.
A team needs to distinguish what is in frame, out of frame, and who owns each part before deeper analysis becomes useful.
The next useful move is to define constraints that protect purpose rather than let complexity expand by default.

failure_modes.mode: Danger When Boundaries Protect Comfort Instead of Purpose
failure_modes.description: Boundaries fail when they are drawn to protect comfort, politics, or convenience instead of the actual purpose of the work.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit whether boundaries serve the work or protect comfort: ask whether moving the boundary would improve the outcome even if it creates discomfort.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Boundaries Protect Comfort Instead of Purpose

failure_modes.mode: Premature Narrowing of Inquiry
failure_modes.description: Premature narrowing can kill options and block the generation of better explanations or solutions.
failure_modes.mitigation: Delay narrowing until at least two alternative framings have been explored; premature closure is more expensive than the extra search time.
failure_modes.source_quote: Premature Narrowing of Inquiry

failure_modes.mode: failure to jump boundaries and instead apply an existing model
failure_modes.description: Boundary discipline breaks when familiar frameworks are applied across situations that require jumping the existing frame.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test the boundary against a novel frame from a different discipline; if the familiar framework cannot accommodate the new case, the boundary is misplaced.
failure_modes.source_quote: failure to jump boundaries and instead apply an existing model

premortem_questions.description: What is this work actually supposed to cover, and what is deliberately out of scope?
premortem_questions.source_quote: The Boundaries of Your Analysis

premortem_questions.description: Are these limits serving the goal, or are they mainly protecting us from difficult evidence or accountability?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger When Boundaries Protect Comfort Instead of Purpose

premortem_questions.description: What important option disappears if we narrow the frame right now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Premature Narrowing of Inquiry

heuristics.description: Formalize the scope and expected output before the analysis sprawls.
heuristics.source_quote: Formalizing the Contract and Scope

heuristics.description: Use boundaries to respect real cognitive limits by breaking work into genuinely manageable chunks.
heuristics.source_quote: genuinely manageable small chunks

heuristics.description: Keep boundaries purpose-aligned and revisit them when they start protecting convenience instead of clarity.
heuristics.source_quote: serve the real objective

danger_when: Scope lines are being drawn mainly to avoid inconvenient evidence, hard conversations, or accountability.
The inquiry is being narrowed so early that important options, causes, or adjacent realities will be filtered out before they are understood.
boundaries.name: Boundaries
reframing_when: The question assumes that the current system boundary is the right boundary — who is inside, who is outside, what is 'our problem' versus 'their problem' — when moving the boundary would redefine the problem.

The question treats scope as fixed ('this is a marketing problem', 'this is an engineering problem') when the real leverage comes from redrawing the boundary to include or exclude stakeholders, costs, or effects.
The question externalizes consequences by drawing the system boundary too narrowly, so that effects on parties outside the boundary are invisible in the problem definition.
The question conflates the organizational boundary with the problem boundary, assuming that the problem stops where the org chart stops.
boundaries.reframing_move: Before accepting the problem scope as given, ask: 'Where have we drawn the boundary of this problem, and what changes if we move it — inward, outward, or to include a different set of actors?' — the boundary reframe makes the scope choice explicit and negotiable.

select_when: The team is stuck too early on one frame or one answer and needs to widen the option set before choosing a path.
Idea volume is genuinely valuable because the search space is still underexplored.
Novelty, reframing, or hidden alternatives matter more than early precision.
Divergence can be separated from evaluation so the group can generate before ranking.

failure_modes.mode: Most useful when the team is stuck too early on one frame or one answer.
failure_modes.description: Teams can lock onto one answer too early and never discover better options that only appear once the search space is widened.
failure_modes.mitigation: Enforce a minimum idea count before any evaluation begins; early convergence kills the search space that produces non-obvious options.
