The question assumes a plan or strategy will work without identifying the specific conditions under which it would fail — the sensitivity analysis question ('by how much would assumptions need to change for this to be wrong?') is absent.
falsifiability.reframing_move: Invert the question toward disproof: instead of 'will X work?', ask 'what specific condition would prove X wrong, and can we test for that condition now?' — the hypothesis pyramid reframe makes assumptions concrete enough to break.

select_when: Outcomes should shape the next action and the system can observe consequences quickly enough to learn instead of repeating the same move blindly.
Behavior changes the environment and the environment changes behavior back, so the problem depends on circular rather than one-way causality.
Measurement can actually close the loop because signals are tied to real levers and the next adjustment can follow from what the system just produced.
The work happens inside a complex dynamic system where delays, adaptation, and iteration matter more than a static snapshot.

failure_modes.mode: Drift to Low Performance
failure_modes.description: A system can drift to low performance when bad results quietly reset the standard instead of triggering correction.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set explicit performance standards and alarm thresholds so that bad results trigger correction instead of quietly resetting the baseline.
failure_modes.source_quote: Drift to Low Performance

failure_modes.mode: Premature Action/Impulsivity
failure_modes.description: Impulsivity can push the team into action before enough feedback has been gathered to understand the consequences.
failure_modes.mitigation: Gather enough feedback before acting: impulsivity that precedes understanding creates oscillation, not progress.
failure_modes.source_quote: Premature Action/Impulsivity

failure_modes.mode: The Implementation Gap
failure_modes.description: The implementation gap appears when analysis describes what to do but the loop never closes into how the system will actually do it.
failure_modes.mitigation: Close the loop from analysis to execution: describe not just what to do but how the system will actually do it and when it will know whether it worked.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Implementation Gap

premortem_questions.description: What core standards have we failed to meet in the past, and how are we ensuring those same metrics remain absolute this quarter?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What core standards have we failed to meet in the past, and how are we ensuring those same metrics remain absolute this quarter?

premortem_questions.description: Have we detailed how this will be executed, or only what should be done?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Have we detailed how this will be executed, or only what should be done? Is the strategy embedded into the continuous workflow, or is it a static document?

premortem_questions.description: Are we mistreating this system because we are unaware of the delays, limiting factors, or false boundaries influencing the results?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Are we mistreating this system because we are unaware of the delays, limiting factors, or false boundaries influencing the results?

heuristics.description: Refine the next move iteratively based on what the last output revealed instead of restarting from scratch.
heuristics.source_quote: Iterative Refinement (Cognitive Science/AI)

heuristics.description: Run the goal-problem-diagnose-design-do cycle continuously so results feed back into the next round of action.
heuristics.source_quote: The 5-Step Process (Business Strategy)

heuristics.description: Use an iterative define-build-test rhythm that revisits earlier stages when later feedback reveals a better path.
heuristics.source_quote: Design Thinking

danger_when: The organization is collecting signal but never translating it into changed behavior, priorities, or design.
The loop is being treated as progress even though the team is stuck in endless questioning or one-way communication that never closes into action.
feedback-loops.name: Feedback Loops

select_when: A feedback conversation needs to stay tied to specific observable behavior rather than personality labels or vague frustration.
The goal is to reduce defensiveness by separating what happened, why it mattered, and what change is being invited next.
Coaching, performance repair, or collaboration quality depends on recurring course correction around concrete incidents.

failure_modes.mode: Feedback can become a dressed-up judgment if the structure skips curiosity about the receiver's actu...
failure_modes.description: Feedback can become a dressed-up judgment if the structure skips curiosity about the receiver's actual constraints.
failure_modes.mitigation: Lead with curiosity about the receiver's actual constraints before stating the behavior and its impact; structure that skips empathy becomes dressed-up judgment.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the model is used as a script to deliver judgment while skipping curiosity, listening, or the receiver's actual constraints.

failure_modes.mode: Selective framing can make the feedback look objective while quietly smuggling in a predetermined co...
failure_modes.description: Selective framing can make the feedback look objective while quietly smuggling in a predetermined conclusion.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit the selection of evidence: check whether the behavior-impact story was assembled to discover or to confirm a predetermined conclusion.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the situation is framed selectively so the structure looks objective while smuggling in a preloaded conclusion.

failure_modes.mode: A feedback process can become highly convincing but still wrong if the initial information step is i...
failure_modes.description: A feedback process can become highly convincing but still wrong if the initial information step is incomplete or motivated.
failure_modes.mitigation: Verify the initial information step against observable evidence; a feedback process built on incomplete or motivated observation will be convincing but wrong.
failure_modes.source_quote: If the initial data is incomplete or influenced by motivated reasoning, the structured process merely yields a highly convincing bad decision.

premortem_questions.description: What observable behavior am I actually pointing to, and what conclusion am I only inferring?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Provide concrete, specific observations of the action or approach

premortem_questions.description: What effect did this behavior have on the person, goal, or system that the receiver can recognize as real?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Explain the effect the action or approach has on you or the system or goal.

premortem_questions.description: What would a collaborative invitation sound like if I were not trying to win the argument?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Extend a collaboration request to address the action or approach.

heuristics.description: Anchor feedback in specific observable behavior before naming impact or implications.
heuristics.source_quote: Provide concrete, specific observations of the action or approach

heuristics.description: Separate the machine-level process problem from the case-at-hand reaction so the conversation improves the system, not just the mood.
heuristics.source_quote: conduct the discussion at two levels: the machine level and the case-at-hand level

heuristics.description: Pair critique with a real invitation to collaborate so the receiver has agency in the next move.
heuristics.source_quote: invitation to collaborate

danger_when: The structure is being used as a script for delivering judgment while skipping curiosity, listening, or the receiver's real constraints.
The situation is being framed selectively so the structure only launders a preloaded conclusion.
feedback-models-sbi.name: Feedback Models Sbi

select_when: Understanding needs to be pressure tested by explanation rather than assumed from recognition, jargon, or recall.
A concept feels familiar but the team needs to know whether it can be explained simply enough to reveal real comprehension.
The next move should expose blind spots by forcing a plain-language explanation before more confident action is taken.
Learning or reasoning quality depends on simplifying without losing the conditions that make the concept actually true.

failure_modes.mode: Simplification can strip out the technical conditions that the real decision still depends on.
failure_modes.description: Simplification can strip out the technical conditions that the real decision still depends on.
failure_modes.mitigation: Check that simplification has not stripped out the technical conditions the real decision depends on; omitting a critical qualifier makes the explanation dangerous.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when simplification strips out the technical conditions, edge cases, or math needed for correct decisions in the real domain.

failure_modes.mode: A smooth explanation can create false confidence when the concept has not been pressure tested in us...
failure_modes.description: A smooth explanation can create false confidence when the concept has not been pressure tested in use.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pressure-test the explanation in use: a smooth restatement that has never been applied creates false confidence about mastery.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when a smooth explanation is mistaken for mastery even though the concept has not been pressure tested in application.

failure_modes.mode: accurate but useless idea
failure_modes.description: The model fails when simplicity becomes so incomplete that the explanation is easy to repeat but useless for prediction or action.
failure_modes.mitigation: Ensure the simplified version is still useful for prediction or action; simplicity that cannot be used is just easy to repeat.
failure_modes.source_quote: accurate but useless idea

premortem_questions.description: Can I explain this in plain English without hiding behind jargon?
premortem_questions.source_quote: write down an explanation of the concept in plain English

premortem_questions.description: What part of this explanation still depends on words I cannot unpack?
premortem_questions.source_quote: identify mental blind spots

premortem_questions.description: What technical condition or edge case would break this simplified story?
premortem_questions.source_quote: technical conditions, edge cases, or math

heuristics.description: Explain the concept plainly enough that any missing understanding becomes visible.
heuristics.source_quote: write down an explanation of the concept in plain English

heuristics.description: Use analogy to connect the new idea to an existing schema only after the core concept is actually understood.
heuristics.source_quote: use analogy to fill blind spots

heuristics.description: Treat simplification as a comprehension test, not as permission to ignore the domain's real constraints.
heuristics.source_quote: simplicity is crucial ... but sometimes the real truth is initially complex

danger_when: Simplification would strip away the technical conditions, edge cases, or math that the real decision still depends on.
A smooth explanation is being mistaken for mastery even though the idea has not been tested in application or prediction.
feynman-technique.name: Feynman Technique

select_when: Existing categories, market assumptions, or inherited rules are distorting the problem rather than clarifying it.
The team needs to separate facts from conventions before deciding what is actually non-negotiable.
A novel solution requires rebuilding the argument from elemental truths instead of optimizing a stale template.
The current recommendation sounds polished, but no one has exposed the premises that make it true.

failure_modes.mode: Anchoring and Framing Biases
failure_modes.description: Anchoring and framing can corrupt first principles work before the reasoning even starts by locking attention onto the wrong premise.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit the starting premises: anchoring and framing can corrupt first-principles work before the reasoning even begins.
failure_modes.source_quote: Anchoring and Framing Biases

failure_modes.mode: Confirmation Bias and Groupthink
failure_modes.description: Confirmation bias and groupthink can make teams defend the first chosen principles instead of testing them against opposing evidence.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require the principles to survive opposition: confirmation bias and groupthink will defend the first chosen premises unless the antithesis is actively tested.
failure_modes.source_quote: Confirmation Bias and Groupthink

failure_modes.mode: Analysis Paralysis (The Perfectionist Trap)
failure_modes.description: Analysis paralysis can turn first principles into endless premise polishing rather than actionable understanding.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a deadline for moving from premises to actionable understanding; endless premise polishing is analysis paralysis wearing first-principles clothing.
failure_modes.source_quote: Analysis Paralysis (The Perfectionist Trap)

premortem_questions.description: What would we have to believe to accept the opposite thesis?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What would we have to believe to accept the opposite thesis?"

premortem_questions.description: What is the single core point we want the audience to remember?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What is the single core point we want the audience to remember?"

premortem_questions.description: Are we unknowingly influenced by our perspective and have we actively tried different frames?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "Are we unknowingly influenced by our perspective (frame of reference), and have we actively tried to look from a variety of different frames?"

heuristics.description: Use repeated 'why' questions to strip the problem down to its elemental truths.
heuristics.source_quote: The "Why, Why, Why" Inquiry

heuristics.description: Disaggregate the problem into logical parts and stress-test the first hypothesis instead of reasoning from unexamined wholes.
heuristics.source_quote: Problem Disaggregation and Logic Trees

heuristics.description: Explain the rebuilt logic in plain English to reveal any blind spots that still hide behind abstraction.
heuristics.source_quote: Write down an explanation of the concept in plain English, simple enough for a five-year-old to understand, revealing gaps in mastery.

danger_when: First principles is being used as a status move to ignore practical constraints, accumulated know-how, or real implementation costs.
The analysis is boiling the ocean or stopping at abstraction instead of producing a concrete, actionable recommendation.
first-principles-thinking.name: First Principles Thinking
reframing_when: The question inherits premises from authority, convention, or industry norms without examining whether those premises are actually true — stripping them reveals a different problem.
The question is built on analogy to past situations rather than on the elemental truths of the current situation, and the analogy may not hold.
The question accepts existing categories or market definitions as given when those categories are the very thing distorting the solution space.
The question jumps to 'how do we optimize X' without first asking 'is X the right thing to be doing at all' — the forward-only formulation skips the foundational question.
first-principles-thinking.reframing_move: Strip the question to its elemental truths: what do we actually know is true here, independent of what we've been told or assumed? Rebuild the question from those truths — the reframed question often has a different subject than the original.

select_when: A recurring operational problem has an observable chain of causes and the team needs to get past symptom-fixing quickly.
Questioning can be tied to logs, process evidence, or physical facts rather than opinion, status, or blame.
The investigation can end in an actionable mechanism, design flaw, or missing control that someone can actually change.
The team needs a disciplined way to trace from the immediate failure back toward a falsifiable root cause.

failure_modes.mode: Root-cause tracing can fail when the first answer or suspect anchors the whole chain and every later...
failure_modes.description: Root-cause tracing can fail when the first answer or suspect anchors the whole chain and every later why is bent to support it.
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge the first answer instead of using it as a foundation for every subsequent why; early anchoring bends the whole chain.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the first answer or first suspect anchors the whole chain and every later "why" is steered to support that initial story.

failure_modes.mode: The method can oversimplify a nonlinear, multi-cause system by forcing it into one linear explanatio...
failure_modes.description: The method can oversimplify a nonlinear, multi-cause system by forcing it into one linear explanation.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use parallel causal branches when the system is nonlinear or multi-cause; forcing a single linear explanation oversimplifies.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when a nonlinear, multi-cause system is forced into a single linear thread and the team leaves out interaction effects, feedback loops, or parallel causes.

failure_modes.mode: Premature Cessation (Stopping at Proximate Cause)
failure_modes.description: Five Whys can stall at a proximate cause if the team stops questioning before it reaches an actionable mechanism or missing control.
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep asking until you reach an actionable mechanism or missing control; stopping at a proximate cause leaves the real root untouched.
failure_modes.source_quote: Premature Cessation (Stopping at Proximate Cause)

premortem_questions.description: What evidence ties this why-chain to logs, process facts, or physical reality rather than to opinion or blame?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Works best when the questioning is tied to logs, process evidence, or physical facts rather than opinion, status, or blame.

premortem_questions.description: If this chain is wrong, which interaction effect or parallel cause are we probably leaving out?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger when a nonlinear, multi-cause system is forced into a single linear thread and the team leaves out interaction effects, feedback loops, or parallel causes.

premortem_questions.description: Which why in this chain first reaches an actionable mechanism, design flaw, or missing control that someone can actually change?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best used when the investigation can end in an actionable mechanism, design flaw, or missing control that someone can actually change.

heuristics.description: Keep asking why until the chain reaches a root cause that is falsifiable and operationally actionable.
heuristics.source_quote: By forcing the investigator to reach a first principle or a falsifiable fact, the model strengthens the foundation of knowledge.

heuristics.description: Use the why-chain to populate a larger causal map when multiple causes are plausible instead of pretending the linear chain is enough by itself.
heuristics.source_quote: The Five Whys sequence can then be used to populate the "causes of causes" within the diagram.

heuristics.description: Tie each why to observable evidence so the conversation stays diagnostic instead of becoming opinion or blame management.
heuristics.source_quote: Works best when the questioning is tied to logs, process evidence, or physical facts rather than opinion, status, or blame.

danger_when: The first answer or first suspect is anchoring the whole chain and every later why is being steered to support that initial story.
A nonlinear, multi-cause system is being forced into a single linear thread while interaction effects, feedback loops, or parallel causes are left out.
five-whys-method.name: Five Whys Method
reframing_when: The question addresses a symptom or proximate cause without asking why that symptom exists — one why isn't enough.
The question has settled on a specific formulation ('how do we fix X?') without verifying that X is the root cause rather than a surface manifestation.
The question ends at an unexamined assertion or organizational assumption rather than a falsifiable fact — the chain of 'why' stopped at cultural myth instead of a first principle.
The question is about treating a recurring problem that keeps returning because the root cause has never been identified.
five-whys-method.reframing_move: Ask 'why?' repeatedly until the question shifts from the presenting symptom to the underlying cause: 'why is X happening?' leads to 'why is Y happening?' which eventually reveals that the real question isn't about X at all, but about Z — a deeper structural issue the surface question cannot see.

select_when: A task has a clear goal, rapid feedback, and challenge calibrated high enough to demand full attention without causing panic.
Deep work quality depends on sustained immersion rather than fragmented multitasking.
A person or team has enough skill to stretch at the edge of competence and benefit from uninterrupted concentration.
The main execution risk is broken attention rather than lack of understanding or missing options.

failure_modes.mode: dip below flow into boredom
failure_modes.description: The challenge can drift below skill and slide into boredom unless the task evolves with ability.
failure_modes.mitigation: Deliberately escalate challenge as skill grows; a static task level will slide from flow into boredom.
failure_modes.source_quote: dip below flow into boredom

failure_modes.mode: anxiety is thought without control
failure_modes.description: If the challenge outruns ability, the state flips from immersion into anxiety or frustration.
failure_modes.mitigation: Calibrate challenge to the current edge of ability; pushing too far flips the state from productive immersion into anxiety.
failure_modes.source_quote: anxiety is thought without control

failure_modes.mode: danger when the team is entering flow on the wrong problem
failure_modes.description: Intense immersion can create tunnel vision on the wrong problem if the big picture is never revisited.
failure_modes.mitigation: Schedule periodic big-picture check-ins during deep work to verify the immersion is solving the right problem.
failure_modes.source_quote: danger when the team is entering flow on the wrong problem

premortem_questions.description: Is the current challenge still sitting at the edge of competence, or has it become too easy or too overwhelming?
premortem_questions.source_quote: challenged at the peak of their ability

premortem_questions.description: What feedback loop will tell us quickly if this work is drifting out of the flow channel?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Immediate Feedback Loop

premortem_questions.description: How does this deep work block solve the ultimate problem instead of just feeling productive?
premortem_questions.source_quote: How does what I'm doing right now solve the ultimate problem?

heuristics.description: Match challenge to skill deliberately instead of assuming immersion will appear on its own.
heuristics.source_quote: The Matching Principle

heuristics.description: Structure deep work as repeated cycles of variable review, next-step attack, and consolidation.
heuristics.source_quote: deep work is an activity well suited to generate a flow state

heuristics.description: Increase challenge as skill grows so the system does not stagnate into boredom.
heuristics.source_quote: pursue new, more complex challenges

danger_when: The team is entering flow on the wrong problem and efficient execution is outrunning strategic sensemaking.
Feedback loops or coordination are too weak to keep immersion anchored to reality.
flow.name: Flow

select_when: A claim needs explicit premises, logical structure, or decision branches rather than rhetorical fluency.
The work is vulnerable to hidden leaps, category errors, or intuitive story glue that cleaner structure would expose.
A team needs to test whether the conclusion actually follows from what it says it believes.
The next step should convert a persuasive narrative into a traceable chain of reasoning.

failure_modes.mode: Danger When Clean Structure Launders Weak Premises
failure_modes.description: Formal structure can become cosmetic when valid-looking logic is built on weak or untested premises.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit premises before trusting the logic: valid-looking structure built on weak or untested assumptions produces confident wrong answers.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Clean Structure Launders Weak Premises

failure_modes.mode: formal rigor does not replace empirical contact with reality
failure_modes.description: The model fails when it confuses internal coherence with truth about the world.
failure_modes.mitigation: Distinguish internal coherence from external truth: a formally valid argument can still be disconnected from how the world actually works.
failure_modes.source_quote: formal rigor does not replace empirical contact with reality

failure_modes.mode: make premises explicit
failure_modes.description: Formal reasoning breaks when hidden leaps stay implicit and no one checks whether the conclusion actually follows.
failure_modes.mitigation: Make every inference step explicit and reviewable; hidden leaps that go unchallenged are where formal reasoning quietly fails.
failure_modes.source_quote: make premises explicit

premortem_questions.description: What premises are doing the real work here?
premortem_questions.source_quote: make premises explicit

premortem_questions.description: If the conclusion is wrong, which inference step breaks first?
premortem_questions.source_quote: follow the logic step by step

premortem_questions.description: What evidence would show that our elegant structure is detached from reality?
premortem_questions.source_quote: does not replace empirical contact with reality

heuristics.description: Make premises explicit before debating conclusions.
heuristics.source_quote: make premises explicit

heuristics.description: Use decision trees or logic branches to expose hidden forks in the argument.
heuristics.source_quote: Decision Trees

heuristics.description: Test whether the structure actually tracks reality instead of admiring the neatness of the argument.
heuristics.source_quote: formal rigor does not replace empirical contact with reality

danger_when: Formal structure is being used to launder weak premises, missing evidence, or fake certainty.
The situation mainly needs empirical observation, field data, or live feedback and the team is substituting symbolic neatness for contact with reality.
formal-reasoning.name: Formal Reasoning

select_when: A recommendation cannot be judged in isolation because its value changes materially with how a rival, partner, regulator, or counterparty responds.
The team needs to make the players, likely moves, information asymmetries, and payoff drivers explicit before choosing a move.
Commitment, threat, promise, sequencing, or signaling may change the game more than optimizing inside a fixed option set.
The next move should test whether the current strategy is truly robust against the opponent's strongest credible response.

failure_modes.mode: Danger when the actors are not actually playing the same game
failure_modes.description: Teams can model the wrong game when one side is optimizing politics, ideology, or internal incentives that the payoff map does not capture.
failure_modes.mitigation: Verify the payoff map captures the real incentives in play; politics, ideology, or internal pressures that do not appear in the model can dominate behavior.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the actors are not actually playing the same game

failure_modes.mode: The cost of strategizing
failure_modes.description: Recursive strategizing can become so computationally expensive that the analysis costs more than the decision is worth.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a complexity ceiling: if recursive strategizing costs more than the decision is worth, simplify the model to the dominant strategic interaction.
failure_modes.source_quote: The cost of strategizing

failure_modes.mode: Incredible Commitments/Threats
failure_modes.description: Incredible threats or promises can be treated as real leverage even when no device makes them binding.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test whether threats or promises are credible by checking what device makes them binding; incredible commitments are not real leverage.
failure_modes.source_quote: Incredible Commitments/Threats

premortem_questions.description: What evidence would show that we chose the wrong players, incentives, or payoff assumptions for this game?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What evidence would show that we chose the wrong players, incentives, or payoff assumptions for this game?

premortem_questions.description: If the opponent's strongest adverse response lands, is the downside still survivable?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If the opponent's strongest adverse response lands, is the downside still survivable?

premortem_questions.description: What specific device makes our stated threat or promise actually credible to the other side?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What specific devices ... make our stated threat or promise absolutely credible

heuristics.description: Name the players, moves, information asymmetries, and payoff drivers explicitly before optimizing the move.
heuristics.source_quote: name the players, likely moves, information asymmetries

heuristics.description: Use the smallest game model that can still move the decision instead of mapping every theoretical branch.
heuristics.source_quote: smallest decision-relevant game model

heuristics.description: Test multiple frames and contradictory evidence before locking the game structure.
heuristics.source_quote: Test multiple frames and actively seek contradictory evidence

danger_when: The actors are not actually playing the same game and the visible payoff model is missing the ideology, politics, or internal incentive that is really driving behavior.
Recursive strategizing is becoming more expensive than the decision warrants, so the team needs a smaller decision-relevant model rather than another layer of countermove speculation.
game-theory-payoffs.name: Game Theory Payoffs

select_when: Retention, alignment, or real understanding depends on people producing the explanation, hypothesis, or plan themselves instead of only hearing it.
A complex or abstract concept will only stick if the learner has to retrieve, articulate, or construct the idea actively.
The team is moving too quickly from borrowed wording to confident action without showing that it can generate the reasoning in its own words.
A decision or teaching path should slow System 1 fluency by forcing structured articulation, explanation, or concept mapping.

failure_modes.mode: Passive exposure can create brittle understanding because people can repeat the wording without gene...
failure_modes.description: Passive exposure can create brittle understanding because people can repeat the wording without generating the reasoning for themselves.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require learners to generate explanations, not just recognize or repeat them; passive exposure creates brittle understanding.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when retention, understanding, or alignment depends on people producing the explanation, hypothesis, or plan themselves instead of merely hearing it.

failure_modes.mode: The Generation Effect is most powerful when dealing with complexity and abstraction.
failure_modes.description: Complex ideas can remain shallow when teams never have to convert them into their own explanation, concept map, or plan.
failure_modes.mitigation: Have teams convert complex ideas into their own concept maps, summaries, or plans before accepting that understanding has occurred.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Generation Effect is most powerful when dealing with complexity and abstraction.

failure_modes.mode: Self-generation can turn into bluffing if it replaces expert correction, evidence checks, or externa...
failure_modes.description: Self-generation can turn into bluffing if it replaces expert correction, evidence checks, or external calibration.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pair self-generation with expert correction and evidence checks; generating answers without calibration can entrench errors.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when "generate your own answer" becomes an excuse to skip expert correction, evidence checks, or external calibration.

premortem_questions.description: Can the team generate the explanation or plan in its own words, or is it only borrowing polished language it has heard before?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when retention, understanding, or alignment depends on people producing the explanation, hypothesis, or plan themselves instead of merely hearing it.

premortem_questions.description: What evidence check or outside correction are we skipping because self-generated confidence feels satisfying enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger when "generate your own answer" becomes an excuse to skip expert correction, evidence checks, or external calibration.

premortem_questions.description: If this explanation had to survive an analogy test, where would its weak spots or hidden simplifications show up first?
premortem_questions.source_quote: The Analogy Test (Feynman Step 4)

heuristics.description: Make people generate the explanation, hypothesis, or plan themselves instead of only consuming the finished version.
heuristics.source_quote: Most useful when retention, understanding, or alignment depends on people producing the explanation, hypothesis, or plan themselves instead of merely hearing it.

heuristics.description: Use concept maps, chunking, or structured articulation to turn abstract material into generated understanding.
heuristics.source_quote: Generating a concept map helps organize material and make clear the connections between different ideas.

heuristics.description: Separate discovery from delivery so the hard generation work sharpens understanding without overloading the final communication.
heuristics.source_quote: Separating Discovery from Delivery

danger_when: Self-generation is being used as an excuse to skip correction, evidence checks, or outside calibration.
The task is so under-scaffolded that asking for generation first would create noise, confusion, or bluffing instead of real understanding.
generation-effect.name: Generation Effect

select_when: Perception, explanation, or design depends on helping people see the right clusters, contrasts, and whole-before-part patterns quickly.
The problem is still chaotic enough that a coherent organizing shape matters more than another isolated fragment.
A team needs to move from scattered observations toward a pattern that can guide interpretation or execution.

failure_modes.mode: Observers can mistake surface grouping for explanation and stop before testing whether the apparent ...
failure_modes.description: Observers can mistake surface grouping for explanation and stop before testing whether the apparent pattern is actually causal.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test whether the perceived pattern is causal: surface grouping can look like explanation without actually explaining the mechanism.
failure_modes.source_quote: gestalt-principles-of-perception conflicts with representativeness-heuristic (representativeness heuristic) when surface pattern grouping is treated as causal proof.

failure_modes.mode: People can privilege the perceptual arrangement that fits prior beliefs and miss equally real counte...
failure_modes.description: People can privilege the perceptual arrangement that fits prior beliefs and miss equally real counter-patterns.
failure_modes.mitigation: Actively search for counter-patterns that the preferred perceptual arrangement might be hiding; confirmation bias exploits gestalt grouping.
failure_modes.source_quote: gestalt-principles-of-perception conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when observers privilege perceptual arrangements that support prior beliefs.

failure_modes.mode: A messy execution space can remain unintelligible if nobody imposes a usable whole-before-part frame...
failure_modes.description: A messy execution space can remain unintelligible if nobody imposes a usable whole-before-part frame.
failure_modes.mitigation: Impose a usable whole-before-part frame on messy execution spaces; without a deliberate organizing structure, the signal stays unintelligible.
failure_modes.source_quote: The model has high leverage when people first need a coherent organizing shape in order to make sense of complexity rather than another isolated fragment.

premortem_questions.description: Are we seeing a real structural pattern, or only a visually satisfying grouping?
premortem_questions.source_quote: surface pattern grouping is treated as causal proof

premortem_questions.description: What counter-example would break the whole we think we see?
premortem_questions.source_quote: observers privilege perceptual arrangements that support prior beliefs

premortem_questions.description: What is the minimum organizing shape that would make this complexity legible without hiding important parts?
premortem_questions.source_quote: helping the mind see the right clusters, contrasts, and whole-before-part patterns quickly

heuristics.description: Start by naming the whole pattern you want the audience to perceive before layering in details.
heuristics.source_quote: Gestalt principles are strongest when perception, explanation, or design depends on helping the mind see the right clusters, contrasts, and whole-before-part patterns quickly.

heuristics.description: Use grouping, contrast, and sequence to make the intended structure visible instead of leaving the audience to infer it from fragments.
heuristics.source_quote: creating a clear, simple framework (a powerful Gestalt) is crucial

heuristics.description: Treat the first visible pattern as a hypothesis to test, not as proof.
heuristics.source_quote: surface pattern grouping is treated as causal proof

danger_when: Surface pattern grouping is being mistaken for causal proof without further testing.
The audience already has the right organizing pattern and another framing pass would only add presentation theater.
gestalt-principles-of-perception.name: Gestalt Principles Of Perception

select_when: Effort is scattered and the team needs one explicit outcome to align trade-offs, sequencing, and measurement.
The goal can be made concrete enough to govern daily decisions while still connecting to a larger purpose that sustains motivation.
Success depends on turning aspiration into owned checkpoints, thresholds, and review cadence rather than verbal commitment alone.
Execution quality is being limited by the lack of a clear target rather than by the lack of effort.

failure_modes.mode: Useless Accuracy/Lack of Concreteness (The Curse of Knowledge)
failure_modes.description: Abstract mission language can sound accurate while remaining useless for frontline decisions and execution.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test goals against frontline decisions: if the mission language cannot guide a specific day-to-day choice, it is abstract decoration.
failure_modes.source_quote: Useless Accuracy/Lack of Concreteness (The Curse of Knowledge)

failure_modes.mode: Premature Solutioning (Blurring the Steps)
failure_modes.description: Blurring goal setting with solution design can hide the real diagnosis step and lock the team into premature plans.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate goal-setting from solution design so the diagnosis step is not hidden or skipped by premature planning.
failure_modes.source_quote: Premature Solutioning (Blurring the Steps)

failure_modes.mode: Sticky goals can survive past their evidence, creating goal inertia instead of strategic adaptation.
failure_modes.description: Sticky goals can survive past their evidence, creating goal inertia instead of strategic adaptation.
failure_modes.mitigation: Schedule goal reviews against current evidence; sticky goals that survive past their data create inertia instead of strategic adaptation.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when goals are sticky enough to organize action but not revisited often enough to reflect changed evidence, constraints, or strategic reality.

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "Is the worst case bad enough?"

premortem_questions.description: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What would you have to believe?"

premortem_questions.description: If your audience remembers only one thing, what should it be?
premortem_questions.source_quote: if your audience remembers only one thing, what should it be?

heuristics.description: Set goals first, then diagnose problems, design plans, and execute in sequence instead of collapsing the steps together.
heuristics.source_quote: It is crucial to perform these steps one at a time and in order; for instance, when setting goals, one should only set goals and avoid thinking about how they will be achieved or what might go wrong.

heuristics.description: Use SMART goals so the objective is specific, measurable, attainable, relevant, and time-bound.
heuristics.source_quote: SMART Goals (Clarity and Measurability)

heuristics.description: Tie low-level goals to one top-level professional goal so the system does not optimize disconnected aims.
heuristics.source_quote: The Goal Hierarchy (Strategic Alignment)

danger_when: The chosen goal is organizing action but is not being revisited often enough to reflect changed evidence, constraints, or strategic reality.
The goal optimizes what is legible or motivating while neglecting the side effects it creates elsewhere in the system.
goal-setting.name: Goal Setting

select_when: Capability is genuinely expandable through feedback, repetition, and deliberate improvement rather than blocked by an immutable constraint.
Setbacks should trigger learning loops instead of identity threat, ego defense, or retreat from challenge.
A team or learner needs a healthier response to error, criticism, and developmental discomfort so improvement can compound over time.
Leaders want to reward improvement quality, receptivity to feedback, and process discipline rather than ego protection.

failure_modes.mode: Works when setbacks should trigger learning loops instead of identity threat.
failure_modes.description: Setbacks can become identity threats when people are trying to prove ability instead of improve ability.
failure_modes.mitigation: Reframe setbacks as diagnostic data about the learning process, not as identity verdicts about ability.
failure_modes.source_quote: Works when setbacks should trigger learning loops instead of identity threat.

failure_modes.mode: Ignoring Strategy for Mere Effort
failure_modes.description: Effort can get mistaken for progress when people work hard without diagnosing what actually produces results.
failure_modes.mitigation: Distinguish hard work from effective practice: effort that does not diagnose what actually produces results is not growth.
failure_modes.source_quote: Ignoring Strategy for Mere Effort

failure_modes.mode: Danger When Growth Language Denies Real Constraints.
failure_modes.description: Growth language can become self-deception when it hand-waves away structural limits, weak strategy, or missing resources.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit growth language for self-deception: real growth addresses structural limits, weak strategy, and missing resources, not just attitude.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Growth Language Denies Real Constraints.

premortem_questions.description: What weakness or mistake are we still hiding because it feels like an indictment of identity rather than a solvable problem?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Radical Receptivity to Feedback

premortem_questions.description: Where are we substituting hard work rhetoric for fact-based diagnosis of what would actually improve the result?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Success does not come from hard work alone, but from working hard on what gets results.

premortem_questions.description: What real constraint, role mismatch, or missing resource are we pretending mindset alone will solve?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger When Growth Language Denies Real Constraints.

heuristics.description: Focus feedback and attention on the process, reps, and diagnosis that improve performance rather than on ego-protecting labels.
heuristics.source_quote: Prioritizing Process Over Outcome

heuristics.description: Welcome criticism as information about a fixable weakness instead of treating it as a threat to identity.
heuristics.source_quote: Radical Receptivity to Feedback

heuristics.description: Use repeated goal-problem-diagnosis-design-action loops so learning compounds into visible adaptation over time.
heuristics.source_quote: The 5-Step Iterative Process

danger_when: Growth language is being used to deny real structural limits, weak strategy, role mismatch, or missing resources.
High effort is being treated as sufficient even though the work is still poorly diagnosed, poorly prioritized, or detached from what produces results.
growth-mindset.name: Growth Mindset

select_when: Execution quality depends on repeating the right small behaviors rather than on one heroic effort.
The team already knows the right move, but friction, forgetfulness, or emotional variability keeps breaking follow-through.
Reliable baseline behavior is needed under stress, overload, or limited attention.
The real question is how to make good action automatic instead of re-deciding it every time.

failure_modes.mode: Automation Replaces Judgment
failure_modes.description: Automatic behavior can keep solving yesterday's problem when the environment has changed.
failure_modes.mitigation: Build in environment-change triggers that interrupt automatic behavior when the conditions the habit was built for have shifted.
failure_modes.source_quote: Automation Replaces Judgment

failure_modes.mode: entrenched behavioral patterns selectively filter evidence
failure_modes.description: Entrenched routines can filter evidence and protect the habit instead of the intended outcome.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test habits against current evidence: entrenched routines can filter information to protect the habit rather than the intended outcome.
failure_modes.source_quote: entrenched behavioral patterns selectively filter evidence

failure_modes.mode: strategy remains academic and irrelevant
failure_modes.description: Strategy stays inert when the required behaviors are never converted into repeatable routines.
failure_modes.mitigation: Convert strategic intent into repeatable routines: strategy stays inert until the required behaviors become habitual.
failure_modes.source_quote: strategy remains academic and irrelevant

premortem_questions.description: What cue, feeling, or belief is firing right before the unwanted behavior starts?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What are you feeling in the moment right before you begin the behavior?

premortem_questions.description: Does this routine still serve the goal, or is it only protecting old comfort?
premortem_questions.source_quote: yesterday's problem

premortem_questions.description: Which small repeated behavior would most improve reliability under stress?
premortem_questions.source_quote: repeating the right small behaviors

heuristics.description: Use cue, craving, response, and reward to redesign the loop rather than relying on vague intention.
heuristics.source_quote: Cue, Craving, Response, and Reward

heuristics.description: Scaffold the response into small repeatable units so the behavior becomes easier to perform.
heuristics.source_quote: small, incremental units

heuristics.description: Design for consistency so useful behavior survives fatigue and overload.
heuristics.source_quote: Design for Consistency

danger_when: The environment has changed enough that yesterday's automatic behavior is now solving the wrong problem.
Automation is being used to avoid judgment on a novel case that still requires deliberate thought.
habit-formation.name: Habit Formation

select_when: A failure, delay, or conflict could plausibly be explained by poor clarity, coordination breakdown, overload, or neglect rather than hostile intent.
The emotional temptation is to assign malice before checking for simpler operational explanations.
The goal is to keep diagnosis constructive long enough to improve the system instead of escalating blame prematurely.
A team needs to distinguish bad outcomes caused by design failure from those caused by genuine sabotage.

failure_modes.mode: Teams escalate conflict when they assign hostile intent before checking for simpler explanations lik...
failure_modes.description: Teams escalate conflict when they assign hostile intent before checking for simpler explanations like poor clarity, overload, or process breakdown.
failure_modes.mitigation: Check for simpler explanations — poor clarity, overload, misaligned incentives — before assigning hostile intent.
failure_modes.source_quote: Works best when the emotional temptation is to assign hostile intent before checking for simpler operational explanations.

failure_modes.mode: Organizations miss fixable design problems when they blame people instead of diagnosing how the syst...
failure_modes.description: Organizations miss fixable design problems when they blame people instead of diagnosing how the system allowed the error.
failure_modes.mitigation: Diagnose how the system allowed the error before blaming the individual; fixable design problems hide behind personal blame.
failure_modes.source_quote: Hanlon's Razor is applied as a de-escalation and diagnostic tool that redirects analysis away from individual blame toward systemic shortcomings.

failure_modes.mode: Danger when the principle becomes a reflexive excuse for repeat harm.
failure_modes.description: Hanlon's Razor becomes negligent when it is used to excuse repeat harm, obvious deception, or incentive structures that reward bad faith.
failure_modes.mitigation: Distinguish honest mistakes from repeat harm: Hanlon's Razor is a diagnostic tool, not an excuse for patterns of negligence or deception.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the principle becomes a reflexive excuse for repeat harm.

premortem_questions.description: What simpler operational explanation could plausibly account for this failure before we infer hostile intent?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is there another reasonable explanation for the events that have occurred?

premortem_questions.description: If this were really neglect or poor design, what systemic change would prevent the same event from recurring?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Shift the focus from who made the mistake to how the system allowed the mistake.

premortem_questions.description: What evidence would force us to stop giving the benefit of the doubt and treat this as bad-faith behavior instead?
premortem_questions.source_quote: It does not rule out intent entirely.

heuristics.description: Default first to neglect, overload, or system failure before jumping to malice.
heuristics.source_quote: Do not attribute to malice that which is more easily explained by stupidity.

heuristics.description: Use the principle to keep diagnosis constructive, not to erase accountability.
heuristics.source_quote: The ultimate benefit is often the improvement of relationships by granting the benefit of the doubt.

heuristics.description: Abandon Hanlon's Razor once repeat patterns, incentive design, or evidence make bad-faith behavior the stronger explanation.
heuristics.source_quote: Danger when the principle becomes a reflexive excuse for repeat harm, obvious deception, or incentive structures that practically reward bad-faith behavior.

danger_when: The principle is becoming a reflexive excuse for repeat harm, obvious deception, or incentive structures that reward bad-faith behavior.
People are using Hanlon's Razor to avoid accountability instead of demanding evidence and systemic correction.
hanlons-razor.name: Hanlons Razor

select_when: A post-mortem, retrospective, or performance review is evaluating decisions that were made under genuine uncertainty.
Past decision-makers are being judged for outcomes that were not, in fact, highly predictable given what was known at the time.
Lessons being extracted from a failure are calibrated to the specific failure path as if it were obviously foreseeable, rather than to improving probabilistic reasoning generally.
A rare event is being retrospectively attributed with warning signs that appeared ambiguous or unimportant before the event.
Decision quality is being conflated with outcome quality — good process is being penalized for bad luck or vice versa.

failure_modes.mode: Creeping determinism
failure_modes.description: Creeping determinism makes the causal path to a known outcome appear clear and necessary in hindsight, collapsing the genuine alternative paths that were equally plausible before the event.
failure_modes.mitigation: Reconstruct the genuine uncertainty at the time of the decision: creeping determinism makes the outcome look inevitable only after the fact.
failure_modes.source_quote: Creeping determinism

failure_modes.mode: Decision-quality contamination
failure_modes.description: Decision-quality contamination conflates outcome quality with decision quality — good processes with bad outcomes are penalized while bad processes with lucky outcomes are rewarded, corrupting the feedback signal for improving judgment.
failure_modes.mitigation: Evaluate decision quality separately from outcome quality: good processes with bad outcomes should not be penalized, and bad processes with good outcomes should not be rewarded.
failure_modes.source_quote: Decision-quality contamination

failure_modes.mode: False lesson extraction
failure_modes.description: False lesson extraction generates post-mortem lessons calibrated to the specific failure path as if it were uniquely predictable, producing 'fight the last war' defenses rather than improved probabilistic reasoning.
failure_modes.mitigation: Anchor post-mortem lessons to the decision process rather than to the specific failure path; false lesson extraction generates rules calibrated to one outcome.
failure_modes.source_quote: False lesson extraction

premortem_questions.description: What does our contemporaneous documentation show we thought and what we were uncertain about at decision time — and how does that differ from what we now remember thinking?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Require contemporaneous documentation of decision rationale, uncertainty, and known alternatives

premortem_questions.description: What was the realistic base-rate probability of this outcome at decision time, based on comparable cases — and does that change our evaluation of the decision?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What was the realistic probability of this outcome at the time the decision was made?

premortem_questions.description: What were the three most plausible outcomes at decision time — and why do we now find it hard to remember them as equally plausible?
premortem_questions.source_quote: When learning from past failures, explicitly reconstruct the alternative paths that were live at the time

heuristics.description: Separate process evaluation from outcome evaluation: ask 'was this a good decision given what was known at the time?' before asking 'what happened?' — the sequence matters.
heuristics.source_quote: Separate process evaluation from outcome evaluation

heuristics.description: Require contemporaneous documentation of decision reasoning and uncertainty at decision time — use those documents in post-event review rather than relying on reconstructed memory.
heuristics.source_quote: Document Pre-Decision Reasoning: the only reliable antidote is timestamped records

heuristics.description: Reconstruct the alternative futures that were live at decision time — force the question of what could reasonably have happened instead, to restore the probability branching that hindsight collapsed.
heuristics.source_quote: Explicitly reconstruct the alternative paths that were live at the time of the decision

danger_when: The decision was made with clear, contemporaneous evidence of high failure probability that was ignored — in those cases outcome-based criticism may be warranted.
The review is explicitly anchored to contemporaneous documentation of what was known and uncertain at decision time, not to post-hoc reconstruction.
hindsight-bias.name: Hindsight Bias
hindsight-bias.input_type: post-event evaluation of a decision made under prior uncertainty
hindsight-bias.output_type: decision quality separation from outcome quality

select_when: Behavior looks irrational on the surface until the reward structure, status signals, penalties, or career consequences are mapped explicitly.
Desired behavior must be reinforced through clear feedback loops instead of vague exhortation, goodwill, or assumed alignment.
A system, compensation plan, workflow, or governance process is being redesigned because the current outputs are being shaped by the wrong local rewards.

failure_modes.mode: Ignoring Extreme Outcomes (Base Case Bias)
failure_modes.description: Ignoring extreme downside can make a reward scheme look acceptable until one ugly tail outcome lands.
failure_modes.mitigation: Model tail outcomes explicitly before committing to a reward scheme; extreme downside can make an acceptable-looking structure dangerous.
failure_modes.source_quote: Ignoring Extreme Outcomes (Base Case Bias)

failure_modes.mode: The Flaw of Mandating Solutions
failure_modes.description: Mandating solutions can fail because inducements and standards do not create real commitment by themselves.
failure_modes.mitigation: Check whether the incentive creates genuine commitment or merely compliance; mandated solutions without buy-in fail at implementation.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Flaw of Mandating Solutions

failure_modes.mode: The Lazy Controller
failure_modes.description: A lazy System 2 controller can let intuitive and emotional incentives steer the choice without real review.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require explicit review before accepting an intuitively appealing incentive; System 2 must audit what System 1 finds rewarding.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Lazy Controller

premortem_questions.description: What factors are involved in the worst-case scenario, and how can we prevent them?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What factors are involved in the worst-case scenario, and how can we prevent them?

premortem_questions.description: Does this detail or statistic genuinely help the audience make a decision, or is it merely accurate but useless?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Does this detail/statistic genuinely help the employee/audience make a decision, or is it merely accurate but useless?

premortem_questions.description: Have we structured this engagement to confront all parties with their freedom and responsibility?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Have we structured this engagement to confront all parties with their freedom and responsibility?

heuristics.description: Use autonomy, mastery, and purpose when the task is heuristic or creative rather than purely algorithmic.
heuristics.source_quote: Motivation 3.0: Autonomy, Mastery, and Purpose (AMP)

heuristics.description: Match reward structure to task type instead of using one incentive design for everything.
heuristics.source_quote: "If-Then" vs. "Now That" Rewards

heuristics.description: Map the real reward structure, including status signals and penalties, before judging behavior as irrational.
heuristics.source_quote: behavior looks irrational on the surface until the reward structure, status signals, penalties, or career consequences are mapped explicitly

danger_when: The measured incentive is becoming the target and people can game the metric while degrading the real objective.
Local incentives look efficient in isolation but create second-order avoidance, escalation, or trust erosion elsewhere in the system.
incentives.name: Incentives

select_when: Pricing, trust, or strategic choice depends on hidden information, concealed incentives, or unshared operating reality.
A vendor, agent, or delegated operator controls the dashboard, references, or pilot evidence being used as proof.
The people who know the most, the people who bear the risk, and the people who approve the decision are different actors.
The next move should change disclosure, diligence, observability, signaling, or incentive structure before commitment.

failure_modes.mode: Partner-controlled demand signals can make manufactured confidence look like real market pull.
failure_modes.description: Partner-controlled demand signals can make manufactured confidence look like real market pull.
failure_modes.mitigation: Verify demand signals independently; partner-controlled data can make manufactured confidence look like real market pull.
failure_modes.source_quote: The principal then scales the channel on signals the partner controls instead of signals the market independently confirms.

failure_modes.mode: The curse of knowledge lets experts communicate at the wrong level of abstraction for the real audie...
failure_modes.description: The curse of knowledge lets experts communicate at the wrong level of abstraction for the real audience.
failure_modes.mitigation: Translate to the audience's level of abstraction; the curse of knowledge makes experts communicate above the level that decision-makers can use.
failure_modes.source_quote: Experts unintentionally communicate abstractly and opaquely because they cannot recall what it is like to lack their specialized knowledge.

failure_modes.mode: Overconfidence and blind spots can make the informed party miss unknown unknowns and resist deeper i...
failure_modes.description: Overconfidence and blind spots can make the informed party miss unknown unknowns and resist deeper investigation.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit for unknown unknowns: the informed party's overconfidence can prevent deeper investigation of what they do not know they do not know.
failure_modes.source_quote: Expertise leads to an illusion of understanding and overconfidence, causing the informed party to ignore unknown unknowns.

failure_modes.mode: WYSIATI makes available information look sufficient even when the decisive data is missing.
failure_modes.description: WYSIATI makes available information look sufficient even when the decisive data is missing.
failure_modes.mitigation: Search for the missing data explicitly; WYSIATI makes available information look sufficient even when the decisive evidence is absent.
failure_modes.source_quote: The belief that the available information is sufficient to make a judgment, neglecting the critical, missing data that defines IA.

premortem_questions.description: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.description: What do you think the other side doesn't get about your perspective?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What do you think the other side doesn’t get about your perspective?

premortem_questions.description: What specific metric, reference, or proof in this plan is controlled by the party trying to win the decision?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What specific metric, reference, or proof in this plan is controlled by the party trying to win the decision?

premortem_questions.description: If the vendor or agent were optimizing for a different payoff than we assume, where would we see it first?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If the vendor or agent were optimizing for a different payoff than we assume, where would we see it first?

heuristics.description: Use social cartography to map who actually holds power, bias, and hidden decision rights.
heuristics.source_quote: Social cartography (drawing a mental map of key players) ensures the strategist has a complete picture of stakeholder biases and preferences.

heuristics.description: Bridge the gap with Socratic inquiry by asking how the expert thinks, not only what they recommend.
heuristics.source_quote: rather than asking what to do ... ask how the expert thinks about the problem

heuristics.description: Ask for stories, not advice, so tacit decision steps become inspectable.
heuristics.source_quote: Asking for Stories, Not Advice: Focusing on facts, timelines, and walking through decisions step-by-step

heuristics.description: Use the PARI method to surface precursor, action, result, and interpretation inside expert judgment.
heuristics.source_quote: The PARI method ... Precursor, Action, Result, Interpretation

danger_when: The critical evidence is externally audited, directly observable in production, or contractually hard for one side to manipulate.
The real blocker is incentive misalignment, weak governance, or lack of authority to act on known facts rather than a missing-information problem.
The team is asking for more information without naming what hidden variable would actually change price, trust, sequencing, or go-no-go choice.
information-asymmetry.name: Information Asymmetry

select_when: Complexity is real but attention is scarce, so the challenge is to preserve the core signal without overloading the audience.
The same decision keeps failing because of message shape, sequencing, or framing rather than because the underlying content is false.
The communicator must decide what not to say so the core meaning survives limited working memory.
The work needs stronger pruning, ordering, and packaging because the current explanation is accurate but not operationally usable.

failure_modes.mode: Signal can fail when experts compress so aggressively that they remove decision-critical nuance alon...
failure_modes.description: Signal can fail when experts compress so aggressively that they remove decision-critical nuance along with the noise.
failure_modes.mitigation: Verify that compression has not removed decision-critical nuance; signal fails when experts strip out too much along with the noise.
failure_modes.source_quote: The primary limitation of this model is that while it optimizes for clarity and efficiency, it is susceptible to human cognitive biases, particularly among experts.

failure_modes.mode: Works when the same decision is failing because of message shape, not message truth.
failure_modes.description: The message can remain correct but unusable because sequencing, chunking, or framing prevents comprehension and adoption.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit sequencing, chunking, and framing: a correct but unusable message still fails if the audience cannot comprehend and adopt it.
failure_modes.source_quote: Works when the same decision is failing because of message shape, not message truth.

failure_modes.mode: Allowing the tool (framework) to define the problem rather than the problem defining the tool.
failure_modes.description: Framework choice can distort the signal when the communicator lets a favorite tool define the problem instead of the problem defining the tool.
failure_modes.mitigation: Let the problem define the framework rather than letting a favorite tool define the problem; framework choice can distort the signal.
failure_modes.source_quote: Allowing the tool (framework) to define the problem rather than the problem defining the tool.

premortem_questions.description: What is the core signal that must survive if everything nonessential is removed?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best when the communicator must choose what not to say.

premortem_questions.description: Which part of this message is failing because of shape or sequencing rather than because the content is false?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Works when the same decision is failing because of message shape, not message truth.

premortem_questions.description: What detail feels optional but would make the recommendation dangerously misleading if removed?
premortem_questions.source_quote: The main challenge is pruning, ordering, and packaging information so the core signal survives contact with limited working memory.

heuristics.description: Prune the message until the core signal becomes visible without removing the detail that makes the action safe.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when the communicator must choose what not to say.

heuristics.description: Sequence and chunk information for limited working memory instead of assuming accurate content will carry itself.
heuristics.source_quote: CLT establishes the limits of working memory.

heuristics.description: Use mutually exclusive structure so overlapping arguments do not bury the signal under confusion.
heuristics.source_quote: MECE ensures that all aspects of a problem are covered without unnecessary overlap, which maximizes clarity and minimizes confusion.

danger_when: The model is being used to optimize compression so aggressively that decision-critical nuance disappears.
The issue is not signal packaging but disagreement about reality, incentives, or causal structure, and cleaner formatting is being treated as a substitute for solving that deeper problem.
information-theory.name: Information Theory

select_when: The desired result is clear, but the team still cannot translate it into repeatable behaviors, habits, and checkpoints.
Activity metrics are abundant while the link between action and real outcome remains weak or obscured.
Process discipline is needed to reduce anxiety, improve execution, or reveal whether the current work pattern is actually moving the result.

failure_modes.mode: Teams can keep hitting activity targets long after it is obvious that the outcome is not moving.
failure_modes.description: Teams can keep hitting activity targets long after it is obvious that the outcome is not moving.
failure_modes.mitigation: Check whether activity targets are still connected to the outcome; hitting input goals after the output stops moving is waste.
failure_modes.source_quote: input-vs-output-goals conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when teams keep activity targets despite weak outcome movement.

failure_modes.mode: Reward design can over-index on visible effort and hide whether value is actually being delivered.
failure_modes.description: Reward design can over-index on visible effort and hide whether value is actually being delivered.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit reward design for effort-visibility bias: visible activity is easy to measure but may hide whether value is actually being delivered.
failure_modes.source_quote: input-vs-output-goals conflicts with incentives (incentives) when reward design over-indexes on visible effort instead of value delivery.

failure_modes.mode: People can fixate on outcomes they cannot directly control and never design the behaviors that would...
failure_modes.description: People can fixate on outcomes they cannot directly control and never design the behaviors that would make progress repeatable.
failure_modes.mitigation: Design the input behaviors that would cause the outcome rather than fixating on outcomes the team cannot directly control.
failure_modes.source_quote: The model is strongest when people can state the desired result yet still fail to translate that result into repeatable behaviors, habits, and checkpoints.

premortem_questions.description: Which of our current input metrics would still look good even if the real outcome kept deteriorating?
premortem_questions.source_quote: activity metrics are abundant but the link between action and outcome is weak or obscured

premortem_questions.description: What repeatable behavior do we control directly that should move this outcome if our theory is right?
premortem_questions.source_quote: translate that result into repeatable behaviors, habits, and checkpoints

premortem_questions.description: Are we rewarding effort theater or real value delivery?
premortem_questions.source_quote: reward design over-indexes on visible effort instead of value delivery

heuristics.description: Translate the desired result into behaviors and checkpoints the team can actually control.
heuristics.source_quote: repeatable behaviors, habits, and checkpoints

heuristics.description: Track input metrics only when there is an explicit causal claim connecting them to the outcome.
heuristics.source_quote: clear metrics tied to the Output Goal is crucial for knowing if the process (Input) is working

heuristics.description: Use process goals to reduce anxiety around distant outcomes while still testing whether the process produces value.
heuristics.source_quote: Focusing on the process helps reduce anxiety and stress associated with overthinking the outcome.

danger_when: Visible effort is being rewarded regardless of whether the activity produces value.
The team is already tightly connecting process measures to outcome movement and another goal split would add bureaucracy.
input-vs-output-goals.name: Input Vs Output Goals

select_when: The main risk is overconfidence in the current frame, and better judgment depends on admitting what may not be known yet.
Disconfirming evidence, unfamiliar cases, or novel conditions are appearing, and the team needs an updating posture before choosing a plan.
A decision would improve if the thinker could separate ego protection from the actual search for what is true.
The next move should expose blind spots and reopen assumptions rather than defend the current interpretation.

failure_modes.mode: I have seen this one before, it is an X problem
failure_modes.description: The model fails when experts assume they have seen this problem before and force a familiar pattern onto a novel case.
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge the assumption of familiarity: force the expert to articulate what is genuinely novel about this case before applying the old pattern.
failure_modes.source_quote: I have seen this one before, it is an X problem

failure_modes.mode: Ignoring Disconfirming Evidence
failure_modes.description: Without intellectual humility, disconfirming evidence gets filtered out in favor of defending the current belief.
failure_modes.mitigation: Protect the flow of disconfirming evidence: without humility, inconvenient data gets filtered out in favor of the current belief.
failure_modes.source_quote: Ignoring Disconfirming Evidence

failure_modes.mode: Danger When Humility Becomes Performative Self-Minimization
failure_modes.description: Humility becomes fake when people signal openness but still avoid real updating, clear claims, or accountable judgment.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require humility to produce observable changes — updated estimates, revised plans, explicit caveats — not just open-sounding language.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Humility Becomes Performative Self-Minimization

premortem_questions.description: What might I be missing because I am treating this as a familiar pattern too early?
premortem_questions.source_quote: I have seen this one before, it is an X problem

premortem_questions.description: What disconfirming evidence would I be tempted to explain away right now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Ignoring Disconfirming Evidence

premortem_questions.description: What update would prove this is real humility rather than openness theater?
premortem_questions.source_quote: signal openness or modesty outwardly but still avoid real updating

heuristics.description: Treat unfamiliarity and blind spots as signals to reopen assumptions instead of defending the familiar frame.
heuristics.source_quote: acknowledging what you don't know

heuristics.description: Use disconfirming evidence as a required part of the reasoning process rather than as a threat to identity.
heuristics.source_quote: identify and reconcile disconfirming evidence

heuristics.description: Ask whether conventional methods are actually best before rebuilding from the facts upward.
heuristics.source_quote: Are they the best?

danger_when: Humility is being performed as self-minimization or soft language while real updating, accountable claims, and clear judgment are still being avoided.
The team already sees the uncertainties clearly and now mainly needs execution, not another cycle of identity-safe openness signaling.
intellectual-humility.name: Intellectual Humility
reframing_when: The question is asked with high certainty about the problem definition itself — the confidence is in the frame, not just the answer, and that frame-level confidence has not been examined.
The question treats the asker's expertise or track record as sufficient warrant for the current framing, when the situation may be novel enough that past success is misleading.
The question dismisses alternative framings too quickly because the asker's identity is invested in the current frame — being wrong about the question feels threatening.
The question assumes completeness of the asker's knowledge ('I know what the issue is') when acknowledging the limits of that knowledge would open a structurally different problem definition.
intellectual-humility.reframing_move: Before proceeding with the current framing, ask: 'How confident am I that I have framed this correctly, and what would I need to be wrong about for a better frame to exist?' — the humility reframe makes frame-level certainty visible and testable.

select_when: Outcomes depend heavily on preparation, response quality, habit formation, or recovery from setbacks.
The environment is noisy or stressful, but there are still clear controllable levers around effort, pacing, or standards.
Responsibility needs to be translated into specific actions that a person or team can actually own and improve.
The current discussion is mixing controllable inputs with external conditions that must be accepted or redesigned.

failure_modes.mode: Internalizing Failures
failure_modes.description: Agency can become self-blame when people internalize failures caused by real external constraints.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate genuine agency from self-blame by mapping which factors the team can actually influence versus which are externally constrained.
failure_modes.source_quote: Internalizing Failures

failure_modes.mode: illusion
failure_modes.description: False confidence grows when people confuse the feeling of control with actual control over the system.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test the feeling of control against actual system dynamics; false confidence grows when perceived control exceeds real leverage.
failure_modes.source_quote: illusion

failure_modes.mode: Maslow's Hammer
failure_modes.description: Tunnel vision appears when a person treats a favorite framework as the only controllable answer.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit whether the chosen framework is the highest-leverage available tool or just the most familiar one.
failure_modes.source_quote: Maslow's Hammer

premortem_questions.description: What here is actually under our control, and what must be accepted or redesigned around?
