premortem_questions.source_quote: Seek out perspectives that genuinely differ from your own

heuristics.description: Ask for independent critique before exposing reviewers to the dominant story so the challenge is not anchored to the current framing.
heuristics.source_quote: people must make independent judgments to benefit from collective intelligence

heuristics.description: Use adversarial review to test the argument before reality does.
heuristics.source_quote: peer review your perspectives

heuristics.description: Favor the feedback that changes assumptions or exposes blind spots over the feedback that merely affirms tone or polish.
heuristics.source_quote: The value is in surfacing blind spots, not collecting praise

danger_when: The review loop is becoming social reassurance, vague brainstorming, or status theater instead of independent challenge.
The decision already has enough external challenge and now needs commitment, execution, or ownership rather than another round of perspective collection.
peer-review-your-perspectives.name: Peer Review Your Perspectives
reframing_when: The question rests on a single perspective that has never been stress-tested by someone who sees the situation differently — the frame has not survived adversarial review.
The question treats its framing as obvious or shared ('everyone agrees that...') when exposing it to a genuinely different viewpoint would reveal hidden assumptions or blind spots.
The question collapses a multi-sided situation into a binary by failing to solicit the perspective that would introduce the missing third option.
The question filters out disconfirming viewpoints by treating them as noise rather than as structural signals about what the frame is missing — survivorship in perspective selection.
peer-review-your-perspectives.reframing_move: Before accepting your framing, ask: 'Who would disagree with how I have framed this problem, and what would their version of the question look like?' — the peer review reframe subjects the question itself to adversarial scrutiny from a genuinely different vantage point.

select_when: Better pattern detection would change real-world performance because subtle cues separate expert judgment from novice confusion.
Decision quality depends on recognition speed rather than fresh analysis from zero on every case.
The domain rewards anomaly detection, signal filtering, or cue discrimination inside noisy inputs.
The real bottleneck is learning what matters in the field of stimuli rather than merely collecting more facts.

failure_modes.mode: Danger When Pattern Recognition Stops Updating.
failure_modes.description: Teams can miss crucial anomalies when old patterns feel familiar enough to suppress the cue that this case is different.
failure_modes.mitigation: Build anomaly-detection habits: train pattern experts to also practice noticing what does not fit the expected pattern, not just what confirms it.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Pattern Recognition Stops Updating.

failure_modes.mode: False Pattern Recognition and Availability Heuristic
failure_modes.description: False pattern recognition can produce disastrously wrong solutions when the handy frame gets applied to a genuinely novel problem.
failure_modes.mitigation: Before applying a recognized pattern, check structural fit: list three features the current case shares with the template and one it does not, then judge whether the mismatch matters.
failure_modes.source_quote: False Pattern Recognition and Availability Heuristic

failure_modes.mode: Curse of Knowledge
failure_modes.description: Experts can become hard to teach or understand when refined pattern libraries turn into abstraction that no longer matches novice needs.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pair expert pattern-matchers with structured novice explanations: require the expert to articulate the pattern in terms a newcomer can verify, exposing abstraction gaps.
failure_modes.source_quote: Curse of Knowledge

premortem_questions.description: What subtle cue would tell us this case does not belong in the familiar pattern bucket we are reaching for first?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when better pattern detection changes real-world performance.

premortem_questions.description: Which signal matters here, and which distractions are we still treating as if they were equally important?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best when a domain rewards anomaly detection, signal filtering, or cue discrimination.

premortem_questions.description: Where has expert abstraction drifted so far from the novice view that the real lesson no longer transfers?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Experts, having refined their models through PL, find it difficult to remember what it was like not to know something.

heuristics.description: Train pattern recognition with stories, timelines, and decision walkthroughs that expose the cues experts actually use.
heuristics.source_quote: Ask for Stories, Not Advice.

heuristics.description: Use plain-English explanation and analogy to expose blind spots in the pattern model before it hardens.
heuristics.source_quote: The Feynman Technique

heuristics.description: Tune the message to the audience so the critical pattern becomes visible instead of staying trapped in expert abstraction.
heuristics.source_quote: Complex ideas ... must be intentionally retuned for the croc brain.

danger_when: Accumulated pattern recognition is suppressing anomaly detection and making the team miss the cue that this case is meaningfully different.
An old familiar frame is being forced onto a new problem because it is the quickest pattern available rather than the right one.
perceptual-learning.name: Perceptual Learning

select_when: The goal requires sustained effort across months or years, and the main risk is quitting before learning compounds.
Repeated setbacks are expected, but clear practice loops and recovery routines still exist.
The challenge is hard because of friction, repetition, or morale decay rather than because the strategy is already disproven.
The team needs commitment and persistence, but only with explicit stop rules and evidence checks.

failure_modes.mode: escalation despite failing evidence
failure_modes.description: Persistence can turn into sunk-cost escalation when evidence says the effort should pivot or stop.
failure_modes.mitigation: Install kill criteria before starting: define in advance what evidence would trigger a pivot or stop, so escalation of commitment cannot disguise itself as grit.
failure_modes.source_quote: escalation despite failing evidence

failure_modes.mode: wrong framing
failure_modes.description: Teams can persist in the wrong frame because the original hypothesis was never pressure-tested well enough.
failure_modes.mitigation: Periodically re-validate the hypothesis: pause persistence at defined checkpoints to ask whether the original premise still holds or whether the team is grinding on a dead frame.
failure_modes.source_quote: wrong framing

failure_modes.mode: Cognitive Load Theory
failure_modes.description: Blind endurance can ignore cognitive limits and turn hard effort into poor learning or burnout.
failure_modes.mitigation: Monitor cognitive and physical load: set explicit recovery and reflection intervals so endurance does not degrade learning quality or push people into burnout.
failure_modes.source_quote: Cognitive Load Theory

premortem_questions.description: What evidence would tell us this is disciplined persistence versus mere doubling down?
premortem_questions.source_quote: what kind of progress would count as proof

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough if we keep pushing the current plan?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.description: What would we have to believe for this continued effort to still make sense?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

heuristics.description: Use SMART goals so persistence has a clear target instead of an abstract aspiration.
heuristics.source_quote: SMART Goal Setting

heuristics.description: Keep attention on process quality during execution and assess outcomes after the task is done.
heuristics.source_quote: Process Over Outcome Focus

heuristics.description: Use interrogative self-talk to pull up specific execution strategies under pressure.
heuristics.source_quote: Can I make a great pitch?

danger_when: More effort would only deepen sunk-cost exposure because the framing, market, or capability mix is wrong.
Persistence is becoming identity defense instead of a disciplined effort attached to real learning.
persistence-grit.name: Persistence Grit

select_when: The insight is already sound but attention, belief, or action is still blocked by indifference, overload, or weak framing.
Adoption is the bottleneck, and the communicator can pair strong substance with simple, credible, emotionally resonant packaging.
A message needs to match how the audience will actually process it emotionally and cognitively rather than how the expert wants to explain it.
The work must turn an intangible idea into something concrete, memorable, and actionable without losing the core message.

failure_modes.mode: brilliant communication of the wrong answer is even more harmful
failure_modes.description: A communicator can accelerate acceptance of a weak answer, turning better packaging into worse downstream decisions.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate packaging from substance: after any persuasive communication, require the audience to restate the core evidence and logic without the framing — if the case collapses, the persuasion was compensating for weakness.
failure_modes.source_quote: brilliant communication of the wrong answer is even more harmful

failure_modes.mode: Motivated Reasoning / Inside View
failure_modes.description: Motivated reasoning can turn persuasion into self-serving message design that seeks assent instead of truth.
failure_modes.mitigation: Appoint a 'message-truth auditor' who checks whether the persuasive frame accurately represents the underlying data, trade-offs, and uncertainties.
failure_modes.source_quote: Motivated Reasoning / Inside View

failure_modes.mode: muddy up an otherwise easy yes
failure_modes.description: Complexity overload can muddy an otherwise easy yes by forcing the audience to process more detail than they can carry.
failure_modes.mitigation: Simplify the message to the decision-relevant core: strip detail until the audience can hold the entire argument in working memory, then stop.
failure_modes.source_quote: muddy up an otherwise easy yes

premortem_questions.description: What do I want the audience to think, feel, and do?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Think, Feel, and Do

premortem_questions.description: Are we selling the solution, or are we still telling the story of the search?
premortem_questions.source_quote: the story of the solution

premortem_questions.description: If the substance were weaker than we think, what part of this packaging would most likely hide that weakness from the audience?
premortem_questions.source_quote: persuasive technique is used to accelerate acceptance of a weak answer

heuristics.description: Use pre-suasion and salience cues to prepare attention before delivering the core ask.
heuristics.source_quote: Pre-suasion

heuristics.description: Package the message so it is simple, concrete, credible, emotional, and story-carried enough to survive overload.
heuristics.source_quote: SUCCESs Checklist

heuristics.description: Match the appeal to how the audience is actually processing the decision instead of defaulting to the communicator's preferred style.
heuristics.source_quote: System 1/System 2 Match

danger_when: Persuasive technique is being used to accelerate acceptance of a weak answer.
Influence tactics would override the recipient's autonomy or suppress the friction that should expose an important flaw.
persuasion-principles.name: Persuasion Principles

select_when: Advancement criteria and actual role requirements are misaligned.
A leadership problem may really be a role-design, support, or talent-allocation problem rather than simple incompetence.
Promotion decisions carry compounding organizational cost across execution, morale, or downstream hiring.

failure_modes.mode: Organizations can promote excellence in one role into incompetence in the next by assuming the same ...
failure_modes.description: Organizations can promote excellence in one role into incompetence in the next by assuming the same skill set scales upward unchanged.
failure_modes.mitigation: Evaluate promotion readiness against the demands of the target role, not the current role: design assessments that test the skills the new position actually requires.
failure_modes.source_quote: promoting individuals based purely on their excellence in a current role rather than their competence and specific skillset for the next role inevitably leads to incompetence at higher levels

failure_modes.mode: It helps separate incompetence, mismatch, and missing support rather than collapsing them into one judgment.
failure_modes.description: Leaders can misdiagnose a role-design or support problem as an individual character flaw.
failure_modes.mitigation: When performance drops after promotion, diagnose the system first: check whether role design, support, training, or team structure is the bottleneck before attributing failure to the individual.
failure_modes.source_quote: It helps separate incompetence, mismatch, and missing support rather than collapsing them into one judgment.

failure_modes.mode: Danger When the Diagnosis Becomes Fatalism
failure_modes.description: The label can become fatalism if nobody redesigns the role, support, or promotion criteria after naming the pattern.
failure_modes.mitigation: After naming the pattern, act on it: redesign the role, add coaching, create parallel career tracks, or adjust promotion criteria — the label without structural change is just fatalism.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When the Diagnosis Becomes Fatalism

premortem_questions.description: What specific skills does this next role require that the candidate has never had to use before?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What are the specific skills this individual has never had to use that are essential for this new role?

premortem_questions.description: If this promotion is a spectacular failure a year from now, what is more likely: bad fit, bad support, or bad role design?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If this promotion is a spectacular failure a year from now, what specifically was the root cause

premortem_questions.description: Are we rewarding visible success in the current role because it is familiar, or because it predicts success in the next one?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Availability Heuristic and Substitution Bias

heuristics.description: Assess the distinct skills of the next role directly instead of treating past excellence as sufficient proof.
heuristics.source_quote: the organization must assess the distinct skills required for each role

heuristics.description: Use complementary team design to cover capability gaps that do not have to sink the promotion.
heuristics.source_quote: find solutions such that their weaknesses do not matter

heuristics.description: Frame the promotion review around future-fit capability, not only around historical reward logic.
heuristics.source_quote: rewarding past performance without validating future-fit capability

danger_when: The diagnosis is being used fatalistically after the fact instead of driving role redesign, support, or promotion-criteria repair.
People only want a cynical slogan about bad bosses rather than a real capability-fit analysis.
peter-principle.name: Peter Principle

select_when: A negotiation or partnership sounds like a price or policy dispute on the surface, but the real issue may be leverage, outside options, or commitment costs.
The team needs to know who can actually wait, walk away, redirect demand, or absorb reversal costs before accepting the stated framing of the deal.
A commitment, integration, or dependency may invert leverage over time even if the current terms look favorable at signature.
The next move should map fallback lanes, enforcement credibility, and the point where lock-in changes what is actually negotiable.

failure_modes.mode: Teams can treat verbal toughness as leverage even when the walk-away is not credible and the fallbac...
failure_modes.description: Teams can treat verbal toughness as leverage even when the walk-away is not credible and the fallback option is worse than the current deal.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit walk-away credibility: before claiming leverage, verify that your fallback option is real, funded, and executable — not just a negotiating bluff.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when a team treats verbal toughness as leverage even though the walk-away is not credible and the fallback option is worse than the current deal.

failure_modes.mode: A signed deal can be mistaken for durable control even though the next commitment hands timing, data...
failure_modes.description: A signed deal can be mistaken for durable control even though the next commitment hands timing, data, or switching-cost leverage to the counterparty.
failure_modes.mitigation: Track commitment gradients across the deal lifecycle: map where timing, data access, or switching costs shift leverage after signing, and negotiate protections for those inflection points.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when leaders mistake a signed contract for durable control even though the next operational commitment hands timing, data, or switching cost leverage to the counterparty.

failure_modes.mode: negotiating headline price while ignoring the commitment gradient
failure_modes.description: Teams can win headline concessions while quietly giving away the commitment gradient that later inverts bargaining power.
failure_modes.mitigation: Distinguish headline wins from structural control: after any negotiation, check whether the concessions you won matter less than the commitment dynamics you gave away.
failure_modes.source_quote: negotiating headline price while ignoring the commitment gradient

premortem_questions.description: Which party can actually absorb a walk-away with less damage this quarter, and what evidence proves it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Which party can absorb a walk-away with less damage this quarter, and what evidence proves it?

premortem_questions.description: What would make our walk-away or delay threat binding rather than rhetorical?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would make our walk-away or delay threat actually binding rather than rhetorical?

premortem_questions.description: At what milestone does our cost to reverse become higher than the counterparty's cost to lose us?
premortem_questions.source_quote: At what milestone does our cost to reverse become higher than the counterparty's cost to lose us?

heuristics.description: Map outside options before discussing terms because fallback quality is part of the real price floor.
heuristics.source_quote: Map outside options before discussing terms

heuristics.description: Separate stated willingness from credible walk-away by checking enforcement cost, internal approval, and operational fallback.
heuristics.source_quote: Separate stated willingness from credible walk-away

heuristics.description: Track leverage inversion over time so early wins do not hide later lock-in and dependency costs.
heuristics.source_quote: Track leverage inversion over time

danger_when: The true constraint is exogenous or non-negotiable, so treating the situation as a power contest would distract from the actual bottleneck.
The team is already over-reading symbolic status or tone as leverage and needs a factual capability or execution review instead.
power-dynamics.name: Power Dynamics

select_when: A few drivers are likely to explain a disproportionate share of the outcome, so broad effort would waste attention.
The team needs permission to ignore the long tail long enough to fix the variables that actually move the system.
Scarce attention or capital must be concentrated on the handful of customers, levers, or constraints with outsized impact.
The current plan treats the workload as evenly distributed even though the source of value is probably highly skewed.

failure_modes.mode: Mistaking the Symptom for the Cause
failure_modes.description: Teams can focus on the wrong 20 percent by mistaking visible symptoms for real drivers.
failure_modes.mitigation: Validate which 20% actually drives the outcome with data before concentrating resources: measure, don't assume.
failure_modes.source_quote: Mistaking the Symptom for the Cause

failure_modes.mode: every uneven distribution is casually assumed to be power-law shaped
failure_modes.description: Prematurely forcing a power-law frame can create false pattern recognition.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test the power-law fit against alternative distributions before committing to the frame; premature pattern-matching produces false confidence.
failure_modes.source_quote: every uneven distribution is casually assumed to be power-law shaped

failure_modes.mode: ignore reliability, fairness, or resilience concerns that live in the long tail
failure_modes.description: Long-tail reliability or resilience concerns can be ignored while chasing dominant drivers.
failure_modes.mitigation: Budget explicitly for long-tail resilience: allocate monitoring and contingency resources to the non-dominant tail where rare but consequential failures live.
failure_modes.source_quote: ignore reliability, fairness, or resilience concerns that live in the long tail

premortem_questions.description: Is the identified 20 percent actually the driver, or merely a visible symptom?
premortem_questions.source_quote: driver of the problem, or merely a highly visible symptom

premortem_questions.description: What evidence would force us to abandon our current theory about the dominant few?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What data or evidence, if true, would force us to abandon our current hypothesis

premortem_questions.description: Which different cuts through the problem expose a better candidate 20 percent?
premortem_questions.source_quote: try several cuts at the problem

heuristics.description: Start by looking for the vital few drivers with the highest leverage.
heuristics.source_quote: 20% of the actions we take are responsible for 80% of the results

heuristics.description: Do not boil the ocean; gather only enough facts to prove or disprove the key hypothesis.
heuristics.source_quote: Don't Boil the Ocean

heuristics.description: Prioritize issues that matter most and that you can actually influence.
heuristics.source_quote: those that they can actually influence

danger_when: An uneven distribution is being casually labeled a power law without checking whether the tail behavior really fits.
The search for dominant drivers would ignore reliability, fairness, or resilience risks living in the long tail.
power-laws.name: Power Laws

select_when: Attention placement will shape how the next message is interpreted almost as much as the message itself.
The audience is overloaded, distracted, or relying on default associations, so receptivity must be created before analysis begins.
A complex idea needs a simple, salient entry frame before it can get a fair hearing.
Sequencing and salience determine whether the audience will even consider the argument on its merits.

failure_modes.mode: Manipulation and Ethical Failure
failure_modes.description: Pre-suasion can cross into manipulation when associative priming is decoupled from the actual merits of the proposal.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit every priming move against the substance it leads to: if the associative frame would not survive scrutiny of the actual evidence, the pre-suasion has crossed into manipulation.
failure_modes.source_quote: Manipulation and Ethical Failure

failure_modes.mode: Confirmation Bias and Availability Heuristic
failure_modes.description: Availability and confirmation dynamics can make the audience more vulnerable to the wrong mental bucket once attention has been primed badly.
failure_modes.mitigation: After any priming exposure, run a de-biasing check: ask what the audience would conclude if they had encountered the proposal without the preceding context.
failure_modes.source_quote: Confirmation Bias and Availability Heuristic

failure_modes.mode: Breakdown of Trust (Inconsistency)
failure_modes.description: Trust can break if the expectations created during the priming stage are not matched by the substance that follows.
failure_modes.mitigation: Match promises to delivery: track whether the expectations set during the priming stage are fulfilled by the subsequent substance, and flag any trust-damaging gap.
failure_modes.source_quote: Breakdown of Trust (Inconsistency)

premortem_questions.description: Can I make a great pitch?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Can I make a great pitch?

premortem_questions.description: Which association are we trying to prime, and would that association still be fair if the audience slowed down and analyzed the proposal?
premortem_questions.source_quote: identify the desired end-state association

premortem_questions.description: What proof will the audience need after the emotional sale so the idea can survive contact with System 2?
premortem_questions.source_quote: handouts and documents as proof

heuristics.description: Guide preliminary attention to the concept you want associated with the message before you make the ask.
heuristics.source_quote: guides the preliminary attention

heuristics.description: Use an emotional sale for the live moment and reserve detailed documents for the later proof burden.
heuristics.source_quote: The Emotional Sale vs. Rational Proof

heuristics.description: Choose a simple, sticky, high-context frame so the audience has a mental bucket before the details arrive.
heuristics.source_quote: high-concept pitch

danger_when: Receptivity engineering is crossing into manipulation theater decoupled from the actual merits of the proposal.
The audience is already in deep analytical mode, so associative priming would add less value than direct evidence and clear logic.
pre-suasion.name: Pre Suasion

select_when: A project is still pre-commitment and the cost of finding failure modes now is much lower than after launch.
Social pressure, optimism, or executive momentum would otherwise suppress dissent and make the plan feel safer than it is.
Success depends on multiple conditions lining up, so the team needs to expose weak links, reversibility limits, and contingency gaps in advance.
The main risk is not effort but unwarranted optimism about a conjunctive plan with too many silent assumptions.

failure_modes.mode: A premortem becomes superficial when it produces an obvious worry list without owners, mitigations, ...
failure_modes.description: A premortem becomes superficial when it produces an obvious worry list without owners, mitigations, or decision changes.
failure_modes.mitigation: Tie each surfaced risk to a named owner and a concrete plan change; if no risk alters the plan, the exercise was performative.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the exercise produces an obvious list of worries without owners, mitigations, or decision changes tied to what was surfaced.

failure_modes.mode: Teams can substitute a shallow pro-con exercise for the harder work of imagining systemic failure pa...
failure_modes.description: Teams can substitute a shallow pro-con exercise for the harder work of imagining systemic failure paths.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use the dialectic standard: every failure hypothesis must be met with a direct challenge before synthesis, not just listed alongside positives.
failure_modes.source_quote: A common anti-pattern is substituting the deep, critical work of a Premortem with a simpler, more available tool, such as a basic pro-con list.

failure_modes.mode: Mental fatigue can make the exercise lazy, obvious, and non-imaginative because participants default...
failure_modes.description: Mental fatigue can make the exercise lazy, obvious, and non-imaginative because participants default to fast thinking instead of deeper failure exploration.
failure_modes.mitigation: Simulate extremes and outside perspectives to force novel failure paths beyond the obvious; stress-test edge cases that depleted participants would skip.
failure_modes.source_quote: If participants are already mentally depleted, they may rely on the fast, intuitive System 1 thinking ... resulting in a superficial analysis.

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.description: Do we still remember the question we are trying to answer, or have we substituted an easier one?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Do we still remember the question we are trying to answer? Or have we substituted an easier one?

premortem_questions.description: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

heuristics.description: Assume the project failed and work backward from that future state to surface the weak links that could have caused it.
heuristics.source_quote: Before the project begins, the team gathers and assumes that the project has failed spectacularly. They then spend time detailing the plausible reasons why it failed.

heuristics.description: Expose second-order consequences and domino effects instead of evaluating only the first visible step.
heuristics.source_quote: The application of the Premortem requires participants to engage in second-order thinking.

heuristics.description: Tie each surfaced failure path to an owner, mitigation, or reversal threshold so the exercise changes the plan instead of decorating it.
heuristics.source_quote: Danger when the exercise produces an obvious list of worries without owners, mitigations, or decision changes tied to what was surfaced.

danger_when: The exercise is producing an obvious list of worries without owners, mitigations, or decision changes tied to what was surfaced.
The team is using premortem language performatively while remaining unwilling to challenge the favored plan or change commitments.
premortem.name: Premortem
reframing_when: The question assumes the plan will succeed and asks how to execute it — prospective hindsight from a simulated failure would surface risks the forward-looking question suppresses.
The question asks 'how do we achieve X?' without asking 'what would guarantee we fail at X?' — the anti-goal formulation is missing.
The question inherits optimistic assumptions about a multi-step plan without stress-testing whether each conjunctive step actually has a high probability of success.
The question is being asked in a social context where executive momentum or groupthink suppresses dissent — the premortem creates psychological safety for airing doubts about the question itself.
premortem.reframing_move: Assume the plan has already failed spectacularly and ask 'what went wrong?' — the shift from forward-looking optimism to backward-looking failure analysis surfaces specific, tractable risks that the success-framed question cannot see.

select_when: Different segments genuinely value the same outcome differently and can be served through distinct offer shapes or terms.
Segmentation can be grounded in willingness to pay, urgency, use case, or risk rather than shallow demographics.
The offer architecture can separate segments clearly enough to capture value without confusing buyers.
The team needs to tailor pricing or packaging to the specific pains, beliefs, and desired outcomes of different buyers.

failure_modes.mode: somewhat generic
failure_modes.description: Generic offers blur segment differences and leave the business sounding like every competitor.
failure_modes.mitigation: Segment messaging around genuine value differences: customize offers based on what each segment actually needs, not just willingness to pay.
failure_modes.source_quote: somewhat generic

failure_modes.mode: customers can arbitrage between tiers
failure_modes.description: Tiering can backfire if customers can compare treatment easily and judge it as unfair.
failure_modes.mitigation: Anticipate transparency: design tiering so that if all customers saw all tiers simultaneously, the differentiation would feel fair rather than exploitative.
failure_modes.source_quote: customers can arbitrage between tiers

failure_modes.mode: damages long-term trust
failure_modes.description: Short-term extraction can damage long-term trust and willingness to buy later.
failure_modes.mitigation: Balance extraction against relationship: cap short-term pricing tactics where they would erode long-term trust, repeat purchase, or referral willingness.
failure_modes.source_quote: damages long-term trust

premortem_questions.description: Compared to what does this offer look like a clear bargain or a risky mistake?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Compared to what?

premortem_questions.description: What would each segment have to believe to accept this price and structure?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would the customer have to believe

premortem_questions.description: Where could segmentation create confusion, arbitrage, or trust damage?
premortem_questions.source_quote: confusing buyers or eroding trust

heuristics.description: Define one specific problem for one specific type of buyer in one specific way.
heuristics.source_quote: 1 Specific Problem, for 1 Specific Type Of Person, in 1 Specific Way

heuristics.description: Translate every feature into a segment-specific benefit before discussing price.
heuristics.source_quote: features-to-benefits conversion

heuristics.description: Tailor the framing and voice of the offer to the buyer's processing style.
heuristics.source_quote: Tailoring Communication by Archetype

danger_when: Customers can easily compare tiers or arbitrage between them, making the pricing feel unfair instead of tailored.
Short-term value extraction would damage trust, brand clarity, or future willingness to buy.
price-discrimination.name: Price Discrimination

select_when: Work is being delegated across an objective gap where the executing party may benefit from choices that do not serve the owner of the outcome.
Specialized knowledge, hidden intentions, or role ambiguity make it hard to tell whether the agent's behavior is aligned with the principal's real goals.
The main challenge is to design incentives, oversight, and role clarity so delegated work stays tied to the larger objective.
Dashboards or formal compliance are starting to look like substitutes for actual alignment, so the team needs to inspect divergence underneath the metrics.

failure_modes.mode: Alignment metrics can hide real divergence when dashboards or formal compliance are mistaken for act...
failure_modes.description: Alignment metrics can hide real divergence when dashboards or formal compliance are mistaken for actual goal alignment.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit alignment beyond dashboards: periodically verify that formal metrics still track the principal's real goal, and sunset metrics that have become performative.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Alignment Metrics Hide Real Behavior: The model is misused when principals mistake dashboards, formal compliance, or reported activity for true goal alignment while the agent still benefits from divergence underneath.

failure_modes.mode: Micromanagement can treat the case-at-hand while leaving the machine-level design error untouched.
failure_modes.description: Micromanagement can treat the case-at-hand while leaving the machine-level design error untouched.
failure_modes.mitigation: Address the system, not the symptom: when misalignment surfaces, redesign incentives, information flows, or role boundaries rather than adding supervision layers.
failure_modes.source_quote: This results in micromanagement, fails to build the Agent's long-term capacity, and prevents continuous system improvement.

failure_modes.mode: Diagnosis can go wrong when leaders confuse capability failure with capacity, system, or incentive f...
failure_modes.description: Diagnosis can go wrong when leaders confuse capability failure with capacity, system, or incentive failure.
failure_modes.mitigation: Diagnose before blaming: distinguish whether poor agent performance stems from misaligned incentives, insufficient capability, inadequate resources, or bad system design.
failure_modes.source_quote: When diagnosing a failure, the Principal confuses a poor outcome due to bad system design or lack of resources (capacity issue) with the Agent's personal flaws or incompetence (capability issue).

premortem_questions.description: What is the one-day answer?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What is the one-day answer?

premortem_questions.description: Who should do what differently?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Who should do what differently?

premortem_questions.description: If this outcome is bad, is the responsible party incapable and/or is the design bad?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If this outcome is bad, is the Responsible Party incapable and/or is the design bad?

heuristics.description: Define roles, decision boundaries, and expectations clearly enough that delegated work stays legible to the principal's actual objective.
heuristics.source_quote: The Agent's role explicitly defines what they are supposed to do and how they fit into the larger architecture.

heuristics.description: Diagnose drift sequentially by separating goals, problems, root causes, design changes, and execution instead of blurring them together.
heuristics.source_quote: The Principal must execute these steps sequentially without blurring them.

heuristics.description: Spell out what would have to be true for the current delegated setup to work, so hidden alignment assumptions become inspectable.
heuristics.source_quote: It involves spelling out all the implicit assumptions underlying a proposal or perspective, forcing clarity and rigor in thinking.

danger_when: The response is collapsing into micromanagement of the current case instead of diagnosing the machine-level design that created the drift.
The model is being used to assume bad faith when the main issue is capability, capacity, or a muddled objective rather than a true alignment split.
principal-agent-problem.name: Principal Agent Problem

select_when: The number of plausible tasks, issues, or opportunities already exceeds the true execution capacity of the team.
Sequencing matters and explicit non-goals or kill criteria can protect high-leverage work from constant interruption.
The organization must choose what gets to be first, what gets deferred, and what gets eliminated entirely.

failure_modes.mode: Boiling the Ocean
failure_modes.description: Boiling the ocean can waste effort on comprehensiveness long after the core question is already clear enough to resolve.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a 'good enough' threshold for analysis before opening the prioritization exercise: define when the ranking is clear enough to act and stop refining.
failure_modes.source_quote: Boiling the Ocean

failure_modes.mode: Unwarranted Optimism (Planning Fallacy)
failure_modes.description: Unwarranted optimism can make a complex plan look executable even when too many steps must go right together.
failure_modes.mitigation: Stress-test the top-ranked items against outside-view failure rates: check whether the plan requires too many conjunctive successes to be realistic.
failure_modes.source_quote: Unwarranted Optimism (Planning Fallacy)

failure_modes.mode: Premature Filtering
failure_modes.description: Premature filtering can steer the shortlist toward subconscious preferences instead of objective leverage.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use blind ranking or independent scoring before group discussion to prevent anchoring and social-proof effects from steering the shortlist.
failure_modes.source_quote: Premature Filtering

premortem_questions.description: What is the one-day answer?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What is the one-day answer?

premortem_questions.description: How does what you're doing solve the problem, and is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: How does what you’re doing solve the problem? How does it advance your thinking? Is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?

premortem_questions.description: Do I really need to be optimizing here, or is the best thing to do simply to eliminate it completely?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Do I really need to be optimizing here, or is the best thing to do simply to eliminate it completely?

heuristics.description: Use the Pareto principle to find the few activities that generate most of the leverage.
heuristics.source_quote: The Pareto Principle (80/20 Rule)

heuristics.description: Use the Eisenhower matrix to keep urgency from quietly displacing what matters.
heuristics.source_quote: The Eisenhower Matrix (Important vs. Urgent)

heuristics.description: Make the work hypothesis-driven and end-product oriented before gathering more analysis.
heuristics.source_quote: Hypothesis-Driven Problem Solving (HDPS)

danger_when: Prioritization is becoming endless reshuffling, so the team talks constantly about leverage but rarely commits long enough to realize it.
Urgent, visible work keeps winning by default and the system is quietly starving the slower, higher-value work it claims to care about.
prioritization.name: Prioritization

select_when: Individually sensible moves are producing a collectively worse outcome because each actor has a credible incentive to defect.
The team needs to separate bad actors from bad rules by making the payoff structure, trust assumptions, and enforcement gaps explicit.
A strategic interaction depends on whether cooperation can be made durable through trust, repeat interaction, or punishment for cheating.
A recommendation must account for countermoves from rivals, partners, or internal actors whose incentives are not naturally aligned.

failure_modes.mode: The PD highlights a crucial insight: sometimes a strategic interaction is governed by "irredeemably lousy rules".
failure_modes.description: Teams can treat a bad payoff structure as if better individual behavior alone will rescue the outcome.
failure_modes.mitigation: Before assuming betrayal dynamics, check the payoff structure: verify that the situation is actually a prisoner's dilemma rather than a coordination problem solvable through process design.
failure_modes.source_quote: The PD highlights a crucial insight: sometimes a strategic interaction is governed by "irredeemably lousy rules".

failure_modes.mode: Anti-Pattern: Treating Every Coordination Problem as Betrayal.
failure_modes.description: People can impose betrayal logic on a coordination problem that is actually better explained by process repair or shared identity.
failure_modes.mitigation: Explore mechanism design: ask whether changing the rules, communication channels, or enforcement structures could shift the game to one where cooperation is the dominant strategy.
failure_modes.source_quote: Anti-Pattern: Treating Every Coordination Problem as Betrayal.

failure_modes.mode: A competitive frame can become self-fulfilling when ambiguous moves are read as proof that the other...
failure_modes.description: A competitive frame can become self-fulfilling when ambiguous moves are read as proof that the other side will defect.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test ambiguous moves charitably before defaulting to defection: when the other side's intent is unclear, design a low-cost probe that reveals intent without triggering escalation.
failure_modes.source_quote: we might misinterpret a competitor's action ... which reinforces our own doubts and pressures us toward the herd action (betrayal)

premortem_questions.description: What frame other than a competitive one could govern this interaction?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What frame other than a competitive one could govern this interaction?

premortem_questions.description: What would you have to believe to accept the competitive outcome as unavoidable?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.description: What trust, enforcement, or repeat-interaction mechanism would have to exist for cooperation to survive?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Works when trust, enforcement, or repeat interaction are the real design variables.

heuristics.description: Make the payoff structure explicit before blaming individual actors for a bad collective outcome.
heuristics.source_quote: The model helps separate bad actors from bad rules by making the payoff structure explicit.

heuristics.description: Simulate likely countermoves so the team reasons from incentives instead of from one-sided intention stories.
heuristics.source_quote: Teams may be broken into an "attack team" and a "defense team," playing out a series of moves and countermoves.

heuristics.description: Change the game when possible by improving cheating detection, punishment, or collaboration conditions instead of optimizing inside a broken rule set.
heuristics.source_quote: The most critical actionable step derived from the PD is the effort to change the game itself.

danger_when: The behavior is better explained by simple process repair, shared identity, or non-adversarial coordination than by defection logic.
The model is being used to impose a hostile competitive frame on a problem whose real bottleneck is understanding, not incentive conflict.
prisoners-dilemma.name: Prisoners Dilemma

select_when: The decision is not yes-or-no but more-likely-or-less-likely, so binary framing would hide meaningful uncertainty.
The team needs to compare ranges, distributions, or expected value instead of treating one forecast as certain.
Uncertainty can be reduced through better priors, disconfirming evidence, or explicit probability ranges rather than by telling a cleaner story.
The current argument sounds precise, but the real task is to quantify how much confidence the team actually has.

failure_modes.mode: Over-reliance on the Mean Outcome
failure_modes.description: Over-reliance on the mean can hide low-probability, high-impact outcomes that matter more than the base case.
failure_modes.mitigation: Report the full distribution, not just the expected value: require decision-makers to acknowledge tail scenarios and state what level of downside they can absorb.
failure_modes.source_quote: Over-reliance on the Mean Outcome

failure_modes.mode: Availability Bias and Representativeness
failure_modes.description: Availability and representativeness can distort probability estimates by privileging vivid stories over statistical fit.
failure_modes.mitigation: Calibrate estimates against base rates and reference classes before adjusting for case-specific details; flag any estimate that deviates sharply from the outside view.
failure_modes.source_quote: Availability Bias and Representativeness

failure_modes.mode: The Flaw of False Precision
failure_modes.description: False precision can make uncertainty look solved when the team is really only hiding what it does not know.
failure_modes.mitigation: Label confidence intervals honestly: when uncertainty is high, widen the range rather than narrowing it for false comfort, and flag any precision that exceeds the data quality.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Flaw of False Precision

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "Is the worst case bad enough?"

premortem_questions.description: What data is missing that might spoil our story?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What data is missing that might spoil our story?"

premortem_questions.description: What percentage confidence do I have in this estimate, and what information would narrow that range?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What percentage confidence do I have in this estimate, and what information would narrow that range?"

heuristics.description: Frame decisions as expected-value calculations instead of looking for options with no downside at all.
heuristics.source_quote: Expected Value Calculations

heuristics.description: Decorrelate errors by collecting independent probability judgments before group discussion starts.
heuristics.source_quote: Obtain multiple, independent judgments or probabilistic assessments on the issue before any group discussion.

heuristics.description: Run sensitivity analysis and communicate uncertainty ranges explicitly instead of pretending to know more than you do.
heuristics.source_quote: Sensitivity Analysis

danger_when: The work requires a concrete commitment now, but the team is using probability language to postpone choosing after the key economics are already clear.
The probability estimates are being treated as exact-looking numbers without enough evidence, base-rate discipline, or sensitivity testing behind them.
probabilistic-thinking.name: Probabilistic Thinking

select_when: The team is working hard on a problem but keeps producing weak answers, weak priorities, or repeated confusion.
Progress depends less on more data than on asking a better question, changing the boundary, or redefining the unit of analysis.
Multiple plausible frames exist and the cost of choosing the wrong one is high.
The current approach feels stuck inside one familiar lens and needs several alternative cuts before analysis deepens.

failure_modes.mode: a flawed problem definition is a primary pitfall leading to irrelevant or costly solutions
failure_modes.description: A flawed problem definition can drive elegant work toward irrelevant or costly solutions.
failure_modes.mitigation: Before investing in analysis, verify the problem statement: ask three stakeholders to independently state the problem and reconcile the differences before proceeding.
failure_modes.source_quote: a flawed problem definition is a primary pitfall leading to irrelevant or costly solutions

failure_modes.mode: Teams can jump into analysis too early and boil the ocean before the real structure of the problem i...
failure_modes.description: Teams can jump into analysis too early and boil the ocean before the real structure of the problem is clear.
failure_modes.mitigation: Timebox the framing phase: set a clear boundary between problem definition and analysis, and do not allow analysis to start until the frame is tested and agreed.
failure_modes.source_quote: A human inclination to rush into data gathering and complex analysis without achieving clarity on the underlying problem structure.

failure_modes.mode: To the man with only a hammer, everything looks like a nail
failure_modes.description: One favored frame can become a hammer that tortures reality to fit the model instead of exposing better cuts.
failure_modes.mitigation: Rotate frames deliberately: after settling on a primary frame, force a pass through at least one structurally different frame to expose what the first one hides.
failure_modes.source_quote: To the man with only a hammer, everything looks like a nail

premortem_questions.description: What other disciplines or frameworks might view this problem entirely differently?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What other disciplines or frameworks might view this problem entirely differently?

premortem_questions.description: What evidence would conclusively prove our current framing wrong?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What evidence would conclusively prove our current framing/hypothesis wrong?

premortem_questions.description: What is the single core question we are trying to answer?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What is the single core question we are trying to answer?

heuristics.description: Test several cuts through the problem before committing to the current unit of analysis or point of view.
heuristics.source_quote: Different frames often yield different insights, necessitating the testing of several approaches.

heuristics.description: Use a structured problem statement so owner, constraints, actors, and success criteria are explicit before analysis deepens.
heuristics.source_quote: A structured framework for rigorous problem definition, ensuring clarity on the Trouble, Owner, Success criteria, Constraints, and Actors involved.

heuristics.description: Keep a one-day-answer mentality so reframing sharpens the next decision instead of becoming endless conceptual drift.
heuristics.source_quote: What’s the one-day answer?

danger_when: The team is using reframing as an excuse to postpone commitment instead of improving the next concrete move.
A preferred hypothesis is already hardening into the answer, so reframing theater would only decorate confirmation-bias rather than challenge it.
problem-framing-and-reframing.name: Problem Framing And Reframing
reframing_when: The question imposes a single frame on a situation where multiple plausible frames exist and the cost of choosing the wrong one is high.
The question's unit of analysis, boundary, or success criteria may be wrong — progress depends on asking a better question, not finding a better answer within the current frame.
The question is stuck inside one familiar professional or disciplinary lens and alternative cuts through the problem would expose different insights.
The question treats a flawed problem definition as settled when refinement through different frameworks would reveal that the real problem is different from the stated one.
problem-framing-and-reframing.reframing_move: Test multiple frames: apply different lenses (economic, social, technical, user-centered) to the same situation and ask which frame produces the most insightful 'cut' through the problem — the right question often emerges from the frame comparison, not from deeper analysis within any single frame.

select_when: A choice is being driven more by perceived gains, losses, or reference points than by objective value.
Different framings of the same economics are likely to change behavior materially, especially under uncertainty or emotional pressure.
A recommendation needs to account for loss aversion, risk-seeking in the loss domain, or reluctance to accept a sure small loss to avoid a larger uncertain one later.
The next move should separate descriptive human response from the normative question of what the decision should be.

failure_modes.mode: Danger when framing is used to manipulate rather than clarify
failure_modes.description: Leaders can exploit loss aversion or threat framing to force compliance without improving the underlying economics, truth, or decision quality.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit framing for manipulation: when loss language is used to drive urgency, verify that the underlying economics genuinely warrant the alarm and are not just leveraging loss aversion.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when framing is used to manipulate rather than clarify

failure_modes.mode: Normative Irrelevance
failure_modes.description: A recommendation can drift into descriptive storytelling about how people behave and silently replace the normative question of what they should choose.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate the descriptive diagnosis from the normative recommendation: after explaining how people tend to react to gains and losses, explicitly state what they should choose given the actual expected values.
failure_modes.source_quote: Normative Irrelevance

failure_modes.mode: immediate emotional impact of gains and losses
failure_modes.description: Short-term emotional reactions to gains and losses can crowd out longer-term utility and prudent risk judgment.
failure_modes.mitigation: Extend the time horizon: before acting on an emotional reaction to a gain or loss, project the decision forward and check whether the short-term feeling aligns with long-term utility.
failure_modes.source_quote: immediate emotional impact of gains and losses

premortem_questions.description: What changes if these same economics are reframed as gains, losses, or a different reference point?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Framing

premortem_questions.description: Are we becoming risk-seeking because we feel trapped in the loss domain rather than because the underlying bet improved?
premortem_questions.source_quote: risk seeking in the loss domain

premortem_questions.description: What would the choice look like under a colder expected-value or decision-analysis lens?
premortem_questions.source_quote: not a plausible normative decision theory

heuristics.description: Inspect the active reference point before debating the option set because behavior is relative to perceived gains and losses.
heuristics.source_quote: evaluation is relative to a neutral reference point

heuristics.description: Test multiple framings of the same choice to see whether the recommendation depends on presentation rather than fundamentals.
heuristics.source_quote: immense power of framing

heuristics.description: Use prospect theory to predict human response, then pair it with a colder analytic framework before committing.
heuristics.source_quote: descriptive decision theory

danger_when: The analysis still needs a normative decision framework and prospect-theory language would only redescribe behavior without improving the choice.
Framing is being used to manipulate compliance without improving truth, economics, or decision quality.
prospect-theory.name: Prospect Theory
reframing_when: The question is framed entirely around avoiding a loss when an equivalent gain frame would open different options — loss aversion is shaping the question's reference point.

The question assumes a specific reference point (current state, past performance, competitor benchmark) without examining whether that reference point is the right one — reference dependence is invisible.
The question triggers a threat response ('how do we avoid losing X?') when a desire frame ('how do we create Y?') would produce less defensive, more generative answers.
The question makes a choice seem binary because one option is framed as a loss and the other as a gain, when both are actually equivalent outcomes seen from different reference points.
prospect-theory.reframing_move: Flip the reference point: if the question is framed around loss, reframe it around gain (and vice versa) — then ask whether the choice changes. If it does, the original question's frame was doing the deciding, not the facts.

select_when: The team is suppressing dissent, risk signals, or upward challenge because status or fear is distorting what gets said.
Better decisions depend on candid truth and error surfacing, but current norms reward comfort, politeness, or silence.
A leader is inviting openness, and the next risk is that standards, ownership, or decision rights stay too vague for correction to matter.
The work would improve if disagreement could be expressed without social punishment while still staying accountable to outcomes.

failure_modes.mode: Psychological safety fails when it is redefined as comfort, politeness, or protection from challenge...
failure_modes.description: Psychological safety fails when it is redefined as comfort, politeness, or protection from challenge instead of candid truth.
failure_modes.mitigation: Define safety as candor, not comfort: make it explicit that psychological safety means people can challenge ideas, raise concerns, and disagree — not that they are shielded from hard feedback.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when psychological safety is redefined as comfort, politeness, or protection from challenge instead of candid truth in service of better decisions.

failure_modes.mode: Leaders can invite openness while leaving ownership, standards, or decision rights too vague for cor...
failure_modes.description: Leaders can invite openness while leaving ownership, standards, or decision rights too vague for correction to matter.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pair openness with accountability: when inviting truth-telling, also clarify decision rights, quality standards, and ownership so that candor leads to action, not just venting.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when leaders invite openness but leave ownership, standards, or decision rights vague, creating talk without correction or accountability.

failure_modes.mode: More discussion becomes a failure mode when the evidence already supports a decision or a reversible...
failure_modes.description: More discussion becomes a failure mode when the evidence already supports a decision or a reversible experiment.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a discussion budget: when evidence already points to a clear action or reversible experiment, cap deliberation time and move to execution.
failure_modes.source_quote: Avoid when teams are using more discussion to postpone hard calls that already have enough evidence for a decision or a reversible experiment.

premortem_questions.description: What risk, dissent, or upward challenge is still not being said because of status or fear?
premortem_questions.source_quote: formal status discourages upward challenge despite visible risk

premortem_questions.description: Are decision rights and accountability clear enough that candor can actually change the outcome?
premortem_questions.source_quote: ownership, standards, or decision rights vague

premortem_questions.description: Would the next best move be more challenge or simply making the call now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: postpone hard calls that already have enough evidence

heuristics.description: Define psychological safety as candid challenge in service of better decisions, not as comfort.
heuristics.source_quote: candid truth in service of better decisions

heuristics.description: Pair openness with clear ownership and decision rights so surfaced problems can actually be corrected.
heuristics.source_quote: ownership, standards, or decision rights

heuristics.description: Use safety to widen the set of real perspectives, not to suppress standards or delay reversible action.
heuristics.source_quote: diverse set of opinions

danger_when: Psychological safety is being redefined as comfort, harmony, or protection from challenge rather than candid truth in service of better decisions.
The team already has enough evidence for a decision or a reversible experiment, and more discussion would mainly postpone commitment.
psychological-safety.name: Psychological Safety

select_when: The explanation sounds polished, but there are signs the reasoning may be a post-hoc defense rather than the real cause of the decision.
A team is protecting a conclusion by cleaning up the story instead of reopening whether the thesis is actually sound.
The gap between action and stated reasoning needs to be surfaced before more commitments are made.
The next intervention should separate genuine analysis from press-secretary storytelling.

failure_modes.mode: The mind often acts like a press secretary for decisions already made.
failure_modes.description: Rationalization turns analysis into a press-secretary function that explains the decision after the fact rather than testing it beforehand.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require the reasoning chain to be written before the decision is announced: if the analysis was constructed after the conclusion, flag it as post-hoc justification.
failure_modes.source_quote: The mind often acts like a press secretary for decisions already made.

failure_modes.mode: Danger When Story Cleanup Replaces Honest Reasoning
failure_modes.description: The model fails when a polished story hides the gap between the real motive and the stated reason.
failure_modes.mitigation: Appoint a devil's advocate to surface the gap between stated reason and likely real motive; if the two diverge, the stated reason is a rationalization.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Story Cleanup Replaces Honest Reasoning

failure_modes.mode: self-scrutiny must improve the next choice, not become an end in itself
failure_modes.description: Anti-rationalization work becomes counterproductive when it collapses into endless motive analysis with no decision correction.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a reflection time limit: once rationalization has been named, pivot to the decision correction within one cycle rather than spiraling into endless motive archaeology.
failure_modes.source_quote: self-scrutiny must improve the next choice, not become an end in itself

premortem_questions.description: What would we say if we had to explain the real reason rather than the respectable reason?
premortem_questions.source_quote: the respectable reason often comes later

premortem_questions.description: What evidence would make us reopen the conclusion instead of improving the story around it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: the mind defends the conclusion instead of revisiting it

premortem_questions.description: If an outsider watched the action before hearing our explanation, what motive would they infer?
premortem_questions.source_quote: actions reveal motives more clearly than after-the-fact stories

heuristics.description: Separate the actual decision path from the later explanation before judging whether the reasoning was sound.
heuristics.source_quote: separate the decision from the explanation for the decision

heuristics.description: Treat a polished narrative as a signal to inspect what is being protected, not as proof that the reasoning was strong.
heuristics.source_quote: rationalization is often a defense mechanism

heuristics.description: Use anti-rationalization pressure to change the next decision, not to perform insightfulness.
heuristics.source_quote: self-scrutiny must improve the next choice

danger_when: The group is already candid about uncertainty and trade-offs, and further anti-rationalization pressure would just create performative self-doubt.
The situation requires fast containment or execution and the team is about to turn anti-rationalization work into endless motive analysis.
rationalization.name: Rationalization

select_when: The problem could be approached through diagnosis, creative exploration, adversarial critique, or execution planning, and choosing the wrong path would materially distort the work.
The cost of selecting the wrong reasoning mode is high because it would waste cycles, hide key assumptions, or bias the answer early.
The team needs to name the current stage of work before mixing goals, diagnosis, design, and doing into one blurred process.
The first framing may be anchoring the method more strongly than the evidence justifies.

failure_modes.mode: Faulty Pattern Recognition (Substitution Bias)
failure_modes.description: Faulty pattern recognition can route a novel problem into a familiar frame and produce a disastrously wrong solution path.
failure_modes.mitigation: Before routing to a familiar method, check structural fit: list three features the problem shares with the template and one it does not, and judge whether the mismatch is load-bearing.
failure_modes.source_quote: Faulty Pattern Recognition (Substitution Bias)

failure_modes.mode: Mode-Selection Overhead
failure_modes.description: Mode-selection overhead can become a tax if the team keeps debating method instead of advancing the next discriminating test.
failure_modes.mitigation: Timebox mode selection: agree on the reasoning approach within a fixed window, then commit and advance to the next discriminating test rather than debating method indefinitely.
failure_modes.source_quote: Mode-Selection Overhead

failure_modes.mode: Anchored Routing
failure_modes.description: Anchored routing can lock the mode choice to the first framing even after contradictory evidence appears.
failure_modes.mitigation: Build in a mode-change trigger: define in advance what evidence would cause the team to switch reasoning modes, so anchoring on the first frame cannot survive contradictory data.
failure_modes.source_quote: Anchored Routing

premortem_questions.description: What are the assumptions embedded in the framework I am currently using?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What are the assumptions embedded in the framework I am currently using?

premortem_questions.description: If this model is wrong, what genuinely novel problem structure might I be facing?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If this model is wrong, what genuinely novel problem structure might I be facing?

premortem_questions.description: What specific output is needed from this work to solve the problem?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What specific output is needed from this work to solve the problem?

heuristics.description: Test multiple frames before locking the route so the first usable model does not become the only one considered.
heuristics.source_quote: Testing Multiple Frames

heuristics.description: Enforce thesis, antithesis, synthesis so routing is pressured by challenge rather than agreement.
heuristics.source_quote: Dialectic Standard

heuristics.description: Define the problem context clearly before diving into tools, data, or deep analysis.
heuristics.source_quote: Enforce the Seven-Step Process

danger_when: Routing debate itself is becoming overhead and the team cannot name the next discriminating question or experiment.
A proven playbook already fits the situation and no meaningful alternative mode is actually in contention.
The router is being used as an excuse for premature complexity instead of clarifying the next reasoning move.
reasoning-mode-router.name: Reasoning Mode Router
reframing_when: The question defaults to analytical reasoning when the situation actually calls for intuitive, creative, or dialectical thinking — the reasoning mode is mismatched to the problem type.
The question applies a convergent reasoning mode (narrowing to one answer) when the problem is still in a divergent phase (generating possibilities) or vice versa.
The question treats all problems as the same type — optimization, decision, exploration — when recognizing the problem type would reveal that a completely different reasoning approach is needed.
The question has been cycling through variations of the same approach because the reasoning mode is locked and no one has asked whether the mode itself is appropriate for the problem structure.
reasoning-mode-router.reframing_move: Before continuing in the current reasoning mode, ask: 'What type of problem is this actually, and does our current reasoning mode match?' — the mode-routing reframe treats the choice of reasoning strategy as a first-class decision rather than an unconscious default.

select_when: Trust or relationship depth must be built before a difficult ask, negotiation, or sale can be evaluated seriously.
The value offered first is real, relevant, and costly enough to signal sincere investment rather than manipulation theater.
The goal is durable goodwill and mutual commitment instead of a one-off extraction of compliance.
A recommendation needs to separate relationship-building moves from psychological pressure that could bypass informed evaluation.

failure_modes.mode: Danger when reciprocity pressure creates obligation without informed evaluation
failure_modes.description: A team can mistake reciprocity pressure for genuine agreement and push people toward yes before they have judged the merits.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate social pressure from judgment: after any reciprocity-driven exchange, ask whether the person would still agree if the prior favor had never occurred.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when reciprocity pressure creates obligation without informed evaluation

failure_modes.mode: you must do it faultlessly and without fail-on time, no excuses
failure_modes.description: Goodwill can be destroyed quickly if early promises or professionalism signals are not delivered faultlessly.
failure_modes.mitigation: Deliver on promises with visible reliability: treat early commitments as trust-building investments whose failure cost is disproportionately high.
failure_modes.source_quote: you must do it faultlessly and without fail-on time, no excuses

failure_modes.mode: The persuasive power of reciprocity can be leveraged to frame a situation deceptively.
failure_modes.description: Relationship-building can slide into manipulation if influence work is not undertaken planfully and transparently.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit influence tactics for transparency: before deploying reciprocity as a persuasion strategy, check whether the approach would survive public disclosure without damaging trust.
failure_modes.source_quote: The persuasive power of reciprocity can be leveraged to frame a situation deceptively.

premortem_questions.description: If the other side accepts now, is the acceptance coming from thoughtful analysis or from the pressure to reciprocate?
premortem_questions.source_quote: is their acceptance based on thoughtful, deliberate analysis of the outcomes (System 2), or merely on the psychological pressure to reciprocate

premortem_questions.description: Have we actually delivered every promise on time, or are we leaning on past goodwill to excuse current defects?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Have we delivered every single thing we promised to do for the client so far, on time and without fail

premortem_questions.description: Are we trying to create a reciprocal relationship or just a compliant transaction?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Are we confusing the goal of generating a sale with the deeper goal of creating a reciprocal relationship

heuristics.description: Lead with real value before asking for commitment so the exchange is anchored in usefulness rather than in obligation theater.
heuristics.source_quote: The more value you give, the more value you get

heuristics.description: Require skin in the game when seeking expert help so reciprocal effort is tied to serious problem ownership.
heuristics.source_quote: you should demonstrate that you have already invested time, energy, and money into solving the problem

heuristics.description: Keep the relationship authentic and explicit enough that reciprocity supports trust instead of bypassing judgment.
heuristics.source_quote: Act Authentically and Transparently

danger_when: Gifts, favors, or access are becoming a distraction from the substance, economics, or ethics of the underlying decision.
The model is being used to chase assent without ensuring the other side has actually judged the merits with deliberative thought.
reciprocity-principle.name: Reciprocity Principle

select_when: Standing still effectively means losing ground because rivals, substitutes, or environmental pressures are improving at the same time.
A team must distinguish maintenance effort from real advantage and decide where continuous adaptation is non-optional.
Competitive moves, regulation, or technology shifts are changing the baseline faster than annual planning or static playbooks can absorb.
The work needs an anti-complacency frame that links ongoing adaptation to feedback rather than to one-off heroics.

failure_modes.mode: effort does not guarantee progress; intense effort (running) is merely the cost of maintaining a relative status
failure_modes.description: Teams can mistake heavy effort for real progress even when rivals' simultaneous adaptation is canceling out the gain.
failure_modes.mitigation: Measure progress against the competitive landscape, not against your own baseline: ask whether the gap to rivals has actually widened, not just whether internal metrics improved.
failure_modes.source_quote: effort does not guarantee progress; intense effort (running) is merely the cost of maintaining a relative status

failure_modes.mode: Continuous adaptation pressure can mutate into analysis paralysis or feature creep when the team nev...
failure_modes.description: Continuous adaptation pressure can mutate into analysis paralysis or feature creep when the team never stabilizes on the real problem.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a stability checkpoint: after each adaptation cycle, decide whether to lock the current approach and exploit it or continue adapting, to prevent perpetual churn.
failure_modes.source_quote: The pursuit of continuous adaptation risks getting mired in endless questioning, leading to analysis paralysis rather than effective action.

failure_modes.mode: Combating Entrenched Thinking
failure_modes.description: Old successful mental models can survive too long in a changing environment and become the reason adaptation fails.
failure_modes.mitigation: Schedule regular model retirement reviews: audit which legacy assumptions and playbooks have been outrun by environmental change and retire them before they cause adaptation failure.
failure_modes.source_quote: Combating Entrenched Thinking

premortem_questions.description: What would need to be true for this idea to work in a baseline that is still moving under us?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would need to be true for this idea to work?

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough if adaptation pressure drives us below acceptable safety thresholds?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.description: What are we actually trying to say here, and what is the single purpose of this adaptation?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What am I actually trying to say here? What is the single purpose of this adaptation?

heuristics.description: Treat anti-complacency as an execution discipline, not just as a motivational slogan.
heuristics.source_quote: focusing on implementation disciplines (execution) to bridge the gap

heuristics.description: Use attack-team and defense-team style simulation to anticipate countermoves instead of assuming the environment stays still.
heuristics.source_quote: breaking a team into an attack and a defense team

heuristics.description: Build continuous feedback loops so adaptation is guided by learning rather than by panic or habit.
heuristics.source_quote: Implementing Continuous Feedback Loops

danger_when: The pressure to keep running is pushing the team into analysis paralysis, feature creep, or action-first thrash without clear problem definition.
The model is being used to excuse speed without naming the safety thresholds, execution limits, or cognitive load that keep adaptation sane.
red-queen-effect.name: Red Queen Effect

select_when: Single-point failure is unacceptable and a backup path would materially change survival or recovery quality.
Resilience, recall, or continuity matter more than lean elegance.
One broken component, missed memory, or absent viewpoint would otherwise cripple the system.

failure_modes.mode: The Redundancy Effect
failure_modes.description: Repeated content can increase cognitive load when the duplication adds no real protection or clarification.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit each redundant element for marginal value: if duplication adds no new protection, clarity, or resilience, remove it to reduce cognitive load.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Redundancy Effect

failure_modes.mode: An over-reliance on seeking redundant analyses or perspectives can lead to getting mired in analysis paralysis
failure_modes.description: Teams can keep accumulating backup analyses or perspectives until the work stalls into analysis paralysis.
