failure_modes.mode: The first dominant frame can quietly take over the whole discussion when no one is deliberately swit...
failure_modes.description: The first dominant frame can quietly take over the whole discussion when no one is deliberately switching modes.
failure_modes.mitigation: Appoint a mode referee who actively interrupts when the discussion collapses back into the dominant frame without completing the other perspectives.
failure_modes.source_quote: Best used when the same group must examine one issue from several angles without mistaking the first dominant frame for the whole problem.

premortem_questions.description: Which cognitive mode is missing right now: evidence, risk, creativity, synthesis, or another needed lens?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when a team needs to separate competing modes of thought.

premortem_questions.description: Are we rotating perspective standards, or only taking turns defending the same underlying frame?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger when the roles become performative labels.

premortem_questions.description: What information sits outside the current dominant hat and still needs to be checked before we move on?
premortem_questions.source_quote: The structure combats this by forcing the consideration of information beyond the immediate view.

heuristics.description: Separate the thinking modes so creativity, evidence review, and risk pressure do not interfere with each other in the same moment.
heuristics.source_quote: Most useful when a team needs to separate competing modes of thought.

heuristics.description: Rotate perspectives deliberately instead of letting the first dominant frame stand in for the whole issue.
heuristics.source_quote: Best used when the same group must examine one issue from several angles.

heuristics.description: Use the structure as temporary scaffolding that reduces cognitive load while the group does real perspective switching work.
heuristics.source_quote: This model acts as cognitive scaffolding.

danger_when: The roles are becoming performative labels and participants have stopped doing the real cognitive work of switching standards and evidence.
The frame rotation is adding theater without improving what gets checked, challenged, or synthesized.
six-thinking-hats.name: Six Thinking Hats
reframing_when: The question is stuck in one thinking mode — purely analytical, purely emotional, or purely creative — when deliberately switching modes would reveal aspects of the problem invisible from the current hat.
The question frames the situation as requiring a single type of judgment ('what are the facts?', 'what feels right?', 'what is creative?') when the problem actually requires all modes in sequence.
The question collapses options prematurely because the group or individual is arguing from mixed modes simultaneously — facts, feelings, and risks tangled together instead of examined separately.
The question assumes the current framing is complete when a systematic mode rotation would reveal that an entire dimension (emotional, risk, creative, procedural) has been left unexamined.
six-thinking-hats.reframing_move: Before settling on the current analysis mode, ask: 'Which thinking hat are we wearing right now, and what would we see if we deliberately switched to the hat we have been avoiding?' — the mode-rotation reframe forces systematic coverage of dimensions the current frame suppresses.

select_when: A decision is moving quickly because adoption, consensus, or endorsement is being treated as evidence even though the underlying mechanism is hard to inspect directly.
The team needs to test whether admired adopters are actually structurally similar to the current case before borrowing their prestige.
A recommendation may be suppressing dissent because high-status approval is making the group converge too early.
The next move should separate credibility acceleration from truth by asking what causal evidence still matters even if the crowd is impressed.

failure_modes.mode: Teams can borrow prestige from irrelevant high-status adopters whose context, incentives, or constra...
failure_modes.description: Teams can borrow prestige from irrelevant high-status adopters whose context, incentives, or constraints do not match the current decision.
failure_modes.mitigation: Verify relevance before borrowing credibility: check whether the cited adopter's context, scale, and constraints actually match the decision at hand.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when teams borrow prestige from irrelevant high-status adopters whose context, incentives, or constraints do not match the current decision.

failure_modes.mode: Social proof can become a substitute for mechanism, evidence quality, or customer truth instead of a...
failure_modes.description: Social proof can become a substitute for mechanism, evidence quality, or customer truth instead of a credibility accelerant.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require mechanism alongside consensus: social proof should accelerate trust, not replace evidence about why the approach works.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when Social Proof is used as a substitute for mechanism, evidence quality, or customer truth instead of as a credibility accelerant.

failure_modes.mode: groupthink
failure_modes.description: Consensus can harden into groupthink that suppresses dissent and carries the team along despite private doubts.
failure_modes.mitigation: Protect dissent channels: create anonymous or structured input rounds so individual doubts survive the social pressure to conform.
failure_modes.source_quote: groupthink

failure_modes.mode: highly connected, homogeneous groups are especially vulnerable to self-reinforcing error cascades
failure_modes.description: A self-selecting consensus pool can make agreement look like proof even when the community has filtered out disconfirming evidence in advance.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit the consensus pool for selection bias: check whether the agreeing community filtered out disconfirming voices or evidence before treating consensus as proof.
failure_modes.source_quote: highly connected, homogeneous groups are especially vulnerable to self-reinforcing error cascades

failure_modes.mode: Inaction by others can serve as social proof that doing nothing is correct — when nobody raises an i...
failure_modes.description: Inaction by others can serve as social proof that doing nothing is correct — when nobody raises an issue, the absence of challenge suppresses necessary checks and creates diffusion of responsibility.
failure_modes.mitigation: Counter bystander inertia: assign named owners for specific risk checks so silence cannot serve as implicit endorsement of the status quo.
failure_modes.source_quote: When you see other people doing nothing, your brain interprets inaction as social proof that doing nothing is the correct thing to do

premortem_questions.description: Which admired adopters actually look structurally similar to us, and which endorsements are just prestige leakage?
premortem_questions.source_quote: look structurally similar to the audience

premortem_questions.description: If we removed the social proof entirely, what mechanism or evidence would still justify this decision?
premortem_questions.source_quote: substitute for mechanism, evidence quality, or customer truth

premortem_questions.description: What dissenting view is currently being muted because consensus is moving faster than analysis?
premortem_questions.source_quote: discouraging members who hold dissenting opinions

premortem_questions.description: Is this consensus drawing from a structurally representative sample, or from a community that mostly selects for agreement with itself?
premortem_questions.source_quote: homogeneous groups are especially vulnerable to self-reinforcing error cascades

premortem_questions.description: Is this path unchallenged because it was vetted, or because no one wanted to be the first to speak up?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Each person's inaction reinforced everyone else's inaction

heuristics.description: Use social proof as a credibility accelerant only after checking whether the endorsers share the audience's real context and incentives.
heuristics.source_quote: credibility accelerant

heuristics.description: Solicit independent opinions before group discussion so high-status contagion does not settle the answer too early.
heuristics.source_quote: Solicit independent opinions and rationales before group discussion

heuristics.description: Require a causal explanation for copied behavior so the team does not mistake correlation for why success happened.
heuristics.source_quote: copy practices that are merely correlational with success, rather than causal

danger_when: The main problem is already a direct evidence-quality or mechanism question and adding more social validation would only distract from the core analysis.
The audience already has context-matched proof and the real bottleneck is execution rather than deciding whether outside validation is trustworthy.
social-proof.name: Social Proof

select_when: Leverage comes from going deeper rather than wider, and broad scope is diluting attention.
Trust, pricing power, or decision authority increase when others know exactly what the team is best at.
The problem rewards repeatable expertise inside a narrow class of work.
The current scope is scattering effort across too many low-value tasks to build a defensible edge.

failure_modes.mode: Maslow's Hammer
failure_modes.description: The hammer problem appears when teams force a familiar specialty frame onto novel work.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test frame fit before applying the specialty lens: list what the familiar framework explains and what it leaves out, and switch tools when the gap is material.
failure_modes.source_quote: Maslow's Hammer

failure_modes.mode: Illusion of Understanding
failure_modes.description: Overconfidence grows when experts assume deep knowledge in one lane is enough for the whole problem.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pair deep expertise with structured cross-domain challenge: require specialists to present their analysis to someone from an adjacent discipline who can surface blind spots.
failure_modes.source_quote: Illusion of Understanding

failure_modes.mode: Focus Becomes Identity Lock-In
failure_modes.description: Focus can become identity lock-in that resists adjacent skills or broader synthesis when the situation changes.
failure_modes.mitigation: Build identity flexibility: encourage experts to define themselves by the problems they solve, not the tools they use, so they can adopt new methods when the situation demands it.
failure_modes.source_quote: Focus Becomes Identity Lock-In

premortem_questions.description: Are we going deeper on the right narrow problem, or just protecting an old identity?
premortem_questions.source_quote: going deeper rather than wider

premortem_questions.description: What different frame from another discipline would expose what our specialty misses?
premortem_questions.source_quote: multiple frameworks

premortem_questions.description: What would we have to believe for this specialized scope to remain the highest-leverage lane?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

heuristics.description: Keep the single focus principle visible and remove work that does not support it.
heuristics.source_quote: Single Focus Principle

heuristics.description: Do not boil the ocean; gather only enough facts to prove or disprove the key thesis.
heuristics.source_quote: Don't boil the ocean

heuristics.description: Define the specialty as one specific problem for one specific audience in one specific way.
heuristics.source_quote: 1 Specific Problem, for 1 Specific Type of Person, in 1 Specific Way

danger_when: Protecting specialist identity matters more than noticing the need for adjacent skills or broader synthesis.
The model is being used like a hammer so every new problem is forced into the same familiar frame.
specialization.name: Specialization

select_when: The case feels intuitive, but the cost of being wrong is high enough that distributions, base rates, or evidence quality must be checked.
Tool choice matters as much as analysis quality because the team must decide between causal analysis, forecasting, or simulation.
The recommendation should reflect uncertainty ranges and evidence strength rather than a heroic point estimate.
The analysis needs disciplined structure before anyone starts building a more elaborate model.

failure_modes.mode: wade prematurely into data without doing the thinking
failure_modes.description: Teams can jump into complex models before clarifying the question or hypothesis they need to test.
failure_modes.mitigation: Start with the question, not the model: clarify what hypothesis needs testing and what decision the analysis must inform before choosing a statistical approach.
failure_modes.source_quote: wade prematurely into data without doing the thinking

failure_modes.mode: statistical rigor is faked rather than earned
failure_modes.description: Statistical rigor can be faked with charts and model outputs that hide weak assumptions.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit assumptions behind every output: require analysts to surface and justify the key assumptions driving their model before presenting results.
failure_modes.source_quote: statistical rigor is faked rather than earned

failure_modes.mode: Focusing on the Mean Outcome
failure_modes.description: Point estimates can hide the downside when analysts overweight the base case and underweight tails.
failure_modes.mitigation: Report the full distribution: show tails, confidence intervals, and sensitivity to key assumptions alongside any point estimate.
failure_modes.source_quote: Focusing on the Mean Outcome

premortem_questions.description: Are we solving a causal question, a forecasting question, or a simulation question?
premortem_questions.source_quote: understand causation or predict a future state

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.description: What would we have to believe for this model structure to be the right one?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

heuristics.description: Start with simple summary statistics before building anything complicated.
heuristics.source_quote: Starting with Simple Statistics

heuristics.description: Frame a clear hypothesis before collecting more data or modeling.
heuristics.source_quote: Hypothesis-Driven Problem Solving

heuristics.description: Use the outside view and reference classes to discipline the inside view.
heuristics.source_quote: Outside View and Reference Class Forecasting

danger_when: Quantitative language is being used to project certainty without checking assumptions, data quality, or structural fit.
The team is wading into complex analysis before clarifying the question, hypothesis, or output the analysis must support.
statistical-discipline.name: Statistical Discipline

select_when: The task is prediction under uncertainty and the organization can learn from enough observations to update performance meaningfully.
The team must balance model complexity, generalization, and decision usefulness rather than chase fit on one sample.
Forecasting quality or probabilistic calibration matters more than elegant intuition or explanation alone.
Before building a model, the team can answer whether it is trying to understand causation or predict a state of the world.

failure_modes.mode: Danger of Model Worship
failure_modes.description: Model worship can push the team into prediction machinery even when the target is unclear, the data is thin, or causal understanding matters more.
failure_modes.mitigation: Clarify the objective before building the model: check whether the task needs prediction, causal explanation, or decision support, and choose the method accordingly.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger of Model Worship

failure_modes.mode: Premature Analysis
failure_modes.description: Premature analysis can waste effort by fitting models before the problem structure, objective, or testable hypotheses are clear.
failure_modes.mitigation: Validate before trusting: require out-of-sample testing and check whether the model's performance holds on data it has never seen before fitting to production use.
failure_modes.source_quote: Premature Analysis

failure_modes.mode: Ignoring the Extremes
failure_modes.description: Distribution neglect can make a model look solid while hiding the tail outcomes or censored failures that matter most.
failure_modes.mitigation: Inspect the tails: report model performance on extreme cases and censored outcomes, not just overall accuracy, to catch distribution neglect.
failure_modes.source_quote: Ignoring the Extremes

premortem_questions.description: Are you primarily trying to understand the drivers of causation, or are you primarily trying to predict a state of the world?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Are you primarily trying to understand the drivers of causation, or are you primarily trying to predict a state of the world?

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.description: What outside-view baseline or reference class should discipline this model before we trust its fit on our local sample?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Anchor to the Outside View

heuristics.description: Decide up front whether the task is causation or prediction so the model class matches the real objective.
heuristics.source_quote: Prediction vs. Causation Diagnostic

heuristics.description: Start with scoping reconnaissance and simple summary structure before moving to heavy learning machinery.
heuristics.source_quote: Scoping Reconnaissance

heuristics.description: Use simulations or scenario analysis to test how the model behaves across plausible states instead of trusting one fitted view.
heuristics.source_quote: Simulation and Scenario Analysis

danger_when: Model worship is treating statistical learning as a universal answer even though the target is poorly defined, the data is thin, or causal understanding matters more than prediction.
The analysis is being started before the problem structure, objective, or testable hypotheses are clear.
statistical-learning-theory.name: Statistical Learning Theory

select_when: Uncertainty is quantifiable enough that ranges, distributions, or sampling logic can improve the decision.
The team needs to distinguish signal from noise rather than argue from anecdotes, vivid cases, or gut feel.
A problem benefits from calibrated estimation, comparison, or inference instead of loose narrative certainty.
Background rates, sampling structure, or distribution shape matter more than one compelling example.

failure_modes.mode: Base-Rate Neglect
failure_modes.description: Base-rate neglect can let vivid case details overwhelm the statistical background information that should anchor the judgment.
failure_modes.mitigation: Anchor to base rates first: before processing vivid case details, look up the statistical background frequency and use it as the starting point for judgment.
failure_modes.source_quote: Base-Rate Neglect

failure_modes.mode: statistical language gets used decoratively
failure_modes.description: Decorative statistical language can hide weak data quality, weak assumptions, or sampling problems.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit data quality behind statistical claims: check sample size, sampling method, and measurement validity before trusting any reported statistic.
failure_modes.source_quote: statistical language gets used decoratively

failure_modes.mode: placing too much emphasis on the mean outcome
failure_modes.description: Overweighting the mean can hide extreme-outcome risk in distributions where tails matter more than the average case.
failure_modes.mitigation: Report distributional shape, not just the mean: show the tails and spread of outcomes when the extremes carry more decision weight than the average.
failure_modes.source_quote: placing too much emphasis on the mean outcome

premortem_questions.description: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.description: What assumption about the sample, baseline, or distribution would make this conclusion collapse first?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Systematic Risk Analysis (Knowns & Unknowns)

heuristics.description: Anchor to base rates and reference classes before adding the vivid details of the current case.
heuristics.source_quote: reference classes

heuristics.description: Use simulations or scenario analysis when the full outcome range matters more than one average estimate.
heuristics.source_quote: simulations are valuable tools for highlighting the range of possible outcomes

heuristics.description: Treat good statistical judgment as a process for making good decisions consistently, not as a guarantee of a good outcome every time.
heuristics.source_quote: good decisions ... the best way to achieve good outcomes in the long run

danger_when: Statistical language is being used decoratively and no one has checked whether the data quality, assumptions, or sample structure justify the conclusion.
The team is leaning on the mean outcome while ignoring extreme outcomes, missingness, or failure distributions that matter more to the decision.
statistics-concepts.name: Statistics Concepts

select_when: An incumbent option may be surviving because changing feels costly rather than because it is actually superior.
Defaults, routines, or prior commitments are shaping judgment quietly and the team needs to separate real switching costs from inertia.
A proposal to stay the course sounds prudent but may be disguising loss aversion, sunk costs, or unchallenged precedent.

failure_modes.mode: The Bias Blind Spot and Intellectual Arrogance
failure_modes.description: Bias blind spot and intellectual arrogance can make the current path feel safer simply because the team can defend it more fluently.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test the default against a clean-sheet alternative: ask what the team would choose if starting from scratch, and explain any gap between that answer and the current path.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Bias Blind Spot and Intellectual Arrogance

failure_modes.mode: Illusion of Knowing
failure_modes.description: Illusion of knowing can make familiarity look like competence and suppress fresh investigation.
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge fluency with fresh investigation: when defending the status quo feels easy, that comfort may be familiarity rather than evidence — require new data before recommitting.
failure_modes.source_quote: Illusion of Knowing

failure_modes.mode: Overoptimism and Planning Fallacy
failure_modes.description: Overoptimism can keep the default plan alive even when a conjunctive chain makes success much less likely than it feels.
failure_modes.mitigation: Stress-test the default plan's conjunctive assumptions: check whether staying the course requires too many things to go right simultaneously, and flag compounding risk.
failure_modes.source_quote: Overoptimism and Planning Fallacy

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.description: What information am I currently ignoring or omitting because it contradicts my desired outcome or belief?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What information am I currently ignoring or omitting because it contradicts my desired outcome or belief?

premortem_questions.description: What would have to be true to accept an alternative strategy?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would have to be true to accept an alternative strategy?

heuristics.description: Use constructive confrontation to spell out the assumptions protecting the current default.
heuristics.source_quote: What Would You Have to Believe?

heuristics.description: Reduce choice overload with the less frame when decision paralysis is quietly preserving the default.
heuristics.source_quote: The Less Frame

heuristics.description: Use radical open-mindedness and perspective taking to pressure-test whether staying put is prudence or inertia.
heuristics.source_quote: Radical Open-Mindedness

danger_when: Every legacy choice is being treated as bad simply because it is old, rather than asking whether the current default still outperforms the alternatives.
The desire to break inertia is turning into novelty-seeking change without enough baseline evidence that the new option is actually better.
status-quo-bias.name: Status Quo Bias
reframing_when: The question asks how to improve the current approach without considering whether the current approach should exist at all — the status quo is an invisible assumption.
The question frames staying the course as prudent when it may be disguising loss aversion, sunk costs, or unchallenged precedent.
The question asks 'should we change?' when the more diagnostic question is 'is the current path surviving because it is actually superior, or because changing feels costly?'
The question inherits defaults from organizational habit, executive vision, or prior decisions without testing whether those defaults still serve the stated goal.
status-quo-bias.reframing_move: Apply the WTWHTB test to the default: ask 'what would you have to believe for the current path to be the right one?' — if the assumptions behind the status quo don't survive scrutiny, the question should be about which alternative to pursue, not whether to change.

select_when: Immediate immersion is obscuring the structure of the problem and a deliberate pause would likely improve the next move.
The work is drifting into detail, motion, or urgency without a clear articulation of the core point or governing purpose.
A high-stakes decision needs the thinker to shift from intuitive reaction toward more deliberate analysis.
The team needs to recheck alignment with the bigger picture before more effort is spent on a local branch.

failure_modes.mode: Reflection can become a hiding place that postpones commitment, trade-offs, or the next concrete mov...
failure_modes.description: Reflection can become a hiding place that postpones commitment, trade-offs, or the next concrete move.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a reflection deadline: agree in advance on when the pause must produce a concrete next action, trade-off, or commitment.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When the Pause Becomes a Hiding Place: The model fails when reflection is used to postpone commitment, avoid trade-offs, or keep options abstract rather than to improve the next concrete move.

failure_modes.mode: Teams can boil the ocean when stepping back turns into over-comprehensiveness instead of sharpening ...
failure_modes.description: Teams can boil the ocean when stepping back turns into over-comprehensiveness instead of sharpening what matters.
failure_modes.mitigation: Scope the step-back: define what question the pause is meant to answer, and stop widening the lens once that question is resolved.
failure_modes.source_quote: An anti-pattern in research where one loses the big picture and gathers an overwhelming amount of data just for the sake of comprehensiveness.

failure_modes.mode: Perfectionism can turn the pause into analysis paralysis rather than a better-sequenced action path.
failure_modes.description: Perfectionism can turn the pause into analysis paralysis rather than a better-sequenced action path.
failure_modes.mitigation: Convert insight to action: after stepping back, require the first output to be a sequenced action plan, not more analysis.
failure_modes.source_quote: For those prone to perfectionism, stepping back can lead to over-analyzing everything to achieve perfection, which stymies important action.

premortem_questions.description: What's the one-day answer?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What’s the one-day answer?

premortem_questions.description: What is the core point, and why does it matter?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What’s your main (core) point? Why does it matter?

premortem_questions.description: Is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?

heuristics.description: Use the pause to recover the big picture and governing purpose before spending more effort on local detail.
heuristics.source_quote: The step back forces the articulation of the main goal or core message, ensuring every subsequent action is aligned with a single purpose.

heuristics.description: Switch from intuitive reaction to deliberate framing before committing to a plan in high-stakes or ambiguous situations.
heuristics.source_quote: By stepping back, one consciously engages the rational mind ... rather than defaulting to habitual, bias-prone shortcuts.

heuristics.description: Reduce the problem into logical pieces only after the needed output is clear enough that the extra analysis will serve a real decision.
heuristics.source_quote: work plans are crystal clear about what output is needed to solve the problem before modeling or analysis begins.

heuristics.description: Deliberately disconnect from stress-inducing noise long enough for the pause to restore framing, not just absorb more agitation.
heuristics.source_quote: consciously engages the rational mind

danger_when: The pause is becoming a hiding place for avoiding commitment, trade-offs, or concrete next steps.
The team is already clear on the problem and now needs execution rather than another round of reflective abstraction.
step-back.name: Step Back
reframing_when: The question is stuck at the detail or execution level when the real uncertainty is at the strategy or purpose level — stepping up one level of abstraction would reveal a different question.
The question assumes the goal is correct and asks about tactics, but the goal itself hasn't been examined — 'How does what you're doing solve the problem?' has not been asked.
The question was formulated reactively in response to an immediate stimulus and a deliberate pause would reframe it toward the underlying structural issue.
step-back.reframing_move: Step up one level: before answering 'how', ask 'what is the actual problem we are trying to solve, and is this question addressing it or addressing a symptom?' — the meta-question often displaces the original.

select_when: Information is available but still fails to feel consequential, memorable, or decision-relevant to the audience.
An abstract service, vision, or analytical conclusion needs to become concrete enough for people to care and act.
Data needs a causal or human arc so listeners can retain the signal instead of only hearing disconnected points.

failure_modes.mode: A vivid story can make weak causal logic feel persuasive enough to escape scrutiny.
failure_modes.description: A vivid story can make weak causal logic feel persuasive enough to escape scrutiny.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate narrative appeal from causal logic: after hearing the story, extract the implied causal chain and test each link against evidence independently.
failure_modes.source_quote: storytelling-frameworks conflicts with first-principles-thinking when a compelling narrative arc substitutes for base-rate reasoning

failure_modes.mode: Narrative polish can smuggle subjectivity into places that actually need formal reasoning and eviden...
failure_modes.description: Narrative polish can smuggle subjectivity into places that actually need formal reasoning and evidence.
failure_modes.mitigation: Flag where narrative replaces evidence: identify the specific points where the story asserts a claim without data, and require those gaps to be filled before the story drives a decision.
failure_modes.source_quote: storytelling-frameworks conflicts with formal-reasoning (formal reasoning) when formal Reasoning relies on strict logic and evidence, potentially opposing the subjective and interpretive nature of storytelling.

failure_modes.mode: Abstract information can remain inert if no narrative frame makes it matter to the audience.
failure_modes.description: Abstract information can remain inert if no narrative frame makes it matter to the audience.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use narrative to make abstract evidence concrete: when data exists but fails to motivate action, wrap it in a story frame that preserves accuracy while adding emotional salience.
failure_modes.source_quote: Storytelling frameworks are strongest when information is available, yet still fails to feel consequential, memorable, or decision-relevant to the audience.

premortem_questions.description: What crucial assumption is this story asking the audience to accept without showing its evidentiary basis?
premortem_questions.source_quote: a compelling narrative arc substitutes for base-rate reasoning

premortem_questions.description: What detail makes this message concrete enough to feel real instead of merely conceptual?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Storytelling helps make abstract ideas concrete.

premortem_questions.description: If we removed the story, what decision-relevant insight would still remain true?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Stories help connect the audience to necessary data with a handful of points

heuristics.description: Use story to make an abstract problem concrete, not to bypass logic or evidence.
heuristics.source_quote: Storytelling helps make abstract ideas concrete.

heuristics.description: Anchor the narrative to the one consequence or insight the audience must remember.
heuristics.source_quote: The Rule of Three/The Core Message

heuristics.description: Pair narrative framing with explicit evidence checks when the story implies causal certainty.
heuristics.source_quote: formal Reasoning relies on strict logic and evidence

danger_when: A compelling narrative arc is substituting for first-principles reconstruction or evidence checks.
The task is already a tight logical proof and extra narrative dressing would dilute precision.
storytelling-frameworks.name: Storytelling Frameworks

select_when: A team is persisting mainly because of prior investment, history, or identity rather than because the future economics still justify recommitment.
The real decision is whether we would start again today from current facts, not whether we can emotionally justify past effort.
A project, relationship, or investment needs to be reevaluated from prospective value instead of from already-incurred cost.
The next useful move is to separate what is irrecoverable from what still has live future upside.

failure_modes.mode: prior investment is used to justify continued exposure
failure_modes.description: Teams keep going because of prior investment even when the future case no longer justifies recommitment.
failure_modes.mitigation: Evaluate only the forward case: strip past investment from the analysis and ask whether the project would be funded from scratch given today's information.
failure_modes.source_quote: prior investment is used to justify continued exposure

failure_modes.mode: evaluate the stock based on its future potential
failure_modes.description: The model fails when history is treated as a reason to continue instead of as an irrecoverable fact that should be ignored for forward choice.
failure_modes.mitigation: Make the exit criteria explicit before starting: define in advance what evidence would trigger abandonment, so the team cannot reinterpret sunk costs as commitment.
failure_modes.source_quote: evaluate the stock based on its future potential

failure_modes.mode: Danger When Anti-Sunk-Cost Logic Justifies Reckless Abandonment
failure_modes.description: Anti-sunk-cost logic becomes reckless when it ignores switching costs, learning value, or real upside that still remains.
failure_modes.mitigation: Check for real remaining upside before quitting: anti-sunk-cost logic should not override genuine switching costs, accumulated learning, or residual option value that still justify continuation.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Anti-Sunk-Cost Logic Justifies Reckless Abandonment

premortem_questions.description: Would we start again today from current facts?
premortem_questions.source_quote: would we start again today

premortem_questions.description: What future upside actually remains if we ignore what is already gone?
premortem_questions.source_quote: future potential

premortem_questions.description: What switching cost or learning value are we using as a reason to stay, and is it real or just emotionally easier than exit?
premortem_questions.source_quote: switching costs

heuristics.description: Reset the choice from current facts and future value instead of from prior investment.
heuristics.source_quote: evaluate ... based on its future potential

heuristics.description: Ask whether the team would willingly recommit today before treating continued exposure as rational.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when the question is whether we would start again today

heuristics.description: Separate irrecoverable cost from still-live options so exit and recommitment are judged on the future, not on shame.
heuristics.source_quote: sunk cost prevents this objective assessment

danger_when: Anti-sunk-cost logic is being used as cover for reckless abandonment without evaluating learning value, switching costs, or remaining upside.
The team already understands the sunk cost clearly and now mainly needs execution on the chosen exit or recommitment path.
sunk-cost-fallacy.name: Sunk Cost Fallacy
reframing_when: The question asks how to continue or improve an existing commitment without considering whether abandoning it is the better move — past investment is anchoring the question.
The question frames stopping as 'failure' or 'waste' rather than as a rational reallocation of future resources — the negative framing of quitting is distorting the inquiry.

The question asks 'how do we make this work?' for a project with accumulating evidence of failure, instead of asking 'if we were starting fresh today with no prior investment, would we still choose this path?'
The question escalates commitment to a chosen course ('how do we go deeper?') when the escalation itself is the problem — knee-deep-in-the-big-muddy dynamics.
sunk-cost-fallacy.reframing_move: Apply the fresh-start test: ask 'if we had no history with this and were deciding today from scratch, would we start this?' — marginal analysis strips away the emotional weight of past investment and forces the question to be about future value only.

select_when: Price, scarcity, substitution, or capacity constraints are central drivers of behavior in the system.
The team needs a clean first-pass model of how market pressure, incentives, and quantity interact.
A business decision depends on whether changes in price or supply conditions will materially shift demand and profitability.
The market reality must be simplified into quantity and pricing pressures before adding behavioral nuance.

failure_modes.mode: The Illusion of Rationality
failure_modes.description: Teams can treat market behavior as fully rational and miss the emotional or political forces shaping demand.
failure_modes.mitigation: Include behavioral and political forces in the demand model: check whether emotional, cultural, or regulatory dynamics are shaping willingness to pay beyond rational utility calculations.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Illusion of Rationality

failure_modes.mode: Torturing Reality to Fit the Model
failure_modes.description: A simple price-volume frame can torture reality when the real problem is novel or behaviorally distorted.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test frame fit before applying a standard price-volume model: verify that the market behaves like a textbook supply-demand system rather than force-fitting the frame onto novel dynamics.
failure_modes.source_quote: Torturing Reality to Fit the Model

failure_modes.mode: An accurate but useless idea is still useless
failure_modes.description: Accurate economic reasoning can still be useless if it does not convert into actionable frontline choices.
failure_modes.mitigation: Translate economic insight into frontline actions: convert supply-demand analysis into specific pricing, inventory, or messaging decisions that operators can execute.
failure_modes.source_quote: An accurate but useless idea is still useless

premortem_questions.description: Which non-economic forces are distorting demand or constraining supply in this case?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What non-economic principles might be influencing customer decisions

premortem_questions.description: What second-order consequences follow from this price or capacity move?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What are the second-order consequences

premortem_questions.description: Is our core message simple enough that people can act on it, not just agree with it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: maximize shareholder value

heuristics.description: Use demand segmentation to identify which motives and market gaps actually matter.
heuristics.source_quote: Demand Segmentation

heuristics.description: Align product benefits with the beliefs, desires, and pains of the buyer.
heuristics.source_quote: desires, identifications, and beliefs

heuristics.description: Model price, volume, and market share together instead of changing one variable in isolation.
heuristics.source_quote: price, volume, and market share

danger_when: Power, regulation, narrative, or habit are doing more explanatory work than market clearing logic.
The model is being used as if buyers are purely rational even though anchors, emotions, or politics are distorting behavior.
supply-and-demand.name: Supply And Demand

select_when: The evidence base is dominated by winners, survivors, or visible success stories, and the hidden denominator may change the conclusion materially.
A benchmark, case set, or strategy playbook feels compelling mainly because the failures are absent from view.
The next decision should widen the sample before drawing lessons from a vivid but incomplete success narrative.
The work would improve if the team explicitly checked what non-survivors, failures, or unseen cases would do to the base rate.

failure_modes.mode: Teams invoke survivorship bias abstractly but still use samples or case studies that exclude the hid...
failure_modes.description: Teams invoke survivorship bias abstractly but still use samples or case studies that exclude the hidden denominator.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit the sample for the hidden denominator: before drawing conclusions from success stories, check what fraction of similar attempts failed and whether those failures are represented.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when teams invoke survivorship bias abstractly but still use samples, benchmarks, or case studies that exclude the hidden denominator.

failure_modes.mode: The correction fails when it becomes so broad that meaningful differences between actual winners and...
failure_modes.description: The correction fails when it becomes so broad that meaningful differences between actual winners and losers are ignored.
failure_modes.mitigation: Preserve meaningful signal: when correcting for survivorship bias, still analyze what the winners did differently — the correction should refine the lesson, not erase it.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the anti-bias correction becomes so broad that meaningful differences between real winners and real losers are ignored rather than analyzed.

failure_modes.mode: Availability Heuristic Trap
failure_modes.description: Success stories can become an availability trap that substitutes a memorable survivor narrative for the real outcome distribution.
failure_modes.mitigation: Counter availability with base rates: when a memorable success story dominates judgment, check the full outcome distribution to see whether the case is representative or an outlier.
failure_modes.source_quote: Availability Heuristic Trap

premortem_questions.description: What non-survivors, failures, or hidden cases are missing from this sample?
premortem_questions.source_quote: exclude the hidden denominator

premortem_questions.description: Are we overcorrecting so much that real differences between winners and losers disappear?
premortem_questions.source_quote: meaningful differences ... are ignored

premortem_questions.description: What outside-view base rate would this conclusion look like if the vivid survivor story were removed?
premortem_questions.source_quote: outside view

heuristics.description: Bring the hidden denominator into the evidence base before trusting the lesson drawn from survivors.
heuristics.source_quote: exclude the hidden denominator

heuristics.description: Use the outside view and base rates to counter the pull of vivid success stories.
heuristics.source_quote: The Outside View/Base Rate Neglect

heuristics.description: Analyze real differences between survivors and non-survivors instead of treating the warning as a reason to flatten everything.
heuristics.source_quote: meaningful differences between real winners and real losers

danger_when: The anti-bias correction is becoming so broad that meaningful differences between real winners and real losers are being ignored rather than analyzed.
The denominator is already explicit and the team now mainly needs a decision, not another generic warning about incomplete samples.
survivorship-bias.name: Survivorship Bias

select_when: A seemingly better alternative exists, but economic, psychological, or operational friction is keeping people on the incumbent path.
A migration or vendor exit plan is being described as reversible even though coexistence drag and reversal cost are quietly growing.
The team needs to distinguish real fit from loyalty driven by sunk effort, habit, or commitment-consistency pressure.
A recommendation must surface the hidden cost of dual-run dependency, exit sequencing pressure, or delayed switching.

failure_modes.mode: The high-risk pattern is "temporary coexistence" becoming the default architecture.
failure_modes.description: Temporary coexistence can quietly become the architecture that traps the organization in both the old and new path.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a coexistence deadline: define when the old system will be fully retired and track dual-run dependencies to prevent temporary parallel operation from becoming permanent.
failure_modes.source_quote: The high-risk pattern is "temporary coexistence" becoming the default architecture.

failure_modes.mode: The result is not just delayed efficiency; it is accumulated irreversibility.
failure_modes.description: Teams can assume rollback stays available even while dual-run dependencies and customizations are rapidly making reversal harder.
failure_modes.mitigation: Monitor rollback feasibility continuously: at each milestone, re-verify that reversal is still possible and flag the moment when customizations or dependencies make it irreversible.
failure_modes.source_quote: The result is not just delayed efficiency; it is accumulated irreversibility.

failure_modes.mode: we are uncomfortable with our inconsistencies and will take steps to reduce or remove them
failure_modes.description: A familiar framework can get defended beyond fit because the mental or organizational cost of switching feels too high.
failure_modes.mitigation: Evaluate framework loyalty against current fit: periodically check whether the incumbent tool still serves the problem better than alternatives, stripping out familiarity comfort from the comparison.
failure_modes.source_quote: we are uncomfortable with our inconsistencies and will take steps to reduce or remove them

premortem_questions.description: What specific capability, workflow, or partner dependency would force both the old and new path to stay alive longer than planned?
premortem_questions.source_quote: what specific capabilities must remain alive during migration

premortem_questions.description: How fast does the cost of going back rise as data history, customizations, and contracts accumulate under the new state?
premortem_questions.source_quote: how fast does the cost of going back increase as data history, customizations, and partner contracts accumulate

premortem_questions.description: What foundational assumptions are embedded in the framework we are currently reluctant to switch away from?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What foundational assumptions are embedded in the primary framework we are using, and are those assumptions guaranteed to hold true?

heuristics.description: Treat dual-run coexistence as a real coordination tax instead of as harmless implementation detail.
heuristics.source_quote: The real economic and operational burden comes from keeping both the old and new path alive at the same time.

heuristics.description: Lower mental switching cost for a good new idea by using analogies that connect it to something the audience already understands.
heuristics.source_quote: Analogies act as "bridges, connecting what your audience already understands to the new ideas".

heuristics.description: Tie exit sequencing to named owners and checkpoints so reversibility decay becomes visible before it is too late.
heuristics.source_quote: exit sequencing that is not tied to named owners, checkpoints, and decision gates leaves the organization without a trigger to stop and re-plan

danger_when: The situation is genuinely novel enough that clinging to the incumbent frame would mainly reinforce cognitive entrenchment.
The model is being used to justify inertia without naming the concrete coordination, dependency, or reversal costs that make switching hard.
switching-costs.name: Switching Costs

select_when: The facts exist but no governing thought exists yet.
Analysis is largely finished but prioritization and connection are not.
The audience needs linked insight rather than an evidence dump.
The real bottleneck is deciding what matters and what deserves to drive action.

failure_modes.mode: Most useful when the facts exist but no governing thought exists.
failure_modes.description: Teams can gather many observations and still fail to act because no governing thought links the evidence into a decision-driving structure.
failure_modes.mitigation: Force a governing thesis: after gathering observations, require one sentence that links the evidence into a decision-driving structure before moving forward.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when the facts exist but no governing thought exists.

failure_modes.mode: Works when analysis is finished but prioritization is not.
failure_modes.description: Evidence can remain inert when analysis keeps expanding but no priority rule says what matters most.
failure_modes.mitigation: Apply a priority filter: rank evidence by decision impact and discard or defer inputs that would not change the recommendation even if they were wrong.
failure_modes.source_quote: Works when analysis is finished but prioritization is not.

failure_modes.mode: Best when the audience needs linked insight, not evidence dump.
failure_modes.description: A synthesis can fail by collapsing into a simple story that no longer carries the key reinforcing logic underneath it.
failure_modes.mitigation: Protect the reinforcing logic: when compressing a synthesis into a summary, verify that the key causal links and supporting mechanisms survive the simplification.
failure_modes.source_quote: Best when the audience needs linked insight, not evidence dump.

premortem_questions.description: What is the governing thought that should organize these facts, and what evidence genuinely belongs under it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when the facts exist but no governing thought exists.

premortem_questions.description: Which conclusion actually deserves to drive action rather than just look interesting on the page?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Works when analysis is finished but prioritization is not.

premortem_questions.description: If this summary were shortened further, what causal spine would we lose that still matters for the decision?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best when the audience needs linked insight, not evidence dump.

heuristics.description: Turn a mass of analysis into a small number of mutually reinforcing conclusions.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when the audience needs linked insight, not evidence dump.

heuristics.description: Use synthesis only after enough analysis exists to justify a real governing thought.
heuristics.source_quote: Works when analysis is finished but prioritization is not.

heuristics.description: Prune facts that do not strengthen the core decision-driving structure.
heuristics.source_quote: Synthesis demands focus and simplification, ignoring data that doesn't contribute to the core message.

danger_when: Synthesis is being faked as compression before the underlying analysis is strong enough to support it.
A simple story is being forced by dropping details that still carry the causal spine.
synthesis-and-integration.name: Synthesis And Integration

select_when: A fast, intuitive judgment has been made in a high-stakes or novel context and needs to be examined for reliability.
Cognitive biases driven by automatic pattern-matching (anchoring, affect heuristic, halo effect) are suspected in a decision.
Expert intuition is being applied outside the domain where reliable patterns were built, creating overreach risk.
A confident conclusion has been reached quickly and the fluency of the answer may be substituting for the rigor of the analysis.

failure_modes.mode: WYSIATI-driven overconfidence
failure_modes.description: WYSIATI-driven overconfidence constructs a confident story from available evidence without registering what is absent, making thin evidence feel complete.
failure_modes.mitigation: Explicitly audit what information is missing before committing to a confident story; ask what evidence would reverse the conclusion and why it is absent.
failure_modes.source_quote: WYSIATI-driven overconfidence

failure_modes.mode: Halo contamination
failure_modes.description: Halo contamination lets one salient attribute flood all subsequent assessments, making independent evaluation of separate dimensions impossible.
failure_modes.mitigation: Evaluate attributes independently and in sequence before allowing an overall impression to form; remove the most salient positive signal and re-score.
failure_modes.source_quote: Halo contamination

failure_modes.mode: Expert overreach
failure_modes.description: Expert overreach applies pattern recognition built in one domain confidently to novel domains where the underlying patterns do not hold.
failure_modes.mitigation: Distinguish domains where reliable intuition can form (fast feedback, stable patterns) from those where it cannot; demand explicit reasoning chains in novel contexts.
failure_modes.source_quote: Expert overreach

premortem_questions.description: Is the confidence we feel here tracking the quality of the story we can tell, or the completeness of the evidence behind it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: WYSIATI — confidence tracks story quality, not evidence completeness

premortem_questions.description: What is the evidence base for this intuition, and does the current situation actually match the cases where the intuition was formed?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Expert overreach: distinguish domains where reliable intuition can form from domains where it cannot

premortem_questions.description: Which attribute is most salient here, and are we letting it shape our view of dimensions that should be evaluated independently?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Halo contamination: one positive attribute contaminates all other judgments

heuristics.description: Treat high fluency and fast certainty as a scrutiny trigger, not a quality signal — System 1 is most confident and most dangerous when its outputs feel most obvious.
heuristics.source_quote: Fluency as Truth: ideas expressed in clear, familiar language feel more true

heuristics.description: Audit the substitution: when answering a hard question, ask whether a simpler heuristic question is being answered in its place.
heuristics.source_quote: The Substitution Heuristic: System 1 answers a simpler heuristic question in place of the harder target question

heuristics.description: In novel domains, require explicit reasoning chains rather than accepting intuitive conclusions, no matter how expert the source.
heuristics.source_quote: Expert overreach: demand explicit reasoning when System 1 outputs emerge in novel contexts

danger_when: Expert intuition in a well-practiced domain with fast, reliable feedback — in those conditions System 1 is accurate and deliberative override degrades rather than improves performance.
The model is being used to dismiss all fast judgment as unreliable without distinguishing calibrated expert pattern recognition from domain-overreached heuristics.
system-1.name: System 1
system-1.input_type: fast intuitive judgment or pattern-matched conclusion
system-1.output_type: bias identification and System 2 scrutiny trigger

select_when: A decision involves genuinely novel situations where no reliable pattern recognition exists and deliberate analysis is required.
Stakes are high and the cost of a fluent-but-wrong fast conclusion exceeds the cost of deliberation.
Probability estimates, logical validity checks, or multi-option comparisons require explicit, rule-governed reasoning.
The team has converged on a confident answer quickly and a deliberate audit of assumptions and missing evidence is needed.
Post-decision rationalizations need to be distinguished from genuine pre-decision deliberation.

failure_modes.mode: Motivated System 2
failure_modes.description: Motivated System 2 recruits deliberate reasoning to construct justifications for preferences already generated by System 1, producing outputs that look deliberative but are not.
failure_modes.mitigation: Install a motivated-reasoning check: after constructing a deliberate argument, ask whether the conclusion was already preferred before the analysis began — if so, run the analysis against the opposite hypothesis.
failure_modes.source_quote: Motivated System 2

failure_modes.mode: Cognitive depletion windows
failure_modes.description: Cognitive depletion windows reduce System 2 capacity across heavy decision sessions, making late-day or end-of-review decisions structurally disadvantaged.
failure_modes.mitigation: Protect late-session decisions: schedule the highest-stakes judgments early in the day or after breaks, and flag any critical call made under cognitive fatigue for re-review.
failure_modes.source_quote: Cognitive depletion windows

failure_modes.mode: Pseudo-deliberation
failure_modes.description: Pseudo-deliberation performs the motions of System 2 engagement — the memo, the meeting, the rubric — without genuinely updating on contrary evidence.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test for genuine updating: after any deliberative process, identify what changed in the team's position — if nothing changed, the deliberation was performative.
failure_modes.source_quote: Pseudo-deliberation

premortem_questions.description: If we had no prior position on this, which option would this analysis actually support — or are we constructing reasons for a conclusion we've already reached?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Motivated System 2: System 2 as advocate rather than auditor

premortem_questions.description: Are we making this decision now because it is the right time for deliberate analysis, or because the meeting happens to end here?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Depletion windows: consequential decisions made late in the day are structurally disadvantaged

premortem_questions.description: Has anyone in this process genuinely identified a reason to change the recommended conclusion — or has every challenge been addressed as an obstacle to resolve?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Pseudo-deliberation: process is theater; System 1 consensus forms first

heuristics.description: Separate the generate phase from the evaluate phase — collect options and independent ratings before group discussion to prevent early System 1 consensus from making System 2 review performative.
heuristics.source_quote: Require anonymous independent ratings before group discussion

heuristics.description: Schedule high-stakes decisions early in the session, when System 2 capacity is highest — not at the end of an exhausting review agenda.
heuristics.source_quote: Schedule high-stakes decisions early in the day, after adequate rest and food

heuristics.description: Externalize the reasoning chain in writing before the group meeting — unwritten assumptions are invisible to System 2 audit and to other participants.
heuristics.source_quote: Externalize the Reasoning: writing activates System 2 and makes its outputs inspectable

danger_when: In well-practiced expert domains where calibrated intuition is more accurate and faster than deliberative override.
When cognitive load is already at capacity — deploying System 2 reasoning in high-depletion conditions produces lower-quality output than accepting well-calibrated System 1 defaults.
system-2.name: System 2
system-2.output_type: calibrated, deliberate judgment with explicit reasoning chain

select_when: Recurring symptoms, repeated escalations, or oscillating outcomes suggest the local patch is not touching the real source of behavior.
Cause and effect are delayed, circular, or distributed across multiple actors, incentives, and constraints.
The chosen intervention will change incentives, behavior, capacity, or future option space rather than only solving today's visible issue.
A rewrite or redesign may be misdiagnosing coordination debt, feedback-loop failure, or ownership ambiguity as purely technical debt.

failure_modes.mode: Incorrectly applying a familiar framework to a novel problem (I have seen this one before, it is an X problem).
failure_modes.description: Maslow's hammer can force a familiar frame onto a system that needs a different cut.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test frame fit before applying: verify that the systems lens captures the dominant dynamics rather than forcing a familiar framework onto a problem that needs a different cut.
failure_modes.source_quote: Incorrectly applying a familiar framework to a novel problem (I have seen this one before, it is an X problem).

failure_modes.mode: The tendency to seek or interpret information that confirms existing hypotheses or the vision of authority figures.
failure_modes.description: Confirmation bias and groupthink can lock the team into the current hypothesis or authority vision.
failure_modes.mitigation: Introduce adversarial perspectives: rotate in an outsider or devil's advocate to challenge the dominant hypothesis before consensus hardens.
failure_modes.source_quote: The tendency to seek or interpret information that confirms existing hypotheses or the vision of authority figures.

failure_modes.mode: Architecture can be used as a misdiagnosis when the real problem is coordination, ownership, or feed...
failure_modes.description: Architecture can be used as a misdiagnosis when the real problem is coordination, ownership, or feedback design.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate architecture problems from coordination problems: when the system diagram looks right but outcomes are wrong, check whether the real issue is ownership, incentives, or feedback loops.
failure_modes.source_quote: Architecture debt is used as the explanation when the recurring failures are actually coordination debt or ownership ambiguity.

failure_modes.mode: Getting lost in the complexity and depth of the system, becoming unable to move from thorough investigation to action.
failure_modes.description: Analysis paralysis can keep the team in endless system explanation without moving toward the next intervention.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set an analysis deadline: define when systems explanation must produce a concrete intervention, and enforce the boundary to prevent mapping from becoming an end in itself.
failure_modes.source_quote: Getting lost in the complexity and depth of the system, becoming unable to move from thorough investigation to action.

premortem_questions.description: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Use tools like What would you have to believe? to spell out all implicit assumptions and implications of a viewpoint.

premortem_questions.description: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: For large projects, ask, Is the worst case bad enough? to ensure sufficient weight is placed on low-probability, high-impact outcomes.

heuristics.description: Expose mental models to the light of day so assumptions become visible, checkable, and revisable.
heuristics.source_quote: A key practice is to expose mental models to the light of day.

heuristics.description: Run the discussion at two levels: the case at hand and the machine that produced it.
heuristics.source_quote: discussions should be conducted at two levels: the machine level ... and the case-at-hand level

heuristics.description: Model the system dynamically with causal loops or stock-and-flow views before choosing the fix.
heuristics.source_quote: Systems thinkers utilize tools like causal loop diagrams and stock and flow diagrams to visualize the system.

heuristics.description: Design strategic metrics by starting from the goal, then mapping the process, measures, and levers that can move it.
heuristics.source_quote: This involves first knowing the goal, then understanding the process ... identifying the key points in the process that are best for measurement, and finally, exploring how to create levers.

danger_when: The problem is linear, bounded, and dominated by a clear proximate cause with an obvious direct fix.
The team cannot name a leverage point, intervention variable, or progress signal, so the system map is becoming description rather than decision support.
Rich diagrams and broad causal stories are replacing the harder move of choosing what to test or change next.
systems-thinking.name: Systems Thinking
reframing_when: The question isolates a component from the system it operates in — the real question is about the system's behavior, not the component's performance.
The question accepts an arbitrary boundary that excludes actors, feedback loops, or second-order effects that actually govern the outcome.
The question treats a symptom or event as the problem when the real issue is structural — the iceberg model shows the question is aimed at the visible tip.
The question asks about one actor's behavior without considering how the system will adapt in response — the question needs to include the feedback dynamics.
systems-thinking.reframing_move: Widen the system boundary: ask 'what system is this question operating inside, and does the question include the right boundary?' — often the question needs to be about the system's structure, not the component the question names.

select_when: A favored framework might be hiding reality by shaping attention too aggressively and filtering out inconvenient evidence.
Different models yield materially different cuts of the problem, suggesting the current map is narrowing what the team can notice.
Progress depends on reframing to uncover missing signal that the dominant theory is causing everyone not to notice.
Experts are defaulting to familiar intellectual routines instead of tailoring their approach to the novelty of the problem.

failure_modes.mode: Illusion of Understanding
failure_modes.description: Illusion of understanding can make experts stop investigating because they confuse familiarity with reality access.
failure_modes.mitigation: Schedule regular 'what are we not seeing?' audits: require the team to name what the current theory cannot explain, and investigate those gaps before doubling down.
failure_modes.source_quote: Illusion of Understanding

failure_modes.mode: Jumping to Abstraction (Dangerous Anti-Pattern)
failure_modes.description: Jumping to abstraction can make the team miss the concrete detail the theory has hidden from view.
failure_modes.mitigation: Ground theory in concrete observation: before accepting a theoretical explanation, require at least one specific, falsifiable prediction that would distinguish it from alternatives.
failure_modes.source_quote: Jumping to Abstraction (Dangerous Anti-Pattern)

failure_modes.mode: Danger When Reframing Becomes Endless Theory Shopping
failure_modes.description: Reframing becomes misuse when people keep shopping for theories to avoid evidence and operational commitment.
failure_modes.mitigation: Cap the theory-shopping: when a reframe is proposed, require it to come with a concrete test and a commitment to act on the result, not just a new narrative.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Reframing Becomes Endless Theory Shopping

premortem_questions.description: What framework is this analysis missing?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What framework is this analysis missing?

premortem_questions.description: What worldview or assumptions are inherent in the model I am using?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What worldview or assumptions are inherent in the model I am using?

premortem_questions.description: What is the one thing that, if true, would shatter my current hypothesis?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What is the one thing that, if true, would shatter my current hypothesis?

heuristics.description: Try several cuts at the problem so different frames can expose different missing signals.
heuristics.source_quote: Try Several Cuts at the Problem

heuristics.description: Use a multidisciplinary latticework instead of one department's favorite model.
heuristics.source_quote: Multidisciplinary Thinking

heuristics.description: Test active hypotheses instead of hiding behind familiar theory before the data is even challenged.
heuristics.source_quote: Active Hypothesis Testing

danger_when: Reframing has turned into endless theory shopping that avoids evidence, testable claims, or operational decisions.
The team is treating the map as the territory and using a familiar framework to torture reality into fit.
theory-induced-blindness.name: Theory Induced Blindness
reframing_when: The question operates within a theoretical framework so deeply accepted that anomalies and contradictions are explained away rather than taken as evidence that the frame itself might be wrong.
The question treats an established model or theory as ground truth rather than as one possible lens — the theory has become invisible infrastructure rather than a testable assumption.
The question dismisses evidence that contradicts the dominant framework as noise, exceptions, or measurement error rather than asking whether the framework itself is the source of distortion.
The question frames refinement of the existing theory as the only path forward when the anomalies actually point toward a fundamentally different explanatory frame.
theory-induced-blindness.reframing_move: Before refining the current theory, ask: 'What anomalies am I explaining away, and what alternative framework would treat those anomalies as the central evidence?' — the theory-blindness reframe makes the dominant theory visible as a choice rather than as reality.

select_when: A conjunctive chain has a binding constraint, so improving non-binding stages will not raise throughput.
There are competing action options and the team can name the current bottleneck with a measured limit before ranking fixes.
Local optimization is masking the true leverage point because many high-quality improvements still leave key outcomes flat.
One blocking dependency, queue, approval step, scarce specialist, or handoff is visibly capping the whole delivery plan.

failure_modes.mode: Local optimization of non-binding step
failure_modes.description: Local optimization can consume energy while the real throughput ceiling remains fixed elsewhere.
failure_modes.mitigation: Subordinate all optimization to the constraint: do not improve non-bottleneck steps until the real throughput ceiling is addressed.
failure_modes.source_quote: Local optimization of non-binding step

failure_modes.mode: Constraint not explicitly measured
failure_modes.description: Constraint language becomes theater when no current cap, governing metric, or movement evidence is named.
failure_modes.mitigation: Name the constraint concretely: require a specific capacity cap, governing metric, and evidence of movement before accepting that constraint language is being used productively.
failure_modes.source_quote: Constraint not explicitly measured

failure_modes.mode: Constraint shift without retest
failure_modes.description: The model breaks when teams keep fixing the old bottleneck after the constraint has already shifted somewhere else.
failure_modes.mitigation: Re-diagnose the constraint after every improvement cycle: once the old bottleneck is relieved, the constraint shifts — re-map the flow to find the new one.
failure_modes.source_quote: Constraint shift without retest

premortem_questions.description: Before committing: what is the single measured limit that would still cap throughput if every local improvement in this plan succeeded next week?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Before committing: what is the single measured limit that would still cap throughput if every local improvement in this plan succeeded next week?

premortem_questions.description: If this first bottleneck actually moves, what step becomes the next cap within two weeks?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If this first bottleneck actually moves, what step becomes the next cap within two weeks, and what evidence in this plan would tell us the constraint has already shifted there?

premortem_questions.description: Which task in this plan is consuming the same scarce team, approval slot, or specialist time needed at the bottleneck?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Which task in this plan is consuming the same scarce team, approval slot, or specialist time needed at the bottleneck

heuristics.description: Define constraints rigorously up front so the problem scope and the bottleneck candidate are explicit before solutioning.
heuristics.source_quote: Clear Problem Definition (TOSCA)

heuristics.description: Disaggregate and prioritize until only the branches affecting the binding constraint stay in focus.
heuristics.source_quote: Disaggregation and Prioritization

heuristics.description: Re-score the constraint after each major change so the action sequence moves with the bottleneck.
heuristics.source_quote: Constraint cadence

danger_when: The environment is too unstable for one fixed bottleneck frame because the limiting factor is moving every cycle.
The team is performing bottleneck theater and cannot name the governing metric, current cap, or evidence that the cap moved after intervention.
theory-of-constraints.name: Theory Of Constraints

select_when: Many tasks look valuable on the surface but only a few meaningfully move the outcome, decision quality, or learning rate.
The team can distinguish leverage from busyness and define the specific end-product that would actually resolve the issue.
Time, attention, or capital are scarce enough that ruthless pruning matters more than completeness theater.

failure_modes.mode: Finished-Thinking Illusion
failure_modes.description: High-level leverage talk can create a finished-thinking illusion while causal assumptions and ownership remain thin.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require causal specificity: after identifying high-leverage work, name the exact mechanism by which it drives the outcome and assign an owner to each causal link.
failure_modes.source_quote: Finished-Thinking Illusion

failure_modes.mode: Focus on Triviality (Parkinson's Law of Triviality)
failure_modes.description: Teams can spend their effort on safe low-value tasks that feel productive while avoiding the truly leveraged work.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit effort allocation against leverage: track where the team actually spends time and flag any pattern of gravitating toward safe, low-value tasks while avoiding the hard leveraged work.
failure_modes.source_quote: Focus on Triviality (Parkinson's Law of Triviality)

failure_modes.mode: Flawed Problem Definition
failure_modes.description: Elegant high-value analysis can still solve the wrong problem if the underlying problem definition is flawed.
failure_modes.mitigation: Validate the problem definition before pursuing the high-value solution: check that the underlying problem is correctly framed so that elegant analysis does not solve the wrong question.
failure_modes.source_quote: Flawed Problem Definition

premortem_questions.description: What concrete end-product would prove that this so-called high-value focus is real rather than rhetorical?
premortem_questions.source_quote: define the specific end-product that would actually resolve the issue

premortem_questions.description: Which allegedly leveraged activity would still matter if we had to justify it with operational evidence tomorrow?
premortem_questions.source_quote: high-level framework thinking drifts from the concrete, operational evidence

premortem_questions.description: What low-value busyness are we preserving because it feels complete or safe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: many tasks look valuable on the surface but only a few meaningfully move the outcome

heuristics.description: Start from the end-product needed to resolve the issue and prune everything that does not clearly move it.
heuristics.source_quote: The team can distinguish leverage from busyness and define the specific end-product that would actually resolve the issue.

heuristics.description: Use strong early hypotheses to focus analysis on the few drivers that actually matter.
heuristics.source_quote: The process should be guided by strong hypotheses

heuristics.description: Ruthlessly narrow away completeness theater when time, attention, or capital are scarce.
heuristics.source_quote: ruthless pruning is more important than completeness theater

danger_when: High-level framing is creating the illusion that the hard causal, sequencing, or ownership thinking is already finished.
People are talking fluently about leverage while skipping the enabling details that make the recommendation executable.
tier-2-high-value.name: Tier 2 High Value

select_when: A claim, idea, or method looks promising but needs to be checked against proven tests, reference classes, or established validation loops before wider commitment.
The team is at risk of trusting novelty, analogy, or polished explanation without enough evidence that the approach actually works.
Repeated validation against known standards, expected answers, or old-but-reliable checks would reduce the chance of fashionable error.
A recommendation needs credibility that comes from surviving structured verification, not just from sounding coherent.

failure_modes.mode: line up all the assumptions that underpin a reference class and test the current case for fit
